It's dropped off considerably since then because, I've ridden better coasters. But Montu still ranks among my top B&Ms and is easily one of the best inverts in the world (only behind Banshee at Kings Island). After that, there's Kumba. This is famous for being one of the first B&Ms to ever open, and it's a good one. Like Montu, this is also a really strong coaster being super intense despite it being on the rough side. Unfortunately, this has been closed for the past year and is on the chopping block for removal. I'm so mad about that I can hardly put it into words. There was also Scorpion, an short but awesome Schwartzkopf looper, which was torn down last year with only a few days' notice. Which again, holy sh*t. United Parks (and ESPECIALLY SIX FLAGS) needs to F*CKING ANNOUNCE THIS AHEAD OF TIME. Sure, Scorpion and Kumba have service lives that are/were probably up. But LOOK AT WHAT UNIVERSAL DID TO RIP RIDE ROCKIT. ONE OF THE SH*TTIEST COASTERS I'VE EVER RIDDEN. THAT CLOSURE WAS ANNOUNCED A YEAR BEFORE IT HAPPENED, AND IT GENERATED 3-HOUR LONG WAITS ON ITS CLOSING DAY. WHY COULD THIS NOT BE DONE FOR SCORPION, A RIDE FAR BETTER AND MORE ICONIC!??!?!?!? So infuriating. Anyways, besides those three, they have Tigris, a Premier Skyrocket II, those are always fun, Cheetah Hunt and Cobra's Curse which are great family coasters. So yeah, they still have a very strong coaster collection, especially with Iron Gwazi now open (really looking forward to that on my next visit).

For their other rides, they have Falcon's Fury, one of the biggest drop towers in the world. Which has a TILTING feature that makes it so when you drop, you stare right at the ground. Easily my favorite drop tower and favorite flat ride. They also have a rapids ride, and they used to have a log ride (Also demolished with hardly any notice. Yeah, it wasn't the best log flume, but it was still a park classic that deserved better.) So yeah, it's mostly just coasters with a few other notable things like the train, the skyride (which, for some reason, is now an upcharge ride, PLEASE fix this). I'm also really concerned for the future of this park as it's been on a decline as of late. Constantly demolishing rides, having multiple rides closed for MONTHS at a time, and I've heard several stories of the operations and quality getting worse and worse. I mean, good god, they had to shut down a restaurant mid-operations due to a f*cking failed health inspection. F*ck that noise!

So yeah, this park sadly lost a spot in my top 10 parks list. But Montu and Falcon's Fury still keep it in the upper half of my park rankings (So help me if they ever decide to get rid of those two rides). So I'd still recommend checking it out. I just hope that the park hasn't gotten as bad as I've heard. ):

Where do I begin?

$35 for parking...insane, but don't waste your money on premium parking ($60). I think those fools walked farther than is.

Wait lines for rides are terrible. It's hot, muggy, and loud. If you want to skip those line ($120 a person). If this was Disney - maybe, but the rides don't warrant that amount of money.

Food is mediocre at best, and that's if you get it. We ate at "Tree Top Kitchen". It's good to escape the heat, but the meal was terrible and service slow to nonexistent. Once my family's order did come, my meal was still missing. I brought it to the host and servers attention multiple times, but they couldn't, or wouldn't help me?

I don't recommend this park to the worst person in the world.

Spend the extra money to go to Disney or Universal.

We had a lot of fun during Madri Gras. They had shows & parades. Photo ops with the dancers & band members. We enjoyed the food & drinks. My son fed the birds, at the attraction the staff takes photos. We rode a handful of rides & enjoyed those as well. Definitely a good day & experience.

45 minutes just to park. Another 45 minutes to get ticket counter. All with the Platinum Pass. After all this, park refused to correct an error made at the ticket counter on a simple issue. Park so crowded we got on one ride only all day. Busch has always been my favorite park, but I doubt I will renew pass again, let alone visit on this year's pass. Mind-boggling how inflexible customer service is.
Charging $7.00 for a 20oz soda is absolutely ridiculous then asking for a tip is over the top pathetic.

Woah. The park is huge. Zoo, seaworld and a lot of roller coasters in same place. The rides are great. The staff are not so friendly. Actually, they are horrible lol, but hey what more do you expect from teenagers. I get it, it's hot, and they have better places to be, but gotta work.
Food not so great, but then again, I don't expect much from amusement park food.
Big park, a lot to do, many rides, fast pass worth it. Just for that 5 star

I only put this as 5 stars to try and get it on the front page! The wait lines for food took over 1 hour and wasnt worth it. Also, half of the rides were closed and the open ones were not family friendly! I would have to give this 1 1/2 stars

Big shout out to John from First Aid. He was amazing. Really helpful and friendly. Thanks so much John. You're what makes the park great


I actually really enjoy Busch Gardens, love the atmosphere and the staff, although the waits are crazy most of the time even during week days when you would think everyone would be at work lol... Prices are ok and it's a fun place to be if you don't mind the lines.

Every December, I bring my family to Florida for two weeks to celebrate Christmas, New Year's, and my son's birthday. This year, at my son's request, we visited SeaWorld and Busch Gardens.

We visited SeaWorld on December 24, 2025, and had a very positive experience. On December 26, 2025, we visited Busch Gardens. For most of the day, our experience was enjoyable. However, as the evening progressed--around 5:00 PM--we noticed the crowd becoming increasingly large, aggressive, and hostile. By approximately 7:00 PM, my wife and I decided it was best to leave the park due to these conditions.

While walking toward the exit near the penguin exhibit, a group of approximately three to four women were taking photographs. As we walked past, one woman stepped backward, causing her to bump into my wife. The woman began yelling at my wife, using profanity. My wife responded that the woman had stepped into her path and continued walking away. As I passed, I calmly stated that the woman had stepped back into my wife and asked her to calm down.

At that point, the woman became increasingly aggressive, verbally threatening and advancing toward me while I was holding my son's hand. Despite repeated attempts to disengage and walk away, the woman continued to scream, invade our personal space, and make threatening gestures. My wife urged us to leave, but the woman persisted.

Moments later, the woman struck me in the face. Fearing for the safety of my family, I felt forced to defend myself. This escalated into a physical altercation in which two to three individuals from the same group attacked both my wife and me. Other guests intervened and separated us, and we attempted to leave the area.

However, the same individual repeatedly broke free from others restraining her and attacked us again--this occurred multiple times as we attempted to walk away. At no point during these incidents did security personnel or park staff intervene.

After the final altercation, my wife realized her phone and earrings had been stolen during the assault. Using a phone tracking application, we located the group again, where the phone was being hidden in a baby stroller. When my wife identified the phone, one of the individuals attempted to destroy it by throwing it. When I bent down to retrieve the phone, I was struck again.

We were eventually able to make our way toward the park exit, still without assistance from Busch Gardens security or staff. My wife was forced to call 911 while we were walking so that police could meet us at the front gate, as we continued to fear being followed and attacked.

As a result of this incident, I sustained a severe shoulder injury, multiple bruises on my back, and a black eye. My wife also sustained multiple bruises and a black eye. All of this occurred in front of our children, which was extremely traumatic.

The responding police officer and medical personnel were professional and helpful. However, no arrests were made and no charges were filed. We were then informed that we would be trespassed from Busch Gardens properties for 365 days--despite being the victims and receiving no protection from park security during the incident.

This decision is deeply troubling. My family visited your park to create positive family memories, yet we experienced a violent assault without any intervention from your security team. We were forced to defend ourselves due to the absence of assistance, suffered serious injuries, and then were penalized by being trespassed from your properties.

Earlier this morning, I was contacted by one of your local attorneys and was advised to seek medical attention immediately. I intend to follow this advice upon returning home and will be seeing my primary care physician.

In closing, while we appreciate the opportunity to visit your park, Busch Gardens failed to provide a safe and secure environment for my family. The lack of security response, combined with the decision to trespass the victims rather than the aggressors, is unacceptable. Due to this experience, we will most likely never patronize any Busch Gardens or affiliated facilities again.

Sincerely,
Vernard M

if i could put zero stars i would. Workers are hard to deal with. They make up rules as they go, and don't state clear news and instructions on their website. when it was brought to their attention, they were defiant and danced around it. My friends and I were then told to leave because we are not 21. very disappointed.

Busy day de we expected waits. But half the rides closed so the ones running were over 90 min wait. Many Animal exhibits "under repair". Every food area seriously understaffed (poor kids) and appeared very undertrained. Went for ice cream- ran out of ice cream. Tried to get on the train ride 3 times!! Over an hour wait each time. We used to come here all the time with our kids when they were young g and have so much fun. Now it is seriously overpriced ($160 per person and no discount for kids). Think a fast pass will get you onthe rides sooner - nope. $160 per person and still those poor people waited more than 30 min. I will never come back and don't recommend.

SLOTH ENCOUNTER! The sloth encounter was a longtime dream of mine. We visited in October and it was a delight beyond words. These special animals are clearly very meticulously loved, cared for and maintained at this facility. Of course the sloths were absolutely amazing, but our guides Justin and Amanda made it even better than expected. Their passion and care for these animals absolutely shined. They were so knowledgeable and friendly and had a great sense of humor. We were so comfortable and felt so welcomed, I was truly impressed with their combination of both people and sloth skills! Wonderful experience all around, and we'll be back not only to visit the sloths Harry and Lorenzo again, but also Justin and Amanda!

20% of the rides are closed for maintenance. Others are running at half capacity. Further, other rides are an additional charge and not advertised as such online. The meal plan food.... Half was not available and the other half was served at dangerously cold temperatures by uninspired staff. I regret the money spent and look forward to not returning to a Sea World family resort.

Total shot show! Understaffed dramatically at food venues. Many of the rides "not currently" operating? Huh!!!!

Such a shame!

We were looking for something to do on day 4/6 of our florida vacay. busch garden tickets were half off for black friday (until nov. 9th btw) we had a great time! Yes, the $40 parking and $6 soft drinks were insane, but the experience here was one of the best parts of our trip. The rides are fun, feel very safe, and there are so many that we didn't even get to ride all of them in 4 hours. my advice, don't shop/eat around and try to get tickets on sale, you won't regret it!

Park is going down the tubes. 25% of the rides were closed the day we visited the park and we experienced ride break down after long waits to get on the roller coasters that were open.
The food court was just awful and very understaffed. The food was terrible and over priced. The business has the feel of going bankrupt soon. I would skip this park.

Busch Gardens now charges $5 per person for the skyride WTH!!!! Since when?
The most overpriced park ever:
$27 for a meal at chick fil a
$16 for a funnel cake
$ 9 for a hot cocoa and $20 for a plate of asian food. Nothing taste good to justify the absurd prices.
Falcons Fury and other 3 main rides weren't working and opened midday.
No more photos after the rides - it seems abandoned.
Employees are grumpy, seem tired and unhappy.
Busch Gardens always been my favorite park and its very upsetting to see how they declined and are desperately trying to compensate the low attendance with skyrocketing prices!!!

Purchased a pass July 2025 that comes with a free photo key where they upload your photos. This is , or should I say was part of the Platinum pass benefits. As I left the park today and walked back out to my car to grab mine & walked back in, I was told they recently discontinued this. I did say, even though this was part of the package when I purchased my pass? The attendant says, yes I'm sorry. I then mentioned so there is no stipulation whatsoever since My pass was purchased before this ended. She said no once again. It appears that since I didn't use my free animal encounter before now, this had been taken away. My last two trips here have been horrible and the last trip we had to stand at costumer service speaking with them for awhile before everything was straightened out. Omg, 8th grade field trip! Talk about Busch gardens need an upgrade!! They really need to do a renovation ASAP! Please wear comfortable sneakers or walking shoes because there's A LOT OF WALKING!! Also, pack your PATIENCE because the lines are CRAZY unless you get the Fast pass!

Unfortunately, a lot of the restaurants weren't open which sucked!! It took us an hour and 40 mins to order lunch isn't that INSANE!! Like WTF.... Short staffed, limited options of food and rides because they were closed its not how you welcome students because they complained too much to the staff. Bush Gardens you need to do better and the prices shouldn't be so CRAZY TOO!! A bottle of water was $10 WTF OMG this trip

Last visit was 25 years ago. My how things change! Very spread out with very few sufficiently air conditioned areas. Rides are in need of paint touch ups and in times look sad. Bundles of old weave hair and gum cling to every ride line's queue. Animal viewing and areas given an afterthought. Penguins all grouped in one area, Tiger pacing, and at least 50% were simply closed. Food was "all we could eat" every 90min. Worth it only because everything is grossly overpriced. Should be stated I got my "dine all day" deal ticket included (because I'm a Veteran) for only $80 and it still feels barely worth it. Better to get the drink all day and only get food you really want once. And last... Customer service was literally ZERO. Everyone we interacted with, every ride operator, every cashier looked and acted absolutely miserable. It was weird. Maybe if all the work areas weren't 90 degrees. Pretty bad showing. I won't be back for at least another 25 years.

Absolutely horrible first experience! We went Dec 30th and paid for all day dining. Got to the park at 130pm and left at 9pm. During that time we got on 1 ride and 1 meal due to lines at a minimum of 2 hours to 4.5 hours! Both rides and food. We went to guest services to ask for some type of credit and basically got an email and phone number to call. Sent an email on Dec 31 and it's Jan3 and no response back. Definitely disappointed and might not ever go back! Lack of communication and lack response is disappointing.

Updating my review to improve the score because Busch Gardens outdid themselves with the 2023 annual pass members summer party they had for those who visited 20 times this summer.

Whoever put this party together should be commended for far exceeding what was expected for Busch Gardens, especially lately. Almost everything they did that night was magical. From the champagne, including mocktails, as you entered, to allowing us to pick from whatever fancy drinks we wanted (times two), to snacks (x2) and even one free play on the fair games where every single person could have won a stuffed animal. No lines for the four coasters they had open this Special night! And then to blow our minds by handing out $35 huge beach towels AND a gourmet cupcake to go! OMG

Being an annual pass holder this summer really meant something!

They also recently had a special day in July where they allowed us to tour one of the haunted houses with a couple surprises at the end AND at the Giraffe Bar had Santa Clause available to visit.

They are building a new coaster in Pantopia where Sand Serpent and a couple kids rides were located, so excited to see what that will be since they've already cleared the land but made no announcement yet.

Thanks for improving Tampa's #1 playground destination and making it more fun again!

Now if you could just get the sky ride open after being down the past five years, that would take the rating to five stars.

Busch Gardens has a lot of rides for adults! Huge roller coasters, and exhilarating rides! If that's not for you then it may not be worth it to go. Came here during the last week of the year and it was extremely packed, over packed. Wait times were insane over 2-3 hours long. However the Christmas stuff was nice to see! Busch gardens does have a variety of food options and if you're looking for a good deal, do their all day dining!

What a waste of time and money!! Almost everything was closed, no shows open and long wait times for the few rides available.
Very disappointing. At 0ver $300 for two adults this was ridiculous. Not impressed at all
Worst park experience I've ever experienced. Everything is additional cost or not open. Not worth your precious time.

This place was a great time for me and my family! We had so much fun with all the rides, found them to be a lot of fun and updated with the times. My favorite was the Iron kawazi it was my favorite rollercoaster here. We also really enjoyed the train ride as it showed us the animals and helped us get to the other side of the park with ease.
What I didn't enjoy was not being informed when I purchased my ticket that it was grad night for a bunch of middle schoolers. Not sure if the park knew themselves but it was horrible (lol) and the monkey habitat was disgusting with bird poop everywhere and I only saw 1 gorilla. I also did not see the tigers or lions :/

The Sesame Street for the little kids was amazing! So many rides and giant jungle gym that they will surely enjoy

Doing tourist things this was on the list of to-do's on our family vacation to the Tampa/Bradenton beach area.....Great place to see all the different things Busch Gardens had to offer. This park was a great time for us as a family.
The food stations/vendors had a variety of menus and were sporadic throughout the park. and pricing was pretty expensive considering the quality of foods....we did the all day dining option.... probably would do again.... The BBQ place had a staffing issue that caused a HUGE delay. In line over 50 mins. Whoever is in charge of logistics for the food staffing should go to Disney...see how they do things and do that here... I would definitely smooth things out. I applaud the Chick-fil-A station.... They had a better process.

The beverage stands had reasonable prices and I was pleasantly surprised.
We got to see quite a few animals.
I highly recommend the train ride...


The parking is out a ways...but I saw tram lines and teams taking people to outskirts parking lots. We had a handicap parking placard thanks to the in-laws... I am in firm belief that the overall planning and logistics of this park are professionally done and handled fairly well.

This venue is easy to get to if in the area and can go to a Busch gardens
The family had fun and that is what matters most!!
7/10 times I would return...
The sesame street experience and all the toddler rides didn't disappoint!

All in all if they smooth out a few things... I'm quite sure we would return. (Questions reach out)

Respectfully,

Bryan
Oklahoma City

I've been coming here for 20 years and it's been getting more and more expensive with less enjoyment. Beers, food, souvenirs. Everything is overpriced. They used to charge $1-$2 for souvenir cup refills now it's $10 for the day. Rides will be closed if there's not enough attendance. At the end of the day they just close random rides because they can. BG isn't what it used to be. Save your money and go to Universal.

More parking lot trams needed too long of a wait
NO healthy food to buy no fruit
In the park
Many attractions were closed with no notice online
No tigers or monkeys to be seen all were inside away from viewing
Some coasters closed

One of N. America's largest zoos located right here in Tampa Bay! There are so many things to do here. If you aren't one for rides you can definitely see the various animals and shows they have throughout the day. There are even animal encounters where can interact and feed the animals. They have a lot of thrill rides and roller coasters.

They also have the Sesame Street Safari area that is dedicated to the kiddos. This area has rides for the kids as well as an above ground rope course and water play area. You can even book breakfast and lunch with Elmo and friends!

Various times of the year they have other special engagements happening such as food and wine.

I've literally been coming here since I was little.
This place is amazing! The rides are really cool and fun, and the ambiance is really nice. I love this place.

Went for a visit during Christmas nights and the park was decorated beautifully. I love walking through seeing the animals and all the lights for the holiday season. Taking the skyride so you can see all the lights from above is always a pleasure.

First visit in prolly ten years! Brought the kiddo out for a visit (5) and the weather ended up turning on us. Park was relatively clean and the staff was friendly per usual. Unfortunately most of the rides ended up closing due to lighting in the distance. My only complaint is that the staff seemed a bit disorganized. They left several rides open and when we waited thru the lines and got on we had other staff members come up and tell the operators that the ride was supposed to be closed and they made an announcement. This happened twice. All in all it's a fun park and just a blah experience but I will still be coming back.

Always a family favorite. I can't give this anything less than a five star review. I respect the veteran appreciation days and really enjoy spending time seeing the animals and riding rides with the family. I would love to see bigger free beers (hint hint) from a place named after beer "Busch" but I understand, what could go wrong drinking and riding extreme coasters!
The new Serengeti Flyer is an instant favorite!

My favorite part of the day was watching the delightful and sometimes death-defying dancing from the skaters in Christmas on Ice. It's a 22 minute show that's worth the comfortable seated wait in the Marakesh Theater. The train ride was a little lackluster. I think I used to see a lot more variety of animals. However it as a beautiful day to ride around the park. No other stations were open so it was a round-trip to Nairobi. Lines for most rides took under 30 minutes. That's pretty good for the holiday season and a Saturday. However the Cheetah roller coaster needs to be revamped so that more cars are on the track and lines can move quickly. My friends noticed that paint was peeling around the park. It's evident that they need more employees. Prices are outrageous? It shouldn't cost over $13 for a pretzel hotdog! Parking is $38! Hire more staff, take care of the park appearance and bring prices back down to make this a more tantalizing park.
I enjoyed the aviary and many of the rides, however, many of the rides and other things were shut down, and the bathrooms were gross.

I can said now the current Busch Garden is lower their standard tremendously. It is drop of two stars from my previous review. Here are reasons why.

1. The tram service do not take you to the gate. We were parking on the overflow parking lot and the tram drop off on the main parking lot. We have to walk 15 minutes from the main parking lot. This is a change from our previous visit. However they do pick up at night at the gate to parking lot.

2. Show cut to 3 different show in the park. We use to see at lease six different shows. Since they cut the difference show. But didn't increase the frequency of the presence show. Only three shows for each different show. This cause majority cannot get in to the show. Basically three show only allow 1500 people to enjoy each show with minimum 10000 park goers. That doesn't measure up.

3. Food line is long. Each time to line up for the food is one hour. Even though they have two line each restaurant or cafe. But the people who assemble the food is only one person. There are a lot of kitchen crew but the order line is only one person each line. The line is slowly moving. Why they cannot hire a person each line. With $15 an hour rate. They should be able to afford it. Which mean only $300 a day. The price of the food in the park are not cheap. The customer at least deserves a faster line for the price paid. If the restaurant set outside the park, they will be no customers visit because of the experience.

4. I saw the park change the system in the park on the rapid. If you want to shot water gun to the rapid goers below, you have to paid.

With so many change, this park becomes done that been there situation. At least Florida residents like me, will not revisit.
The rides are dated, service is non existent, food is below average for amusement park food, and lines are overly long everywhere. The positive is the animals are cute.

I've been an annual pass holder for the past 10 years. Keep in mind that this includes the BG in Virginia (which is sooo much better than this one!) I finally bit the bullet and canceled my pass to move on to Universal parks. The benefits of this pass have declined and it's no longer an attractive offer. First of all, you get preferred parking with your annual pass. However, 95 percent of the time I visit, they tell me the lot is full. Full? Really? Again? I started ignoring this and have ALWAYS been able to find a space in the preferred lot. Secondly, the pass no longer offers 50 percent discount on tickets. And lastly, you still get free guest tickets, but can only use one at a time per quarter.

The park itself is great, beautifully decorated during the holidays. The variety of shows is somewhat lacking, but the ones they do offer are excellent. The best one is the ice skating show. Love that they do pictures with Rudolph, the reindeer crew, at Christmas. The rides are thrilling and worth the wait in the lines. My favorite is iron gwazi.

Now, the onto what needs improving.... the food! The food is mediocre. Compared to the food at the BG Virginia where they offer cuisine appropriate to the country you're visiting (the theme there is the European countries), the food here is not theme park worthy. They do have some diverse offerings during food festivals, but they really should offer that all the time.

Bring the better benefits back to the passes, improve the food (maybe put in a couple nice full service restaurants) and stop lying to me about the preferred parking lot being full and maybe I'll consider coming back. Until then, I'm a universal pass holder

Love Busch gardens. They have great roller coasters and they continue to add new ones. They have great food and drink options. Christmastown when the whole park is lit up is magical. Best time of the year. Great deal for those that live in Tampa to get a fun card or an annual pass.

My husband and I chose Busch gardens of all the Disney options in Florida because we wanted some fun rides and we got them :)

We arrived right at opening (10am) and left right at closing (5pm) in January. We definitely needed the whole time to see and do everything. We successfully did all the rides minus the Sesame Street kid land, the water ride that wasn't open, and one coaster that wasn't operating. We were running around the park to try and do everything and we barely made it to Zagora cafe to close out our all day dining pass with one final meal at 4:30 before the park closed. Note: Zagora was the only place open at this time, so if you are looking for food close to park closing don't do what we did and run in a circle around the park, go straight here.

The rides were a blast: I loved the iron gwazi the best, and the drop that tilts you to oook straight down before releasing into free fall. So we did those ones twice.

The longest line on a Tuesday at the end of January was about 20 minutes. Even then, we barely got all the rides in during the parks hours so there is plenty to do even though it didn't seem like it at first glance.

I also thoroughly enjoyed the animal exhibits. Normally I'm not into zoos but I felt good about the care of the animals here. The safari train ride was cool as were the bird aviaries. I really enjoyed the orangutans who were right up next to the view point saying hello to everyone.

It's a fun park because it mixed the animals encounters with the thrills for a diverse day.

They have a great team system from the parking lot. I would not park off the lot because the surrounding area is pretty sketchy feeling.

I will say that Busch Gardens was definitely hard to do in one day. The placement of coasters/attractions are very far apart, with not a lot of food options in between, but plenty of strange carnival like games. Not too mention, it is safari hot here in Southwest Florida. Thank goodness for the train, if it's in service while you are there. About third of the rides were out of service.

I am grateful I won tickets with a parking pass so I only had to pay $45 for two orders of Chik-Fil-A nuggets, waffle fries, and drinks. Food definitely overpriced but it is a theme park. However, the food at the buffet was much more reasonably priced and more for your money than at Chik.

The employees were not too welcoming, and the train employees didn't seem to want to do their job keeping people safely away from entrances/exits. The employees on the coasters didn't seem to really care if you were locked in on the rides, which is quite concerning, especially when it is the Iron Gwazi.

Definitely overrated, overpriced, and disgusting. And why does everyone drink at theme parks down here? I get that it's five o' clock somewhere but you are seriously at a theme park, with your kids. Why do they make drinking and driving so easily obtainable here? Its a theme park, not a bar.

Would I go again? I do have two more passes but I think I'll just let them expire and save up for a trip to Universal or Disney.
Will never go here again.... Loooong lines...and the resteraunts had longs lines as well.

Large family friendly amusement/safari park. The animal attraction was great especially for younger kids. Park is Dated. Not up to date on accessibility or easy to navigate. Despite busy holiday season most gift shops were out of snack products; choices were soo limited Rollercoaster choices also limited. Rollercoaster choices awesome. Several
Rides were closed. The parking lot was overfill, no attendants with cars parked in non spots blocking exit.

Not what it used to be. The animal parts of it were the highlight for us. We have done the giraffe add on where you feed the giraffes after a jeep ride and this time we did the Gorilla training which was cool, but not so different than what you can see without paying the extra $40pp. Poor signage throughout the park and a lack of staff added to our okayish experience.
My husband liked the big rollercoasters but long waits and some shutdowns made that also okayish. My daughter doesn't like the big rollercoasters so we finally settled on the sky ride only to find it was an additional $5 per person. We had already spent $500 for admission and parking for 3 people and one animal experience so that just didn't sit well.
We were misdirected or given misinformation 3 times by staff, had to wait in a huge line to get in even with prepaid tickets, and found that the preferred parking we prepaid for was also full. Overall, was just unimpressed and felt like it was old and tire compared to what it used to be. They lost a lot of the charm but added a lot to the pricing.
Had a great time with friends at the park. Customer service was exceptional. Food was pretty good as well.

I gotta give the Busch Gardens lost and found some love. My husband decided to take his most expensive pair of sunglasses to Busch gardens (*eye roll*) even after I told him not to lol. Long story short they flew off during one of the roller coast rides and LUCKILY our friend who didn't go on the ride saw them fly off and land on one of the nets right outside the ride. We reported it to lost and found and within 3-ish hours, they called us back and were able to retrieve them.

The theme park itself: well, it's a theme park. It's mostly busy most of the year, but come on a Monday off season and you can get on every ride with a 10 minute or less wait.

Howl-o-scream I don't personally care for. We went for a second time this past October and only got to experience 3 of the 5 haunted houses before the park closed even tho we arrived at 7pm. Our last house we waited about 2.5 hours and it wasn't even thrilling.

I always have a fun when I go to Busch gardens. It's one of my favorite theme parks. I love how they are always bringing new rides to the park or updating the current rides! There are so many roller coasters (which are my favorite things!) and I love that there are animals throughout the park to look at while you're walking around. Unfortunately I don't go that often, during the summer is so so hot and it's too brutal to go but as soon as it calms down, I make plans to go!

We always have a great time visiting Busch Gardens. This time it's for spring break and boy were we ready! We went to the aviary and were able to get two parakeets to land on us. My son was super excited. He raved about it all the way back home. I especially love visiting because there is so much to do besides just doing rides. It's creates a unique experience for both adults and children.

Expensive park but very few rides and not much to look at with the safari train. Bad service from the restaurants. Do not get the all day dining pass. You can't get most of the stuff , better to just pay for what you eat. Worst amusement park I've been to.
so much fun! great animal habitats and fun roller coasters. I liked the tiger the most
Very rude. Needs to have manners and kmust be taught how to treat people.No one knows the correct way of. The wait times. If you see this guy be ready for a nasty attitude

Busch Gardens is a fun, educational, thrill seekers playground. From things like the Ice Show, to the endless tours and animal experiences (feed a Giraffe or a Kangaroo- you can here!), to some to thrill rides like Iron Gwazi, this park has something for everyone.

The Ambassadors were all friendly and smiling (maybe it's the magic of Christmas Town), and work so hard to keep areas clean and the ride's running smoothly. They have lots of locations to get drinks, which is helpful on a hot day. We really enjoy the down season during the weekday when you can go at your own pace, see what you want, ride what you want, and leave feeling happy and exhausted from a long day. If you have the opportunity to go during the weekday, you would be surprised by the difference it makes in the park.
So so. Once you've done it no need to repeat. Rides are short. Food is expensive. You can do almost all the rides in one day.

Super fun for all ages. My 2.5 year old loved riding all the Sesame Street rides and watching the show. She also loved looking at all the animals. My older nieces and nephews and the adults loved the roller coasters. We did the all day dining pass and ate every 90 minutes which was so worth it- the pizza and bbq place were the best. Everyone had a blast and we will definitely be going back!
This place is awesome!!! You can have fun with all your family members! Especially if you have kids!!! They love those places

so this is a theme park mixed with a zoo. it's not cheap. it's big and not shaded. you pay for parking. food is like any other theme park, mediocre, bring your own food and water.
given that- if you carpool, come early and not in peak season you'll likely have a better time. LOTS of fun rides, lots of gift shops and lots of animals (cheetah, penguins, a safari train ride with zebra/rhino etc).
it's a unique place. parking and getting there is a pain. you park in the lot and wait in a long line for a big outdoor tram to take you to the gate. it's hot, and it's sunny. lots of walking.
it's a good place for teenager to go. small kids will like some of the rides and the elmo area and the animals.
if you plan on going for the day, review a map so you have an idea of how you want to navigate the park.
if you want to do all the rides etc and be relaxed- it's much better splitting up in 2 days.
i think it's a fun place if you know what you are getting into. it's definitely worth going for a fun time- but know it's not cheap and lots of walking in the sun.

A few decent rides, but lines were absolutely horrible. Every ride had over a 2 hour wait. Every food stand had at least an hour or two wait, and they were out of almost everything except for drinks. I will not be coming back. Don't waste your time or money here

Can never go wrong with an amusement park. I'm from VA and went every summer, Christmas, Halloween to Busch gardens and I could not wait to see what Tampa had going on. I would definitely recommend buying the fast track but figure out what rides you def want to ride bc the one we purchased did not come with ALL the rides. But over all our experience was soooo fun! Loved the train ride to see the animals and the rides seem to be the same as the Williamsburg just with diff names.

The kids really enjoy going to Busch Gardens. The kids especially enjoy the Sesame Street Safari of Fun and seeing all the animals! I like going for all the themed seasonal festivals and for all the love concerts and shows!

While Busch Gardens doesn't have the legendary Disney cartoon theming or characters and is not as polished as disney, it is still a nice experience without a 1.5 hour car ride, locally on Tampa! Busch Gardens let's you come back all year with a fun pass for the price of a 1 day admission which is a great value! If you're visiting Tampa with young or middle aged children, you'll definitely enjoy a visit to Busch Gardens!

Busch Gardens is so underrated! This was my first time visiting the park and I throughly enjoyed my time here. They have a wide selection of rides and offer an all day eating pass, which was super convenient. While there, there were little to no wait times (not sure if that was normal for this time of year or just luck). I will definitely be back!

We went there on January 15 also MLK so many rides were down a lot of places in this park we're not open it was not a great experience they did give us tickets to come back. We'll see what happens next time.
A downside is we paid for a package we had VIP parking we had all day food passes and we had the skip the line . When we go back, it's just the regular ticket to get in. so at this point, I am thinking they could do a lot better

BGT has really gone downhill over the years. I remember the park being beautiful and very well run a decade ago. I was disappointed to see the state of the park when I visited last week. Gardens were not as well maintained. Bathrooms were in dire need of upgrades. Many attractions and areas closed with others running at super low capacity.

I get a near equal experience at a Cedar Fair park for 2/3 the price amd I can get a way better experience at Disney or Universal for just a few dollars more. BG and Sea World need to improve their operations or it will catch up to them.

It's crazy to me that it takes over an hour just to park your car for their Halloween event. They have no police presence to help direct the traffic, no workers helping direct traffic. It's absurd. Literally sit in traffic for 1-2 hours before you can actually get into the park. Such a rip off to do a huge event at a huge park and not have any direction for traffic.

Der Besuch der Busch Gardens, Tampa war eine bittere Enttäuschung von Anfang bis Ende. Allein fürs Parken waren 43 $ fällig - total ungerechtfertigt, wie wir finden. Ca 500 Meter vor dem Parkeingang mussten wir uns an einer langen Schlange anstellen. Etwa 40 Minuten später durften wir den Park endlich betreten, nur um herauszufinden, dass sehr viele Attraktionen geschossen waren, wobei die übrigen nicht zu voller Kapazität liefen oder Wartezeiten von über 120 Minuten hatten. Barzahlungen werden im gesamten Park nicht akzeptiert und Trinkgelder werden nicht nur erwartet, sondern aktiv gefordert. Der Park sieht sehr ungeplfegt aus, die Karte in der App ist total unübersichtlich, die Preise sind astronomisch. Manche Fahrgeschäfte mussten noch extra bezahlt werden, wobei man meinen könnte, dass der etwa 100$ (pro Person) Eintritt alles abdecken sollte. Circa zwei Stunden später, nachdem unsere Geduld aufgebraucht war und wir zunehmend frustrierter wurden, haben wir uns dazu entschieden, den Park zu verlassen, was anhand der unübersichtlichen Karte ebenfalls eine echte Herausforderung war. Endlich am Auto angekommen, wussten wir, dass wir die Busch Gardens nie wieder besuchen werden und hoffen durch diesen Beitrag auch andere Touristen davor zu warnen bzw. abzuhalten den Park zu besuchen. Es ist nichts Anders als eine unangenehme Zeit- und Geldverschwendung.

I live in Tampa and go to Busch gardens on a regular basis. I am also a platinum member but will downgrade to silver. I normally make a stop at the barbeque place by Tigris. I have to say that the quality of service and food has gone down the crapper. I waited for an hour and a half, it's insane, to order food. As I approached to order my meal, they blared out that they had run out of mac and cheese, and ribs. They managed to cook ribs but made the delay longer. I believe at some point the manager came out to address the kids (staff). I believe this is a management issue, and they should really look into training and holding managers accountable. If this continues, there will be nothing to manage. I do like the place a lot and would hate to lose it due to poor management.

well this park really needs an update of how they treat there customers. Last I went here was in 1984 or 1985. I had invited my friend for here since it's been a long time and I'm disappointed in some ways because of how they have an online website but when you try to scan your admission the guest services should really know how to scan versus bringing you back to the ticket line which waste more time. that extra day for the concession is not even worth it because after a howl and scream they limit you going over there. after this I don't think I would ever come over here there are ways need to change they're still living in the 1990s otherwise amusement parks like this place is way too expensive or overpriced thus 3 stars

During these hot summer days, having an annual pass means you can come and go as you please. With the silver pass we also get complimentary parking - $35 daily or $55 for preferred. This month I got a bonus pass and I can buy 2 extra passes for $40 each. I also get $10 to spend on souvenirs, or food this month.

So much is closed (SJ
My tide, every train station except Nairobi) or broken(fountain drink machines)
Customer service personnel, especially in restaurants had no customer service training or they just don't care. A cashier in oasis pizza wanted to be anywhere but at work, gave short answers, gave the side eye and was just down right rude.
We asked a cashier at the Village food stand by the restrooms if it was a dine all day establishment and she shook her head and rolled her eyes. We've been pass holders off and on for years, we're very excited to be able to have season passes this year, and are re thinking our decision. We aren't asking for staff to be our best friend? Just common courtesy.

I got tickets for myself and my children through the "Waves of Honor" program (I am a combat veteran). I was really looking forward to going there and it was my first trip to Tampa. Before I left I booked a preferred parking spot so I would be closer to the entrance; I have knee and back issues. We left Melbourne five hours before they opened (It takes three hours to get there) so we would be there well before they opened. When we got closer the traffic was horrendous. Really only one to get into the park. We waited for over an hour to get into the park. Once we arrived at the first booth, I explained that we had already paid for preferred parkin. The young lady looked surprised and said I dont't think you will get to park there; she recommended that I go to guest services to get my money back. As we proceeded to the parking lot we were directed to a parking lot that was not preferred parking. The girl was EXTREMELY rude and yelled at us to keep moving. In the meantime I watched two other cars get directed to the preferred parking. We were not allowed to park there even though I had paid for it and it was not cheap. As we were walking toward the shuttle/bus area I saw even more people parking there. The prices are outrageous and the lines move VERY slowly. Most of the employees were very rude. The sky ride and the safari are extra $$. Most of the rides were shut down. It is not like me to complain when something was free, but it was not enjoyable at all. I stood in line for an hour and 55 minutes to get my credit; that was time I could not spend with my family. It was suppose to be a relaxing day with my kids and it turned into the WORST experience I have ever had at an amusement park and I have been to quite a few. Guest services did refund my money for the parking and offered me free food and drink vouchers for my next visit. I declined the vouchers and advised them I will never return. The young lady could have cared less about my experience. This was the first time I have ever used a veteran benefit and it was a horrible experience. Next year I am going to Aquatica instead. Hopefully that will be a better experience.

We came here a few years back and the roller coasters were fun, but had much too long of a wait without the quick queue (which really is every amusement park now). The zoo area was very fun though and we had a blast feeding the giraffes. It's a pretty good place if you're primarily going to see the animals, but we probably wouldn't come back again.

Amazing place you can tell they care for their animals and they care for the guests. My only complaint is the security sucks if someone takes anything from you, my son's blanket was taken off of our stroller and they gave no care whatsoever.

I've been trying to pay for a ticket for over an hour. We came here with our grandchildren and are having credit card problems with Bank of America because we are away from our home and have had a recent change of address. There is no real person here to help us ! The automation sucks!!!

I get that corporate policies are there for a reason but I feel like the ride attendant was taking her frustration out on us when my friend left his phone on the tram. She started gossiping with her coworkers and laughing at us while we were waiting for a supervisor to release his phone.

Rides are really fun and they have a great selection of coasters.

The giraffe experience was also a highlight, along with some of the bigger coasters. Bernie is awesome! Kind, gentle, curious, and very interactive. (I'm sure the other giraffes are also wonderful, but we spent our time with Bernie.) I do wish they didn't hit you with another $45/head, though. $15-25 per person would have been much more reasonable.

As for the food, don't expect much. It might have been better if the Oasis Pizza place hadn't been shut or if we hadn't missed the closing time for the BBQ place - two of the better reviewed restaurants at the park. So, maybe there is better food available than what we had.

The all-day food pass is worth while (especially if you can get it on sale for $30/head), but only because the prices are otherwise insane (i.e., pretty much what you'd expect at an amusement park). I'm serious though -- without the pass, a family of four could easily spend upwards of $160+ for a lunch of burgers and fries.

However, as a comparison, I would say that their food was infinitely better than the swill we paid dearly for in DisneyLand many years ago. Ugh. I'd have to make a concerted effort to make food that bad (Disney).

It's great to have a mix of animals and rides. Watching the cheetah training was also really neat (and no extra cost).

If you go during the off season, you'll likely have the run of the place. That's awesome. However, certain things will be closed or close early - not so good. You probably don't need the quick-pass during off season. We certainly did not. It's definitely worth going during off season, just know what to expect.

So much better than Disney's parks in so many ways and a much better value. I'm never going back to Disney.

If I could give this place a zero star I would. Most ghetto theme park ever, employees rude asf and I mean RUDE!!! Also good luck finding the exit. I spent literally 20 mins trying to find an exit and yes I followed the exit signs but they led to dead ends. Food was disgusting and cold. Really mad I wasted my time, money and gas to drive to Tampa from Daytona for a sub par theme park. Y'all really need to do better I won't be coming back

Just a small selection of my roller coasters that I have ridden- these photos are from Busch Gardens in Tampa. They have some great coasters if that is what you are looking for. Most times, however, they do not run enough trains on the tracks causing a longer line than necessary.

Upon on arrival the security line was insanely long and there was no order or class. When we went to the pass holder line there was a small sign indicating it was for platinum members. Unlike Seaworld we were waiting in a long line for over 25 minutes in the blazing hot sun. The employees scanning people in seemed like they hated their jobs and barely interacted with anyone except their coworkers. The guy that scanned me in was wearing a hideous black and white furry beanie and the only thing he was to say was a grunt. The bathrooms upon entry were absolutely disgusting and smelled terrible. After that we went onto Montu where the employee helping us was wearing the most disturbing and creepy X eye contacts. Once in the ride the ride employees barely said anything they looked as if they hated their jobs. We then went to look for the pass holder lounge come to find out that was no more by two employees and who were standing at the Treetop kitchen looking miserable. We then asked them if the food was good and they kinda just looked at us funny. From here we went to get on Congo River Rapids but once we had gotten there there was a sign saying it was closed yet in the app there was no indication of that. It then started pouring cats and dogs and the staff was doing a whole lot of nothing One girl was even on a phone call while another was sitting on a counter. There should be better rainfall protocols such as having employees start clearing the park or at least giving updates. The employees looked lost and confused when asked if the rides would go back on. The food was decent but the employees at the burger bar were absolutely disrespectful, rude, and overall terrible. My boyfriend and I were being kind and simply asked for no cheese and the lady scoffed at us and tossed out food to us. When we said thank you all she did was yell for next. Then we went to the topping bar and there was no more Pico de Gayo and when i said that the employee that was standing there then got a new one. What was the point of having someone stand there if she wasn't keeping it stocked? The tables that were empty were filthy and the employee that was cleaning the tables was moving at a turtle like speed. When we went to grab drinks there was no ice and no one seemed to change it. This sucked since the weather before raining was terribly hot. If it wasn't for the great rides and nice employees at the pay to win games we would have left earlier.
Busch Gardens on your website says parking $32 but when you pull up it says $40... smallest parking spots ever

Today Saturday September 14 there were so many closed rides. And many closed con session stands. It was as if the park was only half staffed. Very frustrating especially when you only have one chance to visit. Also, there were many animal exhibits with no animals.

Very strange day.and then to find out that if you don't pay extra for a special event they close the park four hours early. No warning when you buy tickets at the gate.

visited Busch gardens on Wednesday Nov 25th. there were 6 in our party. we were all equally disappointed in Busch Gardens and agreed we would not be returning with our grandchildren. a lot of the events were closed for maintenance or closed for staffing issues. many of the animal enclosures were empty.
Parking fees were too high and food was sub standard.
looks like you are catering for those who enjoy roller coasters, not the best environment for seniors.
never again.

if you want to enjoy the rides get the cut in line passes. Otherwise you'll wait over an hour and a half watching them get not only preferred treatment but first class practicality no waiting treatment. I watched them not seat standard passengers because line skip passengers were still showing up.
I understand they paid extra but this is bad

Park sucks! One car for Sheikra, other rides operating at half capacity. So even though the park wasn't crowded, one still had to wait in long lines (50 minute waits at least). Food prices are outrageous. $6.50 for a coke, $11.50 for an average size pretzel. Disney prices for food are cheaper!! Cheeta Hunt not even open day of visit. And now, one has to pay for the Skyride as if it's something extra, and $4 for a locker that is REQUIRED by the park. Will NOT be renewing annual pass or purchasing funcard again. DO BETTER!!

I live in the area, and has been to Busch Gardens as a kid. My wife and I decided to go today, a thing we'd been thinking about for awhile.

We got to the park early, around 10:15am. We went up one side of the park, until we found a ride... closed. We made it to the next on our list: closed. And a third: closed (Stanley Falls Flume). Finally, a fourth ride (Congo River Rapids) has a line in front and sign they'd open at 11am. They didn't... in fact, the staff didn't even arrive until after 11am. It wasn't until about 11:20 that they told us this ride, too, is closed. We finally got to ride on the bumper cars and then Phoenix Rising (a small rollercoaster)... now it was past noon.

This in no way to run a park. They still got our excessive $40 for parking and $180 admissions.

My family paid for ANNUAL platinum park attendance. Nothing really is helpful to pay more for this better membership. We went to the "Sky Lift" to get on and they wanted $5.00 each for our guest passes on our membership. We were hoping for a good "Smoke House" dinner only to get brisket in a plastic wrap with tons of FAT in only 4 small slices for $25.00 a plate !! No smokehouse barbeque sauce only plastic squeeze packs, nothing came with the 4 slices other than a few fries. So, 2 plates at $50.00 that we almost got sick at our stomachs trying to eat and spit a lot of the FAT out. Very disappointing. Most ALL shows were closed other than Cookie monster. ALL roller coasters and water rides were UNDER construction. If you need water or a soda, be prepared to pay $8.00 or more. Ice cream $8.00. Most food plates of only 2 items (fries as one) - $18.00 or higher. We went for the exercise mostly but after paying extra for a Platinum membership, we didn't feel like it was good. NOW we are stuck with an annual membership that is a monthly fee for one year !!!!

I had a conference in Tampa and made a vacation out of it and spent some extra time in town. My good buddy lives in Tampa and we decided to hit up Busch Gardens on a weekday. The nice thing about visiting an amusement park on a weekday is that the lines are so much shorter than what you would experience on a weekend. While we were there, that meant less than 5 minutes of waiting time per ride, and oftentimes, even less than that or no wait at all. So went all out!

We rode every single rollercoaster and really any ride that seemed halfway entertaining. We hit them all in rapid succession to the point where my buddy threw up on one of the rollercoasters. I was dying laughing! I hate waiting in line so being able to breeze through things was a big plus. In between the rides, it was nice to check out the animals too. The highlight for me were the lions. Busch Gardens is African safari-themed and you can pay extra to go on a safari and get closer to the animals. We didn't do that but still enjoyed checking them out from a distance. We had some food too. Your typical amusement park fare. Nothing special.

I have to knock off a star for a couple of reasons. One, tickets were more expensive than I expected. In addition to your ticket, you have to pay for parking too so it ends up being a little pricey. A few of the rides were also closed while we were there. And not just one or two but several popular rides. They didn't mention all of the rides that were closed when we were paying to get in which I think is something that should be displayed prominently so people know what they're getting into. Or, at the very least, offer a discount if a significant number of rides are closed down.

Don't get me wrong, I had a good time. We even experienced a torrential downpour and didn't have umbrellas to help us out. All part of the experience.

We had a fantastic time, but mostly because of the timing. We went the day after hurricane Idalia passed through and the park was empty.
Walked into every ride, multiple times.
No crowds and eerily quiet (which was fantastic)!

The rides were great, lots of animals in big enclosures.

This Busch Gardens was not as good as the one in Williamsburg, VA in my opinion due to the lack of shade. It was hot!
Wish they had some air conditioned spaces to relax between rides when it is hot.

Overall, we would return!

we came mid October (10/14). Half the rides were closed, half the food/ other vendors were closed.... Staff was super unfriendly and ignored us. not worth the money even with the half off discount online.... Maybe you should advertise that more than 30% of the rides and exhibits are closed

It was fun to see some of the animals. But having grown up in the area, remembering when admission was free, it was disappointing.
The appearance of the park was run down in many areas, all water rides were closed, safari ride was closed unless you wanted to pay additional fee to take pay safari tour. I understand the fee is around $60.00 per person. Wondering why one safari tour could run but not the other.
Several coasters were closed, lions, tigers weren't out.
It was disappointing to say the least. For an hour further north you have amazing, knock your socks off parks with Disney or Universal.

Went for howl-o-scream. Was a fun night and highly recommend it. Reason it's a 3 star is they are severely under staffed, not enough new additions to try, they have a safety issue with the fog. At one point they pumped so much I could see and almost hurt myself. Fog is great within open spaces but in a close space with curbs and fences, it should be more controlled. With that said if you're looking for a fun event with no crowds, this is the place to be.

I've loved Busch Gardens as a child and I still find it fun as an adult. So much that I buy an annual pass year after year. Honestly you can't beat the price and the perks of the pass especially when you compare it to Disney and universal. They have a lot of solid roller coasters and the shows in the Moroccan palace are actually quite good.. I believe this is a wonderful theme park aimed at people of all sorts of ages which is great!

I've been to the food and wine festival a few times and unfortunately the past time it was a disappointment. But the park has many yearly events like mardi gras, 4th of July, and and of course Halloween horror nights. If you need to eat in the park I would suggest the Zambia smokehouse.

Be sure to check the website for attraction closures. Even though some of the rides are a bit dated they are they are constantly improving things and have added two new rollercoasters within the past 5 years. One opens in March!

We haven't been to Busch Gardens since a Hawl-O-Scream visit several years ago and were looking forward to attending the park during their annual Christmas Town Event (Nov 12 thru Jan 2). As I mentioned in my recent review of SeaWorld Orlando, on Black Friday 2021, we purchased our SeaWorld & Busch Gardens Tampa two park pass (for two) totaling $131.98, definitely a great deal!

SeaWorld is the parent company to Busch Gardens, so my 3-complaints from my SeaWorld review remain. (1) $30 parking is expensive, why not include this into the cost of the ticket? (2) Food and drink prices can be excessive, $10.99 for a beer, $4.70 for bottled water, $6 for a cookie WTF? (3) once again, no paper map. Guests must use a QR code to download a map or take a photo at the entrance, then continuously use your phone to navigate the park, this is beyond frustrating. Do yourself a favor and download the map from their website and print a copy or two to take with you.

Busch Gardens has a good variety of rides to choose from, and they are all a blast. Unfortunately, on this occasion, Skyride, Congo River Rapids, Cheetah Hunt, and Kumba were closed for repair. In addition, Falcon's Fury closed early, Montu closed down due to heavy rainfall (of course, as were we next in line), and the Iron Gwazi does not open until March 2022. As you can see, we didn't have the best run with roller coasters on this visit, but we still had fun on Tigris, Cobra's Curse, Sheikra, and the Stanley Falls Flume.

I enjoyed that Busch Gardens has a balanced selection of rides, animal encounters, and activities to choose from. There is a lot to do here for anyone, and you can easily spend an entire day exploring. We came for the whole Christmas experience, and it was beautiful, but some of the Festive Shows, such as the 'Christmas on Ice,' were not offered because we came on a Tuesday.

We were not too impressed with the food at Busch Gardens, but if you can wait until Christmas Town opens at dark (6 pm) you have an opportunity to try some of the better food the park has to offer by purchasing one of the two food and beverage sampler packages available. The 8-option is $44.99 & the 12-option is $64.99. Just know that if you order a beer, it is served in what looks to be about 8oz cup, yet the food looks better than many other options we viewed/sampled throughout the day.

My two cents on the Nairobi Train Station's Serengeti Express: Ride the train during the day when you can actually see the animals. We went at nighttime thinking we would see a magnificent light show, but all you see is pitch black darkness until you get back. The best part of the whole ride was when everyone started howling like wolves once inside the tunnel.

Something to note: Elmo's Christmas Wish is the same storyline as the one at SeaWorld and is performed outside in the Sesame Street section of the park. The show is adorable, and the kids seem to love it. You can sit very close to the stage, and the characters will do a photo op with kids/adults after the show. There is also a large playground/jungle gym nearby for kids to play while waiting for the show to start. Nancy and I got in there and had so much fun!

Once again, some minor issues here, just like SeaWorld, but nothing I would consider a deal-breaker, especially since we got such a fantastic deal on our tickets. If you have never been to Busch Gardens or if you are looking for something fun to do in Tampa, I highly recommend a visit here; however, look around for deals online through Groupon or AAA.com.

This review is based on my most recent experience at the park. When we got to the park, at the 2nd entrance (once you've paid and entered with your ticket) there is a sign that lists all the rollercoasters that are not not in service for the day. There was 4 listed. REALLY? Four... not counting Gazi. There are only 9 in total and technically 5 were not working?!?!

Now, I 100% get there are probably major reasons for them to be down that include safety but that just seems insane to me that they would still charge full price for less than half of the rollercoasters which is the only reason we were there and why most people are. Yes, it may not bother some but that is just crazy. It is not a cheap place to go, especially per person.

On a plus note, I did enjoy seeing all the lights and decorations that were out for Christmas. They were beautiful.

Busch gardens coasters are always a 10/10. However, they really need to put a SLIVER of their budget back into events. Howl'o'scream this year AND Christmas town this year were so lacking. Do NOT get the sampler at Christmas town. The food was inedible, the drinks were batched, and it was 60 bucks. Please please BG put money back into entertainment!!!

Love the animals too. Always. Can't beat it.
Poor operations. Only 1 cart on Gwazi and we spent money on fast passes just to wait 45 minutes. So much potential to be a great theme park

Complete rip-off/scam. I truly believe the team has this set-up in a way to charge you as much as possible with no remorse for their poor management. Much of what I'm writing has been echoed in numerous reviews, which I should have listened too before I went.

My family and I spent over $600 on three single day tickets with FastPass Plus add-ons, expecting to experience the park's attractions at their best. Unfortunately, our visit was marred by several issues that significantly impacted our experience.

Firstly, nearly half of the rides were closed for "annual maintenance," which was not disclosed anywhere on their websites or app. We confirmed this with a customer service representative at the park, who stated that the website and app were not updated to reflect the closures. She mentioned that the marketing team was working on having this issue resolved in the future. This lack of communication left us feeling misled and disappointed, especially after paying for tickets and add-ons with the expectation of enjoying all available rides. The app showed everything was operational, what a scam in itself.

Secondly, we encountered problems with the FastPass system. The FastPass lanes were not properly monitored, allowing guests to bypass the system by lane-hopping without any verification of their FastPass access. We only observed one ride (Rising Phoenix) where someone was actually monitoring the FastPass line and scanning passes. On our first attempt to ride, the ride was closed for about 45 minutes after waiting in line for some time. When we returned for a second attempt, our passes were scanned again, but no other rides had their lanes monitored. Additionally, we noticed that some rides had their FastPass lanes roped off in the middle of the day for no apparent reason, while guests continued to hop into the FastPass lane from the regular line.

This combination of ride closures, lack of FastPass monitoring, and inconsistent lane management was extremely frustrating and disappointing. I expected a much higher level of service and coordination, especially with how much money we spent.

It was disheartening to spend a significant amount of money and time and not receive the experience we anticipated. We live outside of the U.S., had we known that there were going to be unannounced closures, and disregard for add-on purchases to "enhance" our experience, we would not have spent the time and money into this location the way we did.

Also good luck trying to get ahold of anyone, they never responded to my e-mail when I informed them of the issue. I had to call after waiting 10 days for a response, in which they basically said there's nothing they can do about how the park manages their staff and advising people of what's open and when. They were also aware that they do not advertise what is closed and when. (Probably on purpose).

Here's some advice:
- Buy the tickets at the park if you decide to go, and call ahead even before you do that to see what's open.
- Don't bother buying fast-pass lanes as no one will be there to monitor it anyways, it'll save you almost 200 bucks per person and you can just skip the line.
- If someone is scanning, don't worry, they probably will only be at one. (if they scan your pass once, you can't get a refund, which is why we couldn't get a refund because they scanned our pass once at the only ride that was being manned and said "well your pass was scanned so we can't issue a refund even if no one else was monitoring the other lanes lanes".
- Bring your own food, most of the stands won't be open anyways and its extremely over priced.

This is one of my favorite theme parks for a couple of reasons. Although it caters more to the rollercoaster enthusiasts, it has things for the little ones and the helpful kiddie wranglers (grandparents) while the rest of the gang is in line or riding a coaster.

Iron Gawzi is my absolute fave. What's not to love?!It's fast. It's high, and that first drop is a doozy. Definitely gets the blood pumping.

Sheikira comes a close second for similar reasons.

Tried to experience the Sarangeti Flyer, but it got shut down when we were one group away from us getting on. It got closed for inclement weather, which is unpredictable for how long the storm will take to pass. So we left our spot in line for something else.

This time around, Tigris was down multiple times, and we weren't able to ride it. The woman working the line there said it's been going down 4x/day.

There is a Sesame Street Land with kiddie rides for the little ones. Kid friendly foods. They even have a Chik Fil A in the park.

For the grandparents who don't want to ride the rollercoasters, there are nice shows they can partake for entertainment such as a cirque show, ice skating show, and live band performances. Also, it is a plus to get out of the hot Florida summer sun for some rest and cooling off.

Tips: Get to the park early. The parking opens 30 mins before the park opens.

Some rides don't have someone standing on the side you board. So pay attention and split up into the different lines.

If you're staying all day, get the all-day dining. You're not supposed to share it.. but.. let's just say that it easily feeds 2 people per band as you can grab a meal every 90 mins. Keep a timer on your phone so you know when you can get another meal. The 90 min timer also helps remind you to take a break and get out of the sun for a bit of rest btwn rides.

Check the weather! All the rides get shut down when inclement weather is in the area. It's hard to tell how long the rides will be down for.

The fireworks show is spectacular check for which nights they have it and go on that day.

Go make fun memories with your loved ones!
Too much walking too expensive not enough variety of attractions fun if you like roller coasters definitely recommend good location

Let me start out by stating how disappointed I am with how this park is being run. My son was there 3/9/25. Along with most of the rides being out of order, he lost his phone on a ride.

He filled out a slip, we lock his phone and set it to lost mode.
The next day Apple sends a notice the phone has been found. We get the notice because someone has touched the screen and/or tried to open it.
I went the next day & they said no one turned it in. Today 3/21 I get a call that they found his license that was INSIDE his phone case.
I'm so disappointed with the honesty of this park, how run down it's looking & the rides out of service.
I caution anyone to make sure you don't lose your belongings because the un honest people that "sweep" the areas for lost items are clearly finding them & taking them for their use.

Busch Gardens is still one of my favorite amusement parks. Unfortunately, they no longer have the abundance of animals I was used to seeing. On this trip for my birthday, (2/2/22), I enjoyed getting away and perusing Tampa and Miami to see what;s changing in my home state.

Florida is always growing and building and so was Busch Gardens to my amazement. Though many of the rides were closed (seasonally), I was able to see the new hybrid wooden/steel roller coaster, the Iron Gwazi. It's currently being tested out with park employees and opening in March 2022.

There is currently a new theme show called: "Busch Gardens, Mardi Gras" and there's was a package you can buy to sample a variety of "New Orleans" Cajun and Creole foods/beverages and beignets.

So, for my personal adventure, The Congo was the only ride I was brave enough to venture. Then there was Falcon's Fury, the only other ride besides The Congo my daughter got on (with her 25 year old scared self) . The entire time, she kept calling Jesus. I was like, "Call Him baby, He'll save you!" I was scared for her tho, that thing went too far in the air for me.

As for the rides that were closed for the season: the Montu, Sheirka, Stanley Falls, Skyride. They all looked awesome but probably only runs when there's an exuberant amount of guests. All other rides were open.

I loved being here this time of year because we didn't have long lines to wait in. We did a lot of walking, which was great but eventually got on the Serengeti train. This train takes you around the park where you can see the animals out in the open. It was pretty cool seeing those majestic creatures; however, I missed the flamingos and just couldn't find them.

I was; however, able to see the penguins and they made me smile with their happy feet self lol. While I was filming them in the water, one swam up to me and just stayed there paddling his lil feet staring at me. I'm like, oooooookay happy feet, time for me to go!

So, parking is $30 unless you're an annual pass member at a certain level and you park for free. Pass members are afforded some great perks and benefits and it's not very expensive. For example, you can get up to 6 free guest passes, unlimited admission, 20%-40% off merchandise, 10%-20% of food/beverages, free admission to year round events and so on.

Anyway, I'd like to see more exotic animals out there and more perks for non annual pass visitors....oh and I don't think they offer free beer. I know that was many moons ago.

We do have a platinum annual pass, that we all truly enjoy. Was incredibly hot during our visit but as you see it didn't stop everyone from creating amazing memories, there were lots of ways to beat the heat from the splash pad area, the many sitting areas with large fans, indoor AC areas, and of the kids favorite the water rides. The only thing that I would suggest is to add fans or misters of some sort to the train ride because it does get quite hot on that ride.

I grew up at this park! as a Tampa Bay Area resident my whole life! If this park lasts another 10 years I will be amazed! They have removed many animals, the food is god awful and Way over priced, the water rides were both closed and many of the coasters were closed, there was only one show and the employees are obviously not getting paid well! The landscaping has been let go as well! I am so heartbroken and disgusted by the shape of the park! Since Busch sold the park it has gone to hell in a handbasket! They are not keeping the park up and it's so sad! I used to be season pass holders and have t been in two years because I saw the decline back then. We will not be going back! Unless someone buys it and recovers it I don't see it open for another decade!

This theme park is a really cool combination of thrilling rides & animal experiences, something you cannot often find. It makes a great fit for our family with the littles enjoying the animals and train through the African grasslands with the parents enjoying a ride or two throughout our time. And the Sesame Street land is a big hit with our 4 year old, who loves the rides and the massive treehouse. I'm impressed by the variety and intensity of the thrill rides too! The keepers are excellent, always happy to teach about the animals at the keeper talks. And the cheetah run is can't miss, so cool to see the animal enrichment in front of you. It's a great experience and we love only having to drive 30-45 minutes for theme park fun!

Busch gardens was fun. Montanu was really fast. They had yum yum chick fil a! The bathrooms were sketchy and dirty, I said there was tape on the toilet. The aviary's were fun. The birds were cute. One ran into me it was funny! One landed on my should. I had fun and took a picture bunch.
I love the animals there and all the rides !! Definitely a great place to have fun at or bring your family too!

I have been an annual pass holder least 6 years and have mostly enjoyed going to Busch Gardens. I don't ride many of the rides, unless we bring family along, but I love the animal aspect. The events such as Mardi Gras, Food and Wine and Christmas town are always fun events.

I understand that COVID has had a major impact on many businesses, and Busch Gardens has suffered also, but for the price you pay, it just isn't worth it anymore. Many of the rides are still closed, and in the afternoons the animals are often inside. I usually go early just so I can see the animals.

I went for the October annual pass holders perk of animal encounters and was extremely disappointed. Three animals...a turtle, an armadillo and a porcupine. None of these are ones which you can have an encounter. Two of the three handlers were very informative, but the last just stood there. I had expected so much more.

They have removed almost all of the hand sanitizing stations which were located everywhere throughout the Park. The last I heard, COVID is still a problem in our world and people need to take precautions so it doesn't keep spreading.

We are opting for Zoo tickets next year and likely won't be returning to Busch Gardens.
Amazing. We were here for a conference and had half the place to our group. Amazing experience. Great staff and overall highly recommend.

I will start by saying simply, don't go here.
