The park food was terrible. The layout of the park was confusing and disorienting. The staff enforced zero rules. We watched people walk around the park and get on rides barefoot. The staff were unable to count and incorrectly put the right number of people on rides causing others to have to return to the line. The park was generally dirty and in disrepair. Rides would "break down" after we had been in line 30 minutes for them. The animals looked sickly and sad.

We decided on taking my son to Busch gardens for his birthday suprise and it definitely paid off!
Of course the lines were long everybody had the day off plus I'm sure just travelers coming to visit so there were a couple rides we missed out on but the rides we did go on we had a blast the kids were passed out as soon as we got back to the car.
The bathrooms I were shocked about because they were just filthy. The one right next to the food court and so was the was in sesame place. The shows were cute we watched Elmo and friends and saw a cool African porcupine taking a stroll.
We stayed until night time to see and enjoy the lights and got to see some beautiful princesses.
--Another issue I had is that there was a 5% surcharge on everything we bought!?! No one could explain what the charge was for and after the third time we were upset because we didn't know why it on every purchase.? Anyways it was still a good time overall.

I am a Gold Pass holder and have past employee of this park. I spent $300 purchasing tickets for others family members. We visited Busch Gardens on 2/15/25 and every water ride was closed. Only 2 roller coaster were running and lines were over an hour long. Most of the concession stands were closed. Those that were open had long lines. We were celebrating my granddaughter's birthday and spent more time standing in line than riding any rides. We went to kids area only to find the splash pads were closed as well. I get dozens of text and emails from Busch Gardens daily and they never mentioned any rides or closures! I am requesting a refund of the $300 I spent on additional tickets! This was a complete disaster! My granddaughter who goes to school in Gainesville and my daughter who drove up from Palm Beach Gardens spent hours driving to meet us at Busch Gardens! What a total waste of money! I will be contacting the Better Business Bureau as well as this was a complete ripoff!

Do not buy the all day meal pass. Lines for food at every outlet are over an hour long. As ridiculously overpriced as the food is, you'll be lucky to get one meal on your pass unless you're willing to spend hours in line for a bad cheeseburger.

It was a beautiful day to visit Mardi Gras at Busch Gardens here in Tampa on Monday January 23, 2023. Not overly crowded at all but everyone that was there was having fun.
The reason for the 3* is because it was not mentioned at the gate, when we bought tickets, on the app that The Mardi Gras celebration is only Friday-Saturday.
It was mentioned we found out on the actual website. No where else.
The only mention of days for the event we found until after we were in the park. Everything related to that event was closed. So our fault for missing that one line on the website but no where else. But...
Then there was a big detour that literally cut through the park so all you did was backtrack.
Needless to say we were disappointed

The weather was very nice so we walked around then left to get supper at Bella's. Yum.

I visited Busch Gardens in January and February during the Mardi Gras Celebration. The overall experience was okay. The rollercoasters are awesome and if you are a roller coaster you will love it! Favorite coasters: Iron Gwazi, Cheetah Hunt, and Sheikra I love the roller coasters and the sky ride!

The food selections are poor and the quality of the food is not good. My first visit I got the bbq sampler to split with my partner. The fries were cold which was a bummer! The bbq items like the rib had a lot of fat on it. For the price charged I was disappointed with the meal.

Mardi Gras is going on at Busch Gardens and I was excited to try the rum Bundt cake. When I arrived to xcursions there was an employee who found cupcakes that were two days old in the fridge. He was telling the other staff that they did not check to make sure old items were removed. Hence, I did not purchase a bundt cake due to not knowing if it was good or if it was fresh.

Overall, the park needs improvements. The park is dated and needs an facelift for sure. Some things just look old. Customer service is lacking and many employees do not smile or seem happy. Parking price is a bit excessive at 30 dollars. That is more expensive than Disney parking!

If you are local or a roller coasters lover I would check it out Busch Gardens otherwise I would skip the park.

I HATE this "busch gardens" place. First of all, my daughter was on the Sesame Street playground having fun for about 1 hour, and this lady approached her and said "Hey kid! Can I check your height?" So she said yes, and when the lady checked her height, she was a CENTIMETER over the height limit. They DID NOT LET HER ON, AND THERE WAS A GROWN WOMAN in the play AREA WITH HER SON ON THE PLAY PLACE! MY DAUGHTER IS TEN YEARS OLD, AND the woman was WAY TALLER THAN HER! they let WOMAN ON BUT NOT MY TEN-YEAR-OLD DAUGHTER?! so ok, we went over to one of the other rides there. I saw a grown woman and her son on the ride, so I went over there so she could ride the ride
and they said she was too tall for it! there was a six foot man on the ride and my four foot daughter was TOO TALL??!?! Don't go to this place, it's bad.

Was excited to visit since I haven't been there in years...very disappointed. Half the rides weren't running, seemed a bit grown with weeds, and a lit of things not there anymore. The mist disappointing thing was the appearance of the one orangutan. He looked as if he had dreadlocks and hadn't been taken care of in forever. His hair was matted and disgusting. Very sad. This is not the park I remember from the past. Another disappointment is paying for a safari ride after paying to get into the park. You pay to get in, it should be included. Bad enough the food and drink cost ridiculous amounts.

Went here for the first time ever this past Sunday. Was meeting some old friends to do "howloscream" so our tickets were for 5pm to midnight.
This is place could not be more different from Disney. The park is located in an undesirable area of Tampa, mostly consisting of chain restaurants, pawn shops and vape/smoke shops.
The guy at the gate where we paid for parking was without a doubt on something or at the very least, high. I hesitated giving him my credit card.

The patrons visiting the park were mainly goth-hoes, drunks and regular hoes, who might possibly be drunk.
EVERYONE is vaping weed or smoking cigarettes so the stench of weed is EVERYWHERE. And like hey, I get being 21 and wanting to get high but, come on guys, can't you just hotbox in your car and then come in to the park.

Oh yeah, and there was a dirty diaper on the floor of the bathroom stall I used. Awesome!
So like, there are some awesome thrill rides here, buuuut, the place is a shithole.

Some of the employees are nice though. Met some friendly people.
And I also caught a cold.

Would I go back? Depends on how badly I want to ride a roller coaster.
Based on the experience though, I'd likely just go to universal instead.
Everything in the park was overpriced on par if not more expensive than Disney except the quality was way inferior. The food is all pre made sitting under heat lamps. 12$ for a bottle of beer. When you consider how rundown the park feels, $12 feels insane.

The rides we were able to ride were quite fun and the employees that actually did their jobs were pretty cool.

Ultimately this place just felt a like a cash grab. Between the disproportionate amount of "gift shops" versus places to find food and then the oddly placed carnival games everywhere, it felt very third rate, and not to sound "uppity" but it was just kindof gross.

This review is mainly for the pass member landing and a bit of the park, the park is good. Safe rides and the whole 9. HOWEVER, they don't respect quick queue, they let the people in the normal queue go first way before the quick queue. For example, jace on cheetah hunt said one second to my group of 3 in quick queue then let I believe 32 people in the normal line go before our group of 3, maybe I'm over exaggerating how many people they let go before us but I know it was tons of people. Besides that the park is amazing good rides and it's very safe. Now the worst part is the pass members lounge, that's the main reason why I put 2 stars. The place is terrible I'm pretty sure half of the people in there wasn't even pass holders. Now if you don't know the other lounge was closed (it was in the Serengeti outlook) which it made no sense to shut that lounge down as it was nice and comfortable. They also checked if you were a pass holder in the Lounge, but nope in the new lounge it's cramped, nobody checks anyone if their a pass holder and you have to drink water out of a fountain that is warm. Now don't get me wrong I'm fine with drinking water o it of a fountain but atleast make it cold.. (they also had ice water, lemon water, lime water, and lemonade in the old lounge) this new lounge just seems like a lounge for everyone in the park. You could fix it up by adding chairs to the tables, making the floor carpet, and add a kitchen since in this new lounge you can't order food like you could in the old one. Do better Busch gardens. EDIT: this is crazy, the old lounge closed when the park closed which now they close at 5. They also don't have a bathroom smh.. DO BETTER!!!

I don't even know where to start... this was my family's first and last time coming to this amusement park. We had a horrible experience from beginning to end. It was hilarious to see that the parking lot and park itself had just one entrance. With an expected ETA of 9:30, we finally made it into the park at 11:30. Yes that's right, 11:30. It took us an extra 58 minutes to get into the parking lot and a whole hour to go through the one security line.

We collectively paid $700 to ride ONE ride, Shaikra. 3 out of what, 10 of the big rides were closed for maintenance or technical difficulties. Other rides held wait times of up to 4-5 hours! Yes you can get a fast pass for an additional $190 PER PERSON! I wouldn't even pay that in Disney world. But I definitely wish we drove the extra hour from my place to Disney world for $20 more!

Busch gardens Tampa is loosing a lot of future customers! I'll be sure to let anyone coming to this area know that it is absolutely not worth your time or money. I hate that we had to spend $700+ to figure out this is the worst amusement park in America, but ya live and ya learn.

This place is okay. No family owned theme park or Disney world, that's for sure. We were there all day and got on maybe 3 rides and saw a few animals? Waited for the Cobra coaster for about 2-3ish hours? Didn't get on any other coasters that day. Saw some cute animals tho

We picked Tuesday to go since we heard it's the least busiest day.

We purchased tickets in advance for three. We had some discounts but still we paid little less than $350 for 3 including $25 parking charge.
We spent about $55 for lunch. It was a lunch for 3. If you have hungry family, maybe paying $30 pass for each person might be a better option.

We got in line before 10am and we walked into the park little after 10am. Our first ride we got on was a bumper car ride. So many rides were temporarily closed. It was little disappointing. By late afternoon some of the temporarily closed rides opened up but some never did. At first we felt like we wanted a refund but because it wasn't so busy, kids were able to go on the favorite rides multiple times.

We stayed at the park until 5pm. It was a perfect fun day for 7 year old.

Solo traveler here! Found a Groupon deal for admission plus all day dining. Can't get much better than that!

On a Thursday, I arrived early to the park (prepaid $25 for parking) and they let us in right around 0950. I came with an agenda and I was able to ride all the big rides (except Kumba which was down for maintenance) in 2 1/2 hours!! I even rode a few of them twice!

Everyone had to wear a mask and I definitely heard staff reminding people to wear them properly more than once. Hand sanitizer is also everywhere! Social distancing was enforced as they left seats open between parties. A few rides I got to ride all by myself literally. The ride with the longest wait was Cheetah hunt, but it was TOTALLY worth it!! Every other ride was pretty much a walk-on earlier in the day. Never had to wait more than about 5 minutes.

The animals are beautiful. The park is beautiful. I had a REALLY fun time and I'll for sure be back. Can't wait to conquer Iron Gwazi!

1/3 of the park closed without any notification of the closure. Very disappointing that all the ride waits were 90 plus minutes on a Monday. They still allowed the same amount of people. With this Busch garden greed, we were only able to ride two rides. Also, we went to Universal Studios highly recommend that..they limit tickets, so customers can ride all rides. Do better Busch!

Wow! We had a such a great time here and I haven't been to an amusement park in about 10 years, and it was the same park!

So thrilling to be a part of the roller coaster action again. Except, I did get a bit frightened when we rode the Scorpion coaster. Specifically, it was after the loop and towards the end of the ride when you go on your side in a funnel-like movement. I was terrified for my life at this point and had to take a minute to calm myself back down to reality after the ride ended.

We also walked through the bird sanctuary and saw flamingos on our way to it in their own outdoor habitat. The birds flew00 freely in the sanctuary and you can feed them if your heart desires as they fly on you.

Ended up meeting with our friend Paige who works with the elephants in the park and she gave us a detailed description of their work with the elephants and how they develop relationships with them throughout the years. So interesting and appreciative of what they do day-to-day to take care of the elephants and their habitat.

Highly recommend stepping into the park if you're craving a thrill for the day in Tampa!

Worst theme park management of all-time.
DO NOT BUY TICKETS THROUGH THE APP!! It's a trap and you will have to go to the ticket office regardless. Tickets on the app are not tickets, it is the receipt for your purchase and does nothing at all.
DO NOT BUY A MEAL PLAN! You cannot activate it at most places that accept it. Management thinks it's a fun game to make it a scavenger hunt type scenario where you run around the park trying to find a vendor where you can activate it and get what you paid for.
Do you like merchandise? Hopefully you are at least 300 lbs because those are the only sizes anywhere. They are using an automated system to restock, somehow they can't figure it out in 2025 because finding a medium is like finding a white whale.
Operations are so slow. I like that they let you see the dispatch screen so everyone on the ride can see it tick past 200 seconds every dispatch.
The food is somewhere between Purina and what you get at the food bank. The pretzel was actually good. Everything else was disgusting. Do you want to dip your fries in ketchup? Better dump ketchup on your tray pig because no containers for you. $25 a meal, but a $0.02 paper cup to put condiments in will destroy the bottom line.
SeaWorld takes all their money and buys their own stock to pump the price. You are not a guest. Not a customer. You are a little pay pig here. And you are treated like it.

Iron Gwazi is good though. Really good. Easily a top 10 coaster in the world. Maybe the thing to do is to get a season pass and never spend a single penny elsewhere.

OK for starters this amusement park is more like a Safari park I did not expect so many animals and habitats within the roller coasters and excitement Tampa Bay Busch Gardens is conveniently located off the main road and is near adventure island there sister water park which I will have to visit next year I went the day after Halloween and was able to see a lot of the decorations they had still up I road all of the coasters And got to see all the animals my favorite was the bird Aviary and the kangaroos the tiger showed out for me as well And I even got to take a selfie with the lion going to this amusement park it's like going to the zoo at the same time I was actually impressed at the animals seemed very well taken care of and healthy they also offer Safari trips where you can get a closer view of the animals and go on a jeep ride I was impressed with the roller coasters and the variety they were very unique to this park I did not see many staff however I noticed a lot of the stores were closed it is probably their off season
I did not complain because the park had less people there was hardly any wait for the rides

My wife and I love going to Busch Gardens for the zoo features of the park. The keepers here do a great job of taking care of the animals and the habitat is as nice or nicer than many zoos.

However, we remain concerned about the pandemic. The park limits entry via a registration process and the number of visitors is definitely limited.

The park posts notices throughout regarding the requirement to wear a mask. However, we have noticed many instances where a visitor is not wearing a mask. However there are not a sufficient number of park employees present throughout the park to enforce the rule.

In one instance, two people not wearing a mask walked right by a park security person and said hello to the employee and he said nothing to the visitors! This is ridiculous!
Very Good Park I recommend front row iron gwazi falcons fury is always closed even thoug the park is very nice with animals to see.

arazmin at parking was amazing
so kind and informative
def need more people like her!
had a great time for my bbi's 13th burrfffdayyyy

I feel terrible updating my review to a 1 star from a 5 star, but I feel like I have to based on our experience on Saturday. There is definitely a safer way to operate during Covid and nearby theme parks are doing it right. (Rating drop was due to Covid safety precautions, park cleanliness, ride wait times, food line wait times, etc)

We made reservations well in advance and purchased passes for Saturday, 11/28/2020. We are aware that Covid is out there, but have felt safe during our Disney World visits and assumed that Busch Gardens would be taking their safety measures on their website seriously. We were wrong and disappointed. The park was packed. It was the second busiest we've seen in 20 years of being passholders. We saw probably 25%+ of people without masks and no staff was asking people to wear them.

Due to the park being so crowded, the rides that were open had 65 min wait to 140 min waits. (A lot of rides were closed while we were there, so call ahead).

All in all, we won't be returning anytime soon. The risk for us isn't worth it.

The rollercoasters were great. They were thrilling and pretty varied as to what type of ride they were.

However, the staff at the park were incredibly rude and had no sense of urgency. We ate lunch at the Nairobi BBQ place. We watched one of the employees behind the counter playing with her hair with her gloves on then touch the food with the same gloves on. The other staff members were barking at customers asking what they wanted to eat or acted as if the customer was an inconvenience to them.

The ride loading is incredibly slow and drawn out. We were in the heat of June with 90+ temperatures and many of the queue lines had poor ventilation and areas of full sun. This was absolutely brutal waiting for rides that at any other theme park would have had a significantly shorter wait time. Multiple rides were running only one car which made wait times multiply.

The food is below average theme park food and fortunately, with the all day dining, I didn't have to pay full price because it was incredibly expensive for what you get.

The bathrooms were dirty, there are only a few water filling bottle stations around the park.

I will skip this park and pay more to go to Disney or Universal in the future.

awful experience, the lines where out of control, the park is overcrowded and not managed well and you can only ride the rides if you pay the extra 200$ for the fast pass. The lines for food took 30 minutes and the park is overpriced and crowded.

Im an out of town visitor, hadn't been to Busch Gardens since I was a child. We visited yesterday afternoon, if you're planning on going, I wouldn't waste my money. We got in for free because friends of ours are pass holders. The Christmas lights were pretty and the shows weren't bad but half the rides were closed down and the park staff all seemed to be wishing they were some where else (no friendly,warm faces in this park) We stopped by the Dragon Fire Grill dining hall for a bite to eat and see the three kings show. Needless to say over priced food that was cold. No service food trays in sight to carry your food to your table unless you wanted to take one from the pile on the dirty trash cans and the food teller couldn't be bothered to check out people in line because he was putting stickers on the drink cups. We also visited a coffee shop within the park that had trash all over the floor and an over flowing trash receptacle with a lid on the floor. The establishment wasn't that busy and plenty of workers to handle the mess to make the shop look presentable. The grounds of the park look run down and dirty, the bathrooms are DISGUSTING (I'm talking fecal matter on the walls) Nothing like Disneyland where everything always seems to be pristine. I'm glad we didn't have to pay to get in, what a disappointment.

This trip was a huge disappointment. As pass members, we love this park but with the current restrictions we really haven't ventured anywhere. Decided to try the park with reservations, hoping for a relaxed family experience. Boy, were we shocked to see the crowds. There was TOO MANY people and very few rides, shows, or stores operational. Not even the tram takes you to the entrance. Your dropped off down the road to begin the day of walking to nowhere.

The crowds were too heavy with some areas bottlenecking. Yes, employees are walking around with signs that say social distancing, but we had to remind folks to back up. The masks are on the whole time, which is okay if everyone kept their mask on, which they don't. We did see security telling folks to put their mask on and over their nose, as we walked among the crowds.

But the major blow was that all the shows were closed up, plenty of the rides were too. So what ended up happening in the rides that were open, you were looking at 90+ minutes wait. There was nowhere to go, with this many people and so many activities and shows not available. Plus, I hated not having a map, none are being given to guests, to help guide us along and better inform us of what was available. Just to spend the day walking around, to find that rides are closed or have extremely long crowded waits is not acceptable.

Paid for preferred parking for our family member's vehicle and when we got there they inform us that the lot was full. Then we had to stand in the customer service line for over an hour to be given a refund with Busch money. It takes them 30 to 60 days to issue you a credit card refund. That's awful, especially when it only took them a second to charge it!

Yes, seeing the one elephant was cute. Taking a one-way train ride after a 40 minute wait to see the other animals running free was cool too. But walking by and not spotting any of the lions, cheetahs, or monkeys was disappointing. Even the penguin tank had maybe 10 in there.

Busch could do better and they had in the past. This was a mistake and glad we had passes, for we have hope it can only get better. Feel sorry for all those families that paid the extreme one day ticket per person to have nothing at the end of the day, except perhaps a picture with a mask on by a lighted tree.

Have always loved Busch Gardens- the rides and the animal exhibits are amazing! If only rating on those items, would easily give 5 stars. However, we chose the Oasis Pizza restaurant for lunch. Lined up almost out the door at 130 in the afternoon. Only one cashier working and lines didn't budge for almost half an hour. Additional cashier eventually came out and was rocking his line. We made the mistake of committing to the OG line. Waited 50 minutes to place an order. The cashier had no idea what she was doing, seemed disinterested and had continual register issues. She also seemed more interested in keeping her hoodie hanging off her shoulder than keeping the line moving. Had we known, we may have tried a different eatery at the park. This was not a great experience and caused us to waste almost an hour of our day. Additionally, BG charges for lockers to be used throughout the day so that you can enjoy the attractions. For as much as the admission ticket costs, the lockers should be free. Most Orlando amusement parks share lockers as a courtesy, free of charge. Time to adjust to market norms on that one....

Long lines, wild amount of waiting time. I think the Cobra ride had like a 60 minute queue. The food for the price is a rip off. All day dining pass kind of seems like trash, there is very limited dining areas you can use it for. The Asian Wok at Busch Garden is literally trash. The fried rice tastes dry, almost like it's half cooked. Like the rice is not fully cooked. The beef and broccoli is terrible, the soup from the thing is good but the portions are terrible. You either get all meat or a mix. Depends on who is serving you. The brownie is a good size, desserts here are good and very sweet. Even with fast pass, some lines don't move at all. Now Asian Wok here at Busch Garden literally looks like New York catering services, or any international flights to Asia from any U.S. airport with sh*t catering. Really the all day dining definitely covers the price because a meal alone for a family of 3-4 can be $100-200 .
Been here for over 3 hours, ride keeps shutting down, could have been at home on the game or something.

Hadn't been here in years, it definitely isn't what it used to be. Looks run down and outdated.

I'm giving 2 stars because the staff was nice & pleasant.

Also, be prepared to pay $30 for parking even if you're a a seaworld and Aquatica pass holder.

The good is the layout, access to shade and the integration of the zoo and the amusement park. The bad....well...we visited the last week in April with my son. We purchased 3 day passes and 2 quick que passes at $125 a pop. We get there and 3 rollercoasters are not running. One of them did get up and running the other 2 did not. All of the quick que lanes were closed so we had to wait in line with everyone else. So there is $250 down the drain. We then purchased 3 of the unlimited souvenir cups which could be used for sodas and ICEEs. We were able to get ICEEs at one location. We went to another location where both machines were not working except for lemonade flavored. We find another one which the machines we were working however we were told that we couldn't use them because the drip trays were not in place. Now note the girl telling us this is literally holding the trays in her hands. It was 337pm and the park closed at 6, so why are we're closing the stand 1.5 hours early?? Overall we were very unhappy with the fact that they basically stole money from us by taking payment for the quick que and not having it opened, and no one was taking any initiative to make sure the refreshment stands were functioning. Poor management. You can do better

First time going but was accompanied by people that had been there before in the past. For a well known theme park it seemed underwhelming. Almost like we will make money off of past popularity but have given up. My expectations were high thinking I would feel like I was in a true safari park with stunning views, plants and animals. No everything was kind of sad. For the money I just think you have to do better.. that being said please go because it's iconic and your kids will appreciate it. I'm just saying they could do a lot better.

Most of the animals were not out, No Lions, Orangoutangs, or Tigers. Only 1 Cheetah, 1 Hyena and 1 Gorilla. Two major roller coasters were down. This was one of the worst visits so far. I was excited to go since it's been a few years to see the animals. I was Just disappointed.

We decided to go to Busch Gardens for my cousin ms 14th birthday. Our tickets were around $90, but with taxes and fees each ticket was about $115. We arrived close to the opening time, around 10:15 am. You have to pay for parking but since we were at a walking distance from the park, we walked to save up the parking fee.

My family and I went on a Friday morning, and surprisingly, there weren't many lines in any of the rides. We firstly started with the rollercoaster Cheetah Hunt, the wait was only five minutes! We then went to Montu and Cobra's Curse, other rollercoasters that also had no wait. By 11 AM, we had already got into three rides.

After this, my family wanted to ride Iron Gwazi, but I was a bit dizzy from the other rides and a bit scared to be honest. So while they rode that rollercoaster (which the wait said was around 45 mins but ended up being less than 30 mins), my sister and I decided to go to the Sesame's Street area. They had a lot of cute merchandise and it is ideal if you have little kids.

We then went to SheiKra, which honestly, was my favorite rollercoaster. It was really tall, fast, and a bit scary, but the ride was smooth and you were able to see all of Tampa from the top. After SheiKra, my family rode Tigris.

We then went to Chik fil A for lunch, which was good but a bit overpriced for what we normally get outside of the park. After eating lunch, we were too full to ride rollercoasters, so we decided to go on a water ride, Congo River Rapids. Be careful, you get REALLY wet!! so prepare!

After this, we rode Sandserpent, rode Cheetah Hunt again, and our day was done!

We were able to take a lot of advantage of the park and we were able to ride every roller coaster we wanted to. We were also able to see several animals throughout the trip, such as alligators, elephants, and penguins!!

Some tips to keep in mind: if possible, go on a weekday, we went on a Friday so the park was fairly empty compared to other times. Most rides had a wait time of 5 minutes, and the most we waited for a ride was 30 minutes. Another tip I would give is to buy the refillable souvenir cup to get your drinks. Even though it was a bit pricey ($20) compared to buying a singular drink, it's worth it. Finally, a crucial tip I would give to anyone bringing personal items to the park: but a belt bag or a bag that you could take on roller coasters if possible. We had to pay for lockers for several of the rides or have someone not ride so they could keep our items.

Overall, Busch Gardens was a really fun, exciting trip and I will come back again!!

While on vacation we decided to check out Busch Gardens Tampa and the grandkids enjoyed themselves. We went during the week and the lines and wait times were not outrageous. We purchased the express pass for the grandkids so they did not have to wait at all. It was a nice theme park with good family fun. The express pass was a bit expensive but well worth not having to wait forever in a line. Luckily the kids were able to use the express pass on all rides. We enjoyed it.

Busch Gardens during normal times has a bad tendency to be a bit of a mess with poorly dispatched rides, non existent service at F&B locations and a park that requires you to go way out of your way to get anywhere. Unfortunately during Covid times they have taken it down a notch with no enforcement at all of their own rules.

Tons of guests with no masks and no one wearing them properly and we never heard anyone working enforcing it. No social distancing of any kind in the ride or food lines and employees not even trying to encourage guests to do it even though they had markers on the ground. It's like Covid doesn't exist at Busch Gardens - and that's not a good thing.

The rides were Amazing! Animals were a huge bonus! The staff we encountered were wonderful, the food was way too expensive-but good. The price of parking I found OUTRAGOUS!! The main problems we had were with the drink system, MANY others had the same issue and that the park was DIRTY. The manager refunded my drink after the issue and later charged it BACK!! I had not been to Busch Gardens in years and wanted to take my kids-we won't return-it's not worth the money and it has gone downhill. It seems impossible to contact customer service, which is not surprising. I'm done getting transferred around. I'll finish my vacation in the Tampa area and deal with it later.

Went with the family for Howl 'o Scream 2022. Was better than I expected. Lots of people, lots of families, everybody having fun. They had a ton of scare-you workers hiding in every dark nook and cranny. The haunted houses were great and the wait times were often less than displayed. Parking was a breeze to get in and to get out.

The experience was definitely worth the price of admission.

This park is GREEDY You need to pay everything even you have purchased the $100 admission. Parking fee is even more expensive than Disney World. A half of its facilities were closed.
ZERO star!!!

Florida resident with annual passes to Universal and Sea World. We were in Tampa with friends and decided to visit. Awful experience from beginning to end. Long lines for everything with employees moving at glacial uncaring pace. Overpriced food with massive lines. Impossible to get on rides without buying the fast pass for the kids at least. Christmas Town was a total joke of an attraction. Nasty disgusting bathrooms. Place just sucks your money with zero in return. Literally never going back and recommend a day visit to any other attraction in Tampa other than this one.
Best amusement park ever love it 5 stars rides are fun love food awesome can't wait to go back
Ride wait times are way off. Don't trust the app. Food stands are constantly out of things. This is a bad park experience. Save up for Disney.

The park itself is fine. The workers refuse to allow you to put your belongings on the side of the rides. Even tho they have designated tables/lockers/crates marked specifically for this purpose with signage saying the park is not responsible for lost or stolen items. Very frustrating.

If you decide to visit this park be sure to carry the bare minimum and don't take anything you don't care to get lost or wet. They do have lockers you can rent. However, the park is large and I'm not paying to rent a locker at every single ride, which is what this park is expecting.
Great experience in Christmas. Good environment happy . Happy Holidays. December style. Good for friends. Entrenamiento para niños.

platinum membership is worthless the parking the staff is horrible and rude
lines are not reasonable

they let too many people in
customer service sucks
colossal waste of time and money

Last weekend we had the opportunity to visit @howloscream at @buschgardens and we had a blast! The haunted houses were very well produced and can easily compete to those from @horrornightsorl

The only downside was that it felt more crowded and lines were longer as there is only 5 houses vs. 10 that @horrornightsorl offers. So a fast pass is highly recommended if you plan on visiting all the houses. Also, there were less food options outside of the traditional food venues which had very long lines as well.

Here is a TIP if you wish to save some time in food lines: stop by the the Giraffe Bar in the Serengeti Plain area which is on the 2nd floor. Drinks and food were pretty good there and lines were much shorter

Overall, @howloscream was super fun! Houses and rides were awesome. We would say rides are probably even better than @universalorlando if you like thrill roller-coasters. Food options are a bit more limited tho and lines are long, specially as we get closer to Halloween...so keep those things in mind before going Visit @orlandotravelfoodies for more reviews and tips of local attractions and restaurants!!!

Was on a trip in Florida but didn't want to deal with Disney and since we are mostly into roller coasters, this was a much better spot for us. Came on a Thursday in early March. Parking was easy, but was surprised they were only running one tram to the main gate (at half capacity due to COVID), so we ended up hoofing it.

Didn't take too long to get in since we pre-purchased tickets online. The park is a bit meandering and kind of tough to walk through but is also a big loop. We knew it was going to be a bit of an atypical experience because of COVID and came with an open mind. Like everyone probably does we started the day at Cheetah hunt and a 45 minute line. We were worried the rest of the day would be like this so from our phones bought the speed pass (which is more the day of than purchasing in advance). We ended up skipping this line, but the rest of the park had relatively minimal lines (as did Cheetah Hunt later in the day).

Much of walking though the part was a bit strange due to how little was open. They had some food and beverage stations, but through the whole park only three full restaurants were operating. Unfortunately we didn't realize this so ended up walking halfway around the park at lunch time.

Since I have family in the area, I'm sure we'll come back again one day, but likely not for a very long time.

My family went to Busch Gardens on 6/19/25. We purchased the dining pass with our meal. It took us nearly an hour and a half to greet our food. There was one cashier and 4 workers serving a very large crowd. One worker fixed drinks, one worker made fries, one worker sliced brisket , and one worker was there to serve the main entree. The bottleneck was obviously serving the entree, and at no time did the person serving drives or slicing brisket assisted the one worker preparing the entrees.

This diversity Hiring has ruined the experience. These families pay a lot of money and travel from around the world just to feel like the employees are at war with the guests. I mean it was bad! The rude interactions when asking a questions to an employee. It was the worst most racist experience I e ever had at a theme park.
As every one on the ride was trying to get her name. She refused and her co workers were not trying to help the situation because , you know, that loyalty thing.
I'll never go again. The diversity employees aren't even doing their jobs. Almost as if they are at singles club flirting with each other not afraid of losing their jobs.

When we arrived at the park we went to get our tickets scanned and were told that our passes were not for park admission but only a "Fast Pass". We couldn't even gain entry to the park. Upon realizing this was were quite upset and asked for assistance. We spoke to a nice gentlemen who advised us of the error we made in purchasing the initial tickets online. He advised us that he'd "void" out our initial tickets that were purchased in error, and get us the tickets that we needed to both get into the park and also gain admission to the "Fast Pass". He said he'd be back in 15 minutes and true to his word he provided us with the correct tickets of which we paid another $420.31. He advised that he voided out the initial charges of $320.32 and that we should see a refund to our credit card within 5-7 days (receipt/proof enclosed).

I went to check my credit card statement last night and the initial charges were not refunded. I then called the customer service number (813-884-4386). In speaking with a representative there I was advise that the initial tickets were "used" that they could not refund our money. These tickets were never used as they were supposed to be voided out when our new tickets were issued. On two difference occasions I was told I'd have to physically go to Busch Gardens in Tampa and request a refund in person. Twice I told the representative that I was from out of town and that this wasn't an option. I went back and forth and once I realized that I wasn't getting anywhere I asked to speak with a Manager. Upon my request I was told that a Manager couldn't assist me either. Nevertheless I still asked to speak with Manager and twice was denied my request. Feeling neglected and clearly not being helped, I advised that I was recording our encounter and that if he didn't forward me to a Manager I'd file a formal complaint. At this point the representative reluctantly placed me on hold and advised me that he would forward me to a Manager. I sat on hold for 45 minutes but a Manager never took my call. Unfortunately I couldn't wait any longer as I had a Zoom work call at works and had to hang up. About week later I e-mailed this time (versus a call) and was told twice I was not allowed to speak to a Manager. I've NEVER been denied access to a Manager at any company in ,y entire life.

This entire ordeal and been the most disappointing experience I've ever had at an amusement park. The experience completed ruined our day. Even though we paid for a "Fast Pass" we were only able to go on TWO rides the entire day due to how crowded the park was. Each ride we waited for over an hour. When we tried to go on a third ride we were told the "Fast Pass" line was 60-75 minutes long and at that point we had enough and left the park both frustrated and in disgust. While I understand it was a holiday week, I am not clear on why Fast Passes were clearly oversold. Waiting over an hour for each ride is not my idea of expectation of paying extra money for a "Fast Pass".

We are visiting from TN and decided to stop at Busch Gardens one day. My husband and I read up on size restrictions for the coasters. My husband is 6 feet tall, 340 pounds. He only fit on two of the rollercoasters even though one of the test seats said he would fit--he got up there and it wouldn't (especially the ones that go over the shoulders) Don't go if you have a bigger chest (male or female) or longer torso. He wears size 2/3XL shirts and I (as a 5'4" woman weighing 150 pounds) can wrap my arms fully around him. No reason he shouldn't fit on rides so I'm pretty disappointed. One broke while we were sitting in it so everyone had to get off and they gave us a priority pass but we already paid for fast passes. I absolutely wish the rides were just a little bigger because my husband is smaller than about half the people I saw there so I know they didn't get to do anything either. Unfortunately not worth going at all if you are anyone that wears even a 2X or larger. Attaching a picture of my husband's body for reference for those also worried about it.

We went here on vacation to Tampa, and weren't disappointed! I would like to disclose that we DIDN'T park at the parking lot, but had someone drop us off, so I have no idea what the parking situation is like. I do believe that there is a large golf cart that brings people to, and from, the parking lot. There is so much to see and do here. You can buy an admission ticket to enter, and see many animals, and cool sights. You can also purchase tickets for tours, safaris, rides, water park, and more! Not to mention the several gift shops around the park. We are doing the Serengeti tour, and I'm sure it will be great! The food is also reasonably prices for an amusement park!

Visited on what seemed like the hottest day of the year - Fourth of July! The park was clean, well kept, and fun. The layout was confusing but maybe that's because it was so hot . Would visit again, but with my own snacks and drinks. Like most amusement parks, the food can be pretty pricey. I would like to visit again

The entire atmosphere of this place is one of rot and decay. At least half the rides were closed. The workers were unpleasant and unfriendly. Costs were extremely high.

Your average zoo or animal refuge will allow much better opportunities for your children's interaction with animals than this sub-standard place. It's clear that management/investors are just trying to milk this place for all it's worth before they inevitably close it down.

I had a caricature drawn of my Gramma many years ago by a fella named Jared. It was a bit odd looking, but he was the only "artist" not drawing at the time, so I figured I'd give him a shot. I wheeled her over to the easel and he got to work and lemme tell ya--his drawing made no sense. It has all of these weird wobbly shapes all over it and it looked like he was doing scribble colors like a little kid would.

My Gramma seemed to like it, so I gave him my money and even bought a frame so she could hang it up.

Fast forward 9 years later and my Gramma is now the newest angel to pass through the pearly gates of heaven. We decided to display her drawing at her funeral right next to the casket. It's what she would've wanted. Well...lemme tell ya--that drawing made a lot of folks upset! They felt like the drawing was some sort of cruel joke that turned the funeral into a carnival. I had to hide it after most of the family complained. It was a sad sight to say the least.

Anyways...don't let Jared draw your Gramma, and if he does--keep that drawing in a dark room where no one can see it!

Biggest Rip-off ever. They don't tell you until you pull into the parking lot that it's $38.00 to park....are you kidding me...after 4 tix, parking, and $34.00 for a 3 ounce burger be prepared to drop a grand here...never going back

I paid full price to take my kids through the scare zones and waited in a line for Rita's to have some nice cold ice cream
After waiting 10 mins I was denied ice cream. She cut me off. Rudest employee of all time. I prayed I offed cash credit anything. She served 2 girls just before us, the drawer want closed
The machine was full of cold ice cream and I was with my children literally begging for ice cream. My voice hurts from being scared to screaming
All I wanted was something cold
And she denied me
As if she was the ice cream God
And I couldn't get what she had
She said she gets off at 10 it was 9:30
She argued with me for 10 mins before calling back up, then they threatened to calm security and throw me out of the park because I was begging kindly for a treat
That I was willing to pay for. She just made up her mind that she wasn't serving me. I wanted to wait for security because I couldn't believe how stubborn she was
She had it all together
Open cash register and loaded ice cream machine. But she was cruel and unkind

She'd rather argue about going home than just go. I left after they close the window in my face

A hungry coupe of kids or an angry employee
But I left as she wished
So sad
Bush Gardens is Great Overall. The Service is Nice. The Value is Good. The Ambiance is Fantastic. The Bush Gardens Location is Great Overall.

I might be biased because I used to come to busch gardens when I was in middle school, but if you haven't tried Iron Gwazi yet, it's time to get on it. Being that this is RMC (rocky mountain construction) first roller coaster in Florida I was excited to finally ride one of their creations. Let me tell you...this ride is amazing and intense in the best ways! The whole ride is an event with no downtime and the long wait is well worth it! What's also nice is that Busch Garden Tampa, has so many other greats rides as well like cheetah hunt, Montu and sheikra. The only down side I had was seeing some of the elephants seeming to be bored and pacing in the same place, I know it's an attraction to see animals in person and learn about them, but just sad to see animals in captivity. Just hope they find a better solution for the animals.

Busch Gardens Tampa is an excellent park to bring your family to. It's an hour from Orlando, but worth the drive.

They have some of the state's best coasters and are Sea World's land animal park. Iron Gwazi just opened recently and it is a great upgrade for a legendary attraction. I would dare say this is Florida's answer to Six Flags.

What's impressed me the most is that safety is their top concern with every attraction. They've taken every precaution to keep their guests safe when experiencing their rides. While they've become more lenient with their mask policy when it comes to COVID, they do offer hand sanitizer in plenty of spots throughout the park. If I lived closer, I'd be an Annual Passholder and that says a lot.

Took our grandson here and we were all dissappointed. Took us 2 hours just in line to get in the park to park our car. Then, General parking is $40 and Preferred Parking is $60 and isn't a whole lot closer plus you cannot ride the shuttle in preferred parking. After parking we had to stand in line for a half hour to get through security, ticket scanned and get in to the park. Everything was so unorganized. Pizza and 3 sodas was $74.00 with tip. Also, they charge extra for certain rides in addition to the price you paid to get in. When I got home I found out they charged me $213.06 for one EV rental for my husband. The price was suppose to be 110.00 plus tax. I only paid $89.00 at Disney for the same. I've notified them for a refund of the over charge. Still waiting for a reply. Save your money. Don't bother with place. You'll be glad you did. Wish someone had told me.

JEFFREY at Guest Relations Desk was controversial & demeaning to my family and myself. He criticized us getting into the park for Free since my son is active military and husband a Veteran, he said, must be nice, what else do you want. We then signed up for annual passes-one of them was a bronze and supposed to be silver. He said since I went the cheap way online he can't help me and that I am stuck with it for a year, it is 3rd party. I told him I did it a few hours ago and didn't even use the pass yet, he snickered, yeah because military wants it all for free. He told us to step aside took the next people in line who were horrified. He went in the back came back and said I have to buy new passes the same stupid way I did it the first time online. Then he could possibly refund the other. He then started mumbling, rolling eyes and complaining that of course he had to work on Easter. At this point other customers saw him visibly being controversial. So a lady, Diana came over and took up two registrars down and said she would help and do it, simple task. No apology or anything but she did it in two minutes. Someone like Jeffrey should NOT be working there or in any customer service field.
Other then that the park was empty and awesome and we got on every ride!

The Gardens used to be fantastic.It is now just a shell of its former self. Animals are way down in numbers. The food is basically the same in each location.It almost seems like Chick fil A is their star restaurant.Guest services acts like you are a nuisance to request help.Never again.On top of that $40 parking...yup $20 wasn't enough.Rides seem to be fun as long as the lines are not huge.

Ambience was so bad..used to give you a great feel now it is non existent.

My experience being raped at the Busch gardens chik fil a
- On Jan 20, 2024, I was raped in the Busch gardens chik fil a, I approached the chik fil a counter and proceeded to order a sandwich, fries and a drink. The cashier replied "since we don't do combos, that'll be $25." I've never felt so violated in my life. I stood there in disbelief and asked if I had heard her correctly. Sadly, I had. I ended up paying because my gf was hungry. I walked away with my tail between my legs wondering how I could ever move foward. I'm telling my story here today, so it doesn't happen to anybody else. #metoo

Confusing ticket system, half of our group can't ride together because of it. Mistakenly charged my credit card twice and refused to refund. A bunch of rides shut down. Food closed all over. Others opening late. Service is awful. Temu Six Flags at best.

First time back to Busch Gardens in years and overall did not disappoint. We went in a weekday so it was as busy but not overly so which was nice. Parking was easy enough. There is a tram that will take you to the park from the lot. All of the coasters were fun especially iron gwazi and sheikra.
The park is set up a bit odd and can be confusing so recommend using the map.

Riding: if you are a rider they have lockers near each ride that you can put your stuff in. They close 10 dollars all day and you can move your stuff from locker to locker. If you travel a tad lighter you can wear a Fanny pack on most of the rides. Ladies, it does have to be a Fanny pack with plastic clips for it to be allowed.

The only thing that keeps this from being 5 star was the heat. While we expedited it to be hot, I mean it is Florida in June, they lacked enough fans. They had fans closer to when getting on the ride and were very spread out. A lot of amusement parks have misters as well near the fans when standing in line but Busch gardens did not have them. There is one part of the park with a mister tunnel however you couldn't even feel it.

Highly recommend going outside of summer months.

This is my second visit to this theme park and it never disappoints. The lines where moving fast, even in the most popular rides. We chose to do the safari excursion with them and it will not disappoint you. Will recommend and will continue to visit.
No stars. Still haven't gotten a response from Busch gardens. Customer service is terrible and so is the park

Self service kiosk in the front of the park all out of service and the line for service is extremely long as a result. Over 95 degrees and you are receiving that much volume of customers and you can't even supply a self kiosk is a bit overwhelming for me pricing, just a complete lack of respect.
VERY fun coasters 10/10 food 10/10 experience 10/10 falcons fury is intimidating but awesome btw montu best ride. also very affordable
My first visit was amazing. Lines were long but moving along. Food was hot and good. Get the quick card it's faster for the lines.

So 1) I haven't been here in close to 20ish years, 2) we are Disney people and I know you can't compare things to Disney but this place was a disappointment.
Went on a Saturday - tickets were $90ish to get in but came with a free meal (will get to that in a second) and because the rollercoasters had well into the 2-3 time frames to ride, we spent another $99 a person for the unlimited fast passes.
The park was fairly dirty - garbage on the ground, and dirty eating tables.
The ride lines were long but I think the fast pass was worth it in this case. Just really expensive.

Most of the restaurants were closed so we had to look forever to find a restaurant we could use the free meal at, and because it was the only one open it was like a 30-40min wait.
The servers were the LEAST FRIENDLY people I have ever encountered. I get it, you'r job isn't the most pleasurable but the girl didn't smile, didn't say thing, and barely made eye contact. Check out wasn't any better.
Came out to a super dirty table to eat our food at, and the food was incredibly subpar. How do you mess up chicken fingers and fries????

Overall wouldn't go back. It's not the experience it use to be. Save your money.

Fun family trip (hadn't travelled since 2018 for a family trip) and my older teens loved the rides, my husband and I loved the animal areas and gorgeous landscaped areas and shaded walkways with so much to see! Once 4 pm hit a tremendous influx of people seemed to be entering, guessing for the Christmas Town foods for purchase and the related festivities?
Plenty to stay busy with, lots of walking (wear your best comfy walking shoes)! Food prices are up everywhere and amusement park food and beverage prices are always high. We opted for the all day dining wristbands which was $44 per person, but once we saw water priced at about $4/bottle and all food prices hefty we knew the bands were the way to go since staying til close. There are certain eateries the meal bands work at so investigate that via the Busch Gardens app.
We'd all 4 say only eat at the Smokehouse place if getting pulled pork or the chicken. The ribs and the brisket were so extremely fatty it was a bust! They also threw all the meats from a sampler platter atop the waffle fries that come with the sampler therefore creating soggy, cold, bbq sauced waffle fries. We found the large indoor Dragon Fire Grill & pub for dinner and it offers several fast counter service places to choose from plus beverage stations. This worked well and was conveniently located and had tons of seating.
Ride lines were reasonable from opening time til about 3 or 4 pm when a huge influx of people seemed to enter the park leading to super crowded walkways, seating areas outdoors, and much longer lines for many coasters. A special Christmas Town list of festivities and foods began at 4 we think. The festive foods are exorbitantly priced and appeared to be mere samples. The park sells 12 samples of Christmas Town "specialties" at a separate price if $40 something per person. We hardly saw anyone partaking in this but it's there.

I wanted to buy a Quick Queue (normal) for my daughter and I was charged for a unlimited and reserved a normal quick queue and half of the rides were closed and she got turned away from multiple rides and was scanned 3 times and they wouldn't give me a refund and half of the animals weren't out this place is going downhill

This place is going downhill. So many closed rides, broken down everything. Food areas are so dirty and food is terrible and soooo expensive. Fountain pop, NOT EVEN FULL IS $6.50! This is just a theme park waiting to go under while begging to survive off your prisoner status. Animals look run down and enclosures are in sub par condition. Soo dirty everywhere. Save your money!

Busch Gardens is a very clean park especially for the age of it. The staff here takes a lot of pride in their park and it shows. They always have great deals for Florida residents the fun pass allows you to pay for a day and come back all year. If you are going to go more than a couple times get at least one silver pass so parking is included well worth the cost instead of paying $30 for parking each time. If you are an annual pass holder make sure you check out the new pass member lounge. It gives you a great view of the safari area you can't see from anywhere else and it is air conditioned!

It was freakin AMAZING! We haven't been on vacation in years and we are so happy we chose Busch Gardens. The rides are absolutely, beyond a shadow of a doubt, some of the most incredible things you can do as a human being. Every coaster is surreal. Religious experience level. The animals are also amazing! We saw tigers, elephants, giraffes, hippos, and chimps up close in beautiful habitats. Most of the staff are really nice. Some are a little less enthusiastic but it's super hot out right now and they have to focus and be serious about the job which I felt like that's what they were doing. They do a decent job of keeping all the waiting areas covered and fanned. There's air conditioned areas and spots with mister fans but it's Florida and it gets hot. It feels like a tropical jungle, not miserable. They even had free sunscreen dispensers! The food is definitely pricey & is "theme park quality" if you know what I mean, but our tickets were incredibly affordable and the gift shops have really nice stuff that isn't insanely overpriced. We found some items for under $10. They have fancier stuff for more money, but it's good quality. They have name brand stuff like Guy Harvey. Parking was $35 but that was the only extra charge we were hit with. The park was really nice so I felt like the money I was spending was being used to actually maintain the place. I didn't feel like they robbed us like other theme parks... the hotels in the area really could be nicer, but downtown is close and has nicer hotels & food if that's what you're looking for. Overall, if you're a roller coaster fan, this place is paradise. Epic. Iron Gawazi Roller Coaster is just... wow. We were blown away. You have to experience it in your lifetime! No words do it justice.

Cobra's Curse...more like Kumba's Curse. Over ten years ago I went to Howlscream at Busch Gardens and someone threw up while on the front row of Kumba and it caused a 30 minute clean up delay. I had yet to witness a delay at Busch Gardens over the years until today. My friends and I decided to wait a little longer to be front row on Kumba and lone behold, as we were about to get on the rollercoaster next, a girl threw up in the front row. What was more disturbing was how the team cleaned it up. They used a spray hose to spray it down with water which caused it all go to the floor where you exit the ride. They ran the rollercoaster cars twice to dry it off and then sprayed it clean. It took about 30 minutes and seemed like the crew was figuring it out as they went. It's concerning that they did not have gloves nor did they wash their hands after. Working healthcare myself, Busch Gardens should provide their staff with PPE for moments like this.
Other than "Kumba's curse", the Skyride, Cheetah Hunt was closed in the morning, and Kobra's Curse was closed. Being a Florida resident, visiting parks in the winter months seems like off season is the worst time to visit which is why the Christmas Town happens. I was also disappointed that the safari ride seems to be only something guests pay for now as an extra excursion.
There are only a hand full of rides that are open, everything else is closed and under maintance!

Only 3/9 roller coasters were open. Charging everyone full price naturally! You're better off driving to Disney. Half of there animals look like they are about to collapse, quite sad. Won't ever be back.
A beautiful place with a variety of wonderful, well-cared-for animals in what seem to be comfortably designed habitats, beautiful gardens, a variety of rides and delicious food options.

Don't arrive when they open. Lines a mile long. Not an exaggeration. Preferred parking actually a negative as it drops you off at ticket gate entrance but line to enter stretches almost to remote parking lot.
So your day at Busch Gardens starts with horrific line.
Sea World better alternative. Better organized.

In my opinion, this is one of the best all around amusement parks I'm the country. It is a little on the pricey side though. For that got to knock down a star. Some of the best coasters this country has got to offer or at this park it's a good thing to it as well very easy to get in and out Park is very accessible with transport of a shuttle from the parking lot to the front gate you will not go wrong with the quality of coasters at this park .

Grade: B+

As a season pass holder I always have a great time at bush gardens , their parks are always clean and well
Maintained they offer great deals for pass members the staff is always friendly , I enjoy the fact that you can see animals as well as ride the rides ! One complaint is they don't have expectant mother parking . But I wanted to
Compliment their "christmas town" experience , I can't say enough how beautiful the light displays were and how great the shows were and how magical the scenery was ! I've been a pass holder four years and never attended boy was I missing out ! Will now make it a Christmas tradition !

We went 9/8/2024. I was so disappointed that there were a lot of rides and exhibits that were closed. Also dozens of Kiosks were shuttered too! We ate at Serengeti and the food was mediocre and extremely high priced! You also have to schedule and pay extra to see the elephants now. I feel that they are going down hill dramatically since the last time we were there! I should also mention that they now charge $5 a person for the sky ride unless you have a pass! In addition, they seem to be losing animals and not replacing them; like the lions and tigers.

Personally, I feel that the admission price should be enough to keep the park operating and allow access to all the rides and exhibits. They also are charging over $30 to park. I just can't justify going again with all of these issues!

The park obviously is fun the rides are great but they have an extreme issue with dining. They sell you "all day dining" but in reality you wait all damn day to eat once. The food line for the parks only decent meal is 2 hours or more. The park is filled with soda and fried bullshit but if you want actual food the only option in the whole park is smokehouse and by the time you eat the park is closed. Line up for dinner as soon as you get here in the morning.

... update. It's been an hour since I posted this and now I'm still in the line to get into the next line.

If you're for thrill rides, Busch Gardens will not disappoint.

We purchased a fun pass back in July when we visited & was able to use it once during our stay, which is good until the end of the year. We did this because we planned to come back during the holidays to use it again, so we were able to get 2 more uses out of it.

Parking is $30, unless you have some sort of annual pass where general parking is covered. You then get in line for a shuttle to bring you to the park from the parking lot, but honestly, sometimes just walking to the park without the shuttle will get you there even faster, especially when it's crowded & lines are long. They could use a few more of these shuttles to keep the lines & waits shorter.

Lines can be long & hectic, so best to plan accordingly. We didn't look into it, but I believe you can purchase some sort of fast queue to shorten or bypass the general line. Not sure how this works, or how much, but info should be listed on their site. I do know they have limited quantities on this. So if you're short on time, or just don't want to wait in the long lines, you may want to take advantage of this.

We've rode Cheetah Hunt, which is our favorite so far. We even got to ride it one evening where they did the fireworks show, so it was a great experience to ride this while fireworks were going in the background.

We've also rode Montu. For some reason, this ride is usually our first ride of the day & then we just make our way through the rest of the park. Tigris was also fun & we have tried to ride Cobra's curse 3x on all 3 different occasions, but either there is always maintenance going on, or the line was over 2 hrs.

Most of the times aren't always accurate. I feel like we get through the line faster than what is displayed.

We also enjoy disneyworld, but when it comes to thrill rides, I highly recommend Busch Gardens.

I heard Iron Gwazi is being built & should be open to ride sometime in March of 2022 & will be known as North America's tallest hybrid coaster & the fastest & steepest hybrid coaster in the WORLD!

I'm saddened by the way this park is being run now a days. I'm sure it unfortunately has to do with staffing issues. But it sucks to walk into a park and every food stand we saw that was farther then 40 yards into the park was closed. The rides are good and all and Iron quasi is hands down the best " conventional " coaster I have ever been on! only out done by guardians of the Galaxy at Epcot!! We used to love this park. And it still looks amazing. It just felt closed i don't know lol

I was hoping to get more pictures but I was too busy riding the rides. The park is pretty large and unfortunately the full train line was down for maintenance as was the sky ride so I definitely got a workout. The pluses you can experience both animals and extreme rides all on one place. I had already been to the zoo but there are a few items here not at the zoo like lions. The cost as part of the Tampa city pass which I bought for $100 and included this as a place to visit is a great deal. Even if your paying full price it's no more than comparable parks. There are many great coasters. I rode all that were operational. I would note several are in the top ten globally in terms of number of inversions with two having 7. Also one coaster is in the top ten for longest track. The lines were non existent on this day which was another plus. Note I visited during the week on a Monday in October. They have plenty of lockers to put your valuables which is another reason I didn't get many pictures as these coasters I would advise putting the phone in the locker while riding.

There are several shows but we were focused on rides. With no lines we easily rode all coasters by 3 pm and took a 1 hr lunch break in the giant pizza hall. The food was ok while I had a pizza that tasted pretty good the mozzarella cheese sticks had sat under the heat lamp too long and had firmed back up. The cup for soda is pretty essential while it's around $15 you get free refills all day you buy it. The best part is that there is a coco cola freestyle machine that provided all the choices I like in soda.

The first ride Sheikra was amazing. It takes you up the incline and then suspends you briefly at the edge before the drop. It was very smooth and a good ride. I liked it and felt it was in the top coasters I've ridden. Also Montu felt like I was in a fighter jet with inversions leading into tight turns.

The park of course is beautifully landscaped. The only negative is the layout is very difficult to navigate there are several dead ends and a few areas where you'll backtrack to get to another area. They do have an app with gps to tell you where to go but it doesn't habe bathrooms on it. Like other parks the bathrooms will be well hidden and difficult to find so be warned try and go way before you need to as you may not be near one or you'll have trouble finding one in some parts of the park because they are well disguised to blend into the buildings.

This was my first time visiting Busch Gardens and it did not disappoint. My fiancé and I arrived right at rope drop so we arrived at 9:30am and got let in 9:45 but they had the park still sectioned off until 10am. The rides are quite intense so I wouldn't recommend the rollercoasters for little kids but they do have a kid area for easy rides. Here are my tips and tricks:

1. Stay at a hotel close by and just uber there instead of parking since it was $10 for total Ubers of getting there and leaving.
2. I recommend the ice show since it was very entertaining and had amazing colors and ice dancing. Great for kids!
3. Bring a small bag if you and your group are planning to go on the big rides since you have to put your items in the lockers. The lockers are quite small. I would recommend the $10 all day pass cuz you can switch different lockers throughout the day when you're going to different rides.
4. Bring a water bottle since you can refill it with the water fountains instead of buying their expensive water.
5. Go on the train if you need to take a break and you can see some animals during the tour but not as much as you would on the safari tour. *The safari tour is about $80-$100.*

Rides on order to visit:
1. Iron Gwiza - one of the best rides of the park! It opened about a year ago so it is one of the newer rides at the park.
2. Montu - if you like going upside down this is the ride for you! Your feet does hang so wear tennis shoes otherwise you can set your sandals or flip flops to the side.
3. Sheikra - this one of my favorite rides! You do drop about 20 ft and you do not get wet but you do go through water. This ride can hold about 12 people so they do go faster with the line.
4. Kumba - similar to Montu where you do go upside down! I did get nauseous after the ride.
5. Cheetah Run - Honestly based on TikTok this ride was hyped up and said it was the one to go to right away. I didn't think it was that great as it wasn't the most intense as Montu and Iron Gwiza. It honestly just goes really fast is what makes it unique.
6. River ride - You will get wet on this ride so bring an extra set of clothes and wear flip flops! It is really fun!

Ridiculous on the pricing....chicken fingers and fries for 6 cost 170 bucks. Dirty, poorly maintained.... there was even an alligator with a paw cut off and EXPOSED .... really gross NEVER going back. Out of town guests that paid 500 bucks to get in this dump....very embarrassed.
Ride cleanup delay took forever while 5 people watched 1 do the slowest cleanup job on record.
Worst amusement park experience ever. Half the exhibits, rides and vendors were closed for renovations. Expensive and limited food options

I loved the coasters, I loved how low the wait times were. Cheetah Hunt is my favorite ride of all time! But there was a particular experience that soured things for me:

"Can I have a water?" I asked the Village Snacks employee. She started filling up a water cup for me. 

"Oh, no, sorry- a bottled water," I said.

This large woman with a huge mole was extremely rude for literally no reason at all. "That would've been free," she said sarcastically.

"I wanted to be able to close the cap," I muttered. I was going to be carrying the water around the park; it was much easier to have a bottle, not a soda cup. She made me feel like I was weird for asking for a bottle. She continued to be rude, asking "which one?" and getting mad that I didn't immediately understand that she was asking what brand of water I wanted. "Which one?!" She said again, annoyed. She then got irritable again when I didn't put my card in the right spot right away. 

This was 10:45AM on Monday, August 19, 2024, at "Village Snacks" by the entrance. It is unfortunate that this nasty individual put a bad taste in my mouth first thing in the morning, because every other park employee I encountered was very kind the rest of the day. Perhaps don't put your mean, nasty, rude for no reason employees in the front of the park.

Today, we took my daughter to Busch Gardens. She's 9, and we are visiting from mass for school vacation week. We bought tickets and speed passes to try and be able to get full experience. About 70% of the rides were down for yearly maintenance. This was NOT told while buying tickets online. And guest services were no help to the cause. Then they had other rides that had mechanical errors. Which happens. Totally understandable. But when you have over half your rides down at once for The yearly maintenance and you do not inform guests online or signs at gate. It's a scam. These were planned closings and guests have the right to know, especially when spending almost as much as a Disney ticket. When guest relations only say it's corporate fault, that's bologna. Nobody would spend hundreds to go to the zoo.

Very poorly maintained. No structure. Wait times were way off. Kids cutting in line. Half the rides were closed. Not one attendant spoke to us the entire day to explain ride safety. The standards have definitely gone down in the past 10 years. Will never visit this park again.

This was an unpleasant experience. Several rides "broke down" after we had been waiting in line for a long time (30 min +). We got the all-day food pass and learned the quality of the food is on par with prison gruel.

The staff enforced none of the rules. I saw people walking around the park and on rides barefoot. People were allowed to bring bags and water bottles on the rides causing them to go flying everywhere constantly.

The animals were emaciated and some appeared starving and sad. Everything was horrible about this park. We should've gone back to Universal Studios instead.

Don't go here unless you find a way to go free and they give you an all-day alcohol pass for free. You'll need a buzz to get through this one.

Just in the past but today prolonged weather delays well after the storm was gone, many rides closed, and rides breaking when we got to the front of the line and waited forever. Not sure if this is the new state of Busch Gardens or just terrible luck, but a horrible waste of money today.

Spent my daughter's birthday here and had a good time. It started to rain so we went for lunch at the place called Dragon fire grill and pub and for the price the food was not good. The chicken sandwich seemed like low quality meat and the burger was the same for 18 dollars each. The park wasn't busy because school started the next day so the lines weren't long at all. It was a fun day all the rides were great except the scorpion that ride seems a little unsafe with the way it jerks you.

Omg, I went to Busch Gardens Tampa Bay Monday morning at the end of January and had such a great time. I arrived just before the park opened, so I was able to wait inside the park toward the coasters I wanted to start with. Cobras Curse, and Falcons. Fury were both closed that day for some reason. I also noticed multiple smaller shops, and eateries were closed. My guess is staffing and low attendance during this time but I never asked anyone. I was able to ride Cheetah Hunt (twice), Montu, Iron Gwazi (twice), Serengeti Express, Sheikra, and Tigris (twice) plus browsing gift shops all between 10am and noon. This was during their summer concert series so the bridge near the bird garden was closed. This caused people to have to walk around the entire other direction to get to Stanleyville where Sheikra and Tigris are. It was also on Monday, so there were no Mardi Gras festivities happening.

Don't know why this place has bad reviews.

Everything was super fast for us... parking, tram, getting in etc. we showed up as the park opened because I read the bad reviews about waiting and we didn't need to. I was stressing for nothing.

If you don't want to wait in line, get the quick queues. You're in and out of a ride in a few minutes. If you don't, there were still people who walked on the ride and off the same exact time as us and we had the quick queues.

If you go early, there's very few there! We went as soon as it opened up and everyone pretty much walked in the rides.. the later you go, the more people.

The rides and stores open up throughout the day. If you make it a day trip, you'll see it come alive.

There are a few attractions closed but I spent a full day there and was very satisfied. The food and drinks are expensive but there's also a McDonald's like across the street.. eat a quick meal before coming in. Then buy some snacks or a meal before you leave.

The shows are awesome. The animals were cool to see and we enjoyed the rides!

Bring bug spray !

Tampa's African Safari amusement park! Whenever I've came to Busch Gardens, it's mainly to see the animals. I would go as far as to say that they're not known for their rides. Don't get me wrong, they have some pretty fun rollercoasters, such as the Sheikra( My favorite) and Montu, but the main attraction are all the animal exhibits and tours.

We decided to go on the Serengeti Safari Tour! Whew I had a BLAST. We saw all types of animals, but being as giraffes are one of my favorite animals, I jumped at the chance to feed one. They were so calm and sweet. I honestly don't think they gave a damn about us being there, they were more so interested in the wedges of lettuce the Safari Instructor handed to us to feed them. They also had a cameraman on our truck to take out photos which were available for ridiculously high prices after the tour.
