One thing I will say is that although I always have a blast here, it sure will cost you a pretty for just about everything. In my opinion, the only way to make Busch Gardens worth it is to buy the a citypass and visit multiple places in addition to Busch gardens because it costs most as much to get into the park.

Also the tours can really add up in price as the safari tour was an extra $40 per person and you had to reserve your seats days in advance!

Seems like I'm talking about money a lot but I just wouldn't want anyone to come here expecting a deal on anything haha. Otherwise it's fun for just about anyone regardless of age. I'd definitely come back if I were to visit Tampa again!

The only good thing about this park is the animals. We saw them via the train. I think it's terrible the actual safari is $40 a person. The train was free and we got pretty close to some of the animals.

The park itself looked run down and like it was struggling. We took the sky tram, which was a waste. Didn't see much and the line was long. The rest of the rides has very long lines.

Two hours in and my worst experience at an amusement park, period. The rides that aren't closed for wind issues are closed for annual maintenance. For example, do both water rides need to be down the same day! In addition, some of the animal attractions are also closed. Save your money and go to Orlando, or at least something worthwhile like the Tampa aquarium or the zoo. Their rides usually run and the animals are better. Top off it all with a 40$ parking fee, and this place is just sad.

A couple of months before the end of 2024, I asked my kids what they wanted to do for Christmas. They told me that they wanted to go to Busch Gardens. Of course my kids wanted to go on the rides ESPECIALLY on the new Iron Gwazi. I was fortunate to be able to get a package deal to go to Sea World as well. In addition, I paid the meal package for the four of us for both Sea World and Busch Gardens.

So, I paid well over $1000 between airfares, hotels, parking, gas, entrance fees, food, etc. to make that happen. We didn't have any major issues with Sea World. However, our experience at Busch Gardens was a nightmare. Since I am a senior citizen, there are limited rides I can handle. The one I really wanted to go on was the Congo River Rapids. Both the Kumba and the Congo River Rapids were closed. In fact, other rides were shut down temporarily or permanently for the day. Three times my son waited on line for at least 30 minutes for a particular ride and then it was shut down by the time he got to the front of the line. My partner and I primarily came to see the shows. You had to get there 30 minutes early to be able to get in. By the time a show was over, you had to walk many times 20 minutes or more to go to the exact opposite side of the park to see the next show, so, by the time you got there, it was completely full and you couldn't get in.

My daughter and I are vegetarian. Prior to going to Busch Gardens, I studied the menus to know the places we could get salads or other vegetarian items. The wait to get most food was an average of 30 minutes and then once we got to the place to finally order, they were out of salads or whatever else we were able to eat. Then, it was tough to get a table to sit down. Once we had to eat standing up.

I noticed that one of your restaurants offered funnel cake. I was so looking forward to having one. It would be considered a full meal ticket, but, that was fine with me. We waited 40 minutes and when we finally were able to order, we were told that we could not get a funnel cake because it was considered a dessert. We would have to order chicken fingers or whatever non-vegetarian meal they offered....nothing of which we could eat.

But, the real kicker was this....The main goal of going to Busch Gardens was for my kids to go on the Iron Gwazi. When my kids arrived at the line and read the sign stating there would be a 2 plus hour wait, one of the Ambassadors in charge of the line was telling everyone to come back in the evening because the line would be much shorter then. So, that's what my kids did. However, when they came back to finally get on the ride, the line closed down 2 hours early because the park was having fireworks that night. So, they never got on the one ride I came all the way from Arizona to have them ride.

There was no sign earlier in the day stating it would close early and it was THEIR Ambassador who told them to come back later.

So, this entire experience was a very frustrating, horrible experience. I sent this email to them 3 times and no one ever bothered to respond.

We will likely never come back to Busch Gardens after our experience today! It's like all the rudest people chose THIS day, the day we decided to attend, to also go to the park. It started with a group of 10 or so kids blatantly cutting from the very beginning of the cheetah, walking all the way through as close to the front one at a time (to be sneaky like) and stop right in front of us. When we called them out on it they just waited the jumped over the rope later to cut This sort of thing happened all day. On another ride (Cobra's Curse) there were 3 groups of two that violently pushed their way to the front of Cobra's Curse. If you dare stop them, they're ready to throw down! When you let staff know, they don't acknowledge the issue. Why bother posting signs that patrons will be removed from the park for cutting off there's no follow through? These patrons know that won't happen!

Food is overpriced and eh. No one, literally no one, hardly wears masks! And it's no longer enforced. If that bothers you, stay home. People are up in your bubble in line. Social distancing is a thing of the past. At Busch Gardens, covid no longer exists! It appears they're open at full capacity as the parking lot was nearly entirely full when we arrived at noon.

Lines are over an hour long in most cases and the park is only open from 8-10. You can purchase passes $30 per person per ride (essentially a fastpass) but even those lines aren't super quick. For that price I better get right on!

There's hardly any quiet spaces to sit and people watch, or just relax.

The tables are gross and filthy. Cleaning seems to have stopped as well.

Busch Gardens, you REALLY need to up your game! Yuck! Not worth the money!

This was a much worse experience than ZooTampa.

We were really looking forward to it thinking it would be a lot like SeaWorld since they have the same ownership now. It is much different and more poorly run.

First of all - don't waste your money on the food pass what a rip! There are only 3-4 restaurants where you can use it and they each have an hour or more line. They had way too few employees - we finally left because we were so hungry.

We went on a little safari ride where we got to feed giraffes - that was fun. Other than that don't waste your money.

I will not go back and I want a refund on the food pass.

Unbelievable how much has changed since we had last been there around 10 years ago or so. Very disappointing experience. Rides were good when they were open, lol. They would just randomly close rides down when it was clear out. Get all the way up after waiting for period of time and close it down for no reason, very aggravating for time spent driving and money spent. Not cheap anymore. Park was supposed to close at 10:00. They closed almost every ride down at 8:30 and the food stands down between 8 and 8:30. That's just crazy and very frustrating. Families driving from all over and spending lot of money to be there. Staff were very miserable, it was so bad. We ordered 3 double cheeseburgers at one place and I thought she was going to tell us no by the way she was acting, it was so odd. We also ordered funnel cake there at 8:30ish, he said they only had one left, lol. They were shutting down early. So I asked him why they were shutting things down so early. He said, he was just wanting to get home, lol. (Why work there then) I told him families were traveling to come here long ways and spending lot of money, it's just not the same anymore at places like this. I'm sure they are understaffed and they need better employees that care about what they do and making it fun for families there. But it starts with good management, management has failed there. You can tell it is just about the money there. Just greedy companies that don't care about the people that are keeping them open today. That is very sad. When you see how well a company like Chick-Fil-a is ran, u see the difference in good management. Will never return after this experience, was so bad. Pay all that money for them to close everything down randomly throughout day and hour and half earlier than advertised time when you purchase tickets. They should be obligated to give some money back when they do things like this. Maybe then, management will actually take a look at it and care when they start losing money. Will just pay more money to go to Universal next time.
The park was dirty. Employees unhelpful with directions. And the food was less than impressive.

What a waste of money. Purchased park pass and all day meal pass. Totally ripped off. You are allowed food every 90 minutes from 4 or 5 places only. Guess what, the line is at least 90 minutes long at every 'restaurant' you are allowed to use.
Half the rides/attractions were closed, which means the lines for everything else is doubled. 90-120 minutes is ridiculous to wait. Wait, it gets better - after waiting on line for 90 minutes, the ride breaks. Sorry, this one is closed too. What a joke.
Because half the crap is closed, all the shows are full way before they even start.
This was a total waste of a day.

Drove from Orlando to come to Busch gardens on a Wednesday. I have been very disappointed with the park. More than half of the rides and shows are closed and because of that all of the open attractions have very long lines. The BBQ restaurant only has one of the four lines open and have been waiting for 20 minutes and not even half through the line still. The only reason the park didn't get 1 star is because we can still see the animals. I typically like coming here over the Orlando theme parks because of the roller coasters, but most likely will not be coming back anytime soon

Have you ever heard the saying of someone who can't find their own ass in the dark with two hands? Welcome to Busch Gardens Tampa Bay. Wait times grossly inaccurate. For example Cheetah hunt Reported 60 minutes when we went in at 10:30 am half hour after opening. Actual time was over 3 hours. No stoppages during that time either. Tell me they isn't gross under reporting. Would've liked to have chosen to not go on the ride. Wasted half a day on one ride. I would've actually bought an express pass if I had known how long the lines were really going to be. Then at other rides they don't have wait times posted at all. No staff at the front of the line either, so it's just a crap shoot. Not sure if that's better than the gross underestimates. I suppose it is because my expectations are set.

Get an express pass. I saw the same kid go in more than half a dozen times before I even reached half way point waiting in line.

LOVE this place! Absolutely a great place to visit when in Tampa, you won't regret it. The prices for admission are fair and the staff is knowledgeable. I love the theme and environment; a mix of nature and fun. Which is what their logo symbolizes! It's a roller coaster tree, pretty interesting. The park has some interesting history behind it. I also like how it's a nice park but it's not overly big and not very small. With events throughout the year it makes coming to Busch Gardens exciting every time!

After spending $100 per person on tickets and $34 on parking I expected more than 1/2 of the park to be open. There are more overpriced vendors than there are rides and animal exhibits. I was also offended to be approached in the middle of the park by a time share salesman. Overall it was a very sub par experience.

Date Nite was a HIT!! We had an amazing time. So grateful for the nice weather today. It's been raining a lot.
Here are my thoughts on hitting up Busch Gardens.
1. Going in the evening is a good idea. It's not as hot.
2. Make sure you wear comfy shoes. How do people wear flip flops? Lol
3. Tigris is All That. It was amazing. Loved it. Make sure you try it out.
4. Cheetah Hunt... always my Fave. Sit in the front. And hold your hands high.
5. Funnel cake. The disappointment of the nite. It was HORRIBLE. I was so sad. Will not be getting it ever again from there.
Overall great evening. So awesome to have an amusement park in our city!!

Don't waste your money over 5 of the rides were broke down went on February 22nd 2025. When you asked when will be open no response very rude. We drove 3 hours for grandson birthday big mistake. Merry go round no strap for young children (nothing like Universal who is very careful and has safety first priority) The Little boat for the little ones no strap he almost fell out. Train ride terrible when stopped everyone was jerked so hard horrible. Need to really dress you employees with uniforms wearing hoodies with hood on not professional. Maybe take a look at Disney or Universal. This place needs improvement all over very sad experience.

Yalllllll. I always have a blast here, PERIODT.


We have season passes (which we spent WAYYY too much on but we don't care- it's fun).

Firstly, we have passes so we have preferred parking. Sometimes if the park closes at like 6and we pop in at 3-4, they don't even have an attendant for parking and you can go on thru no matter if you have a pass for all.

They take your temp.

You must make reservations due to COVID19. If you're buying at the gate put "0000000000000" as hour ticket number for reservations.

Then it's go time! We go to the back of the pro first to Tigris, then we work our way thru.

This isn't Disney and it's not Universal... it's not magical and employees aren't giving you the best experience ever..but if you take it for what it's worth it's sssooo fun

The animals are neat and the park is clean.

The issue is that you get what you pay for. For example, the Montu ride said 20 minute wait, been waiting for an hour because they are cheap and running 1 train instead of 2 or 3. Seen this on multiple rides. I think this is due to how cheap the tickets are so they want people to buy skip the line add ons instead of providing a good guest experience.

We are Disney pass holders, even though you pay more the experience is premium versus the cost cutting and the "I don't five AF" attitude of their employees

Came here on Veterans weekend to use my free ticket. Always appreciate that they've given military and their dependents free entrance since 2004. You get the tickets through Waves of Honor.

We got there at about 1pm, they place was packed and they were out of preferred parking. They were only open until 7pm sadly.

We did get to ride many of the rides but 6 hours doesn't give you enough time to make it through the park because the roller coaster lines are ridiculously long.

I highly recommend paying the $12 to feed the kangaroos! They were super cute and photogenic. You can also arrange a sloth interaction. Recommend booking ahead online, they only have 6 slots per day and only 1 time. Needless to say, we missed that.

Busch Gardens is like the Big Lots of amusement parks....I was really upset that the smoking area is right next to the ticketing counter where we are forced to wait (with children). So wonderful that we have to inhale all the second hand smoke. I went here on a Saturday and I waited in the ticketing line for at least 55 minutes. They were not fully staffed, as all booths were not open. They didn't even have fans to help circulate the air in a crowded space! Another issue was the fast pass! I paid over 150 for 3 fast passes and sometimes had to wait 15 minutes to get on a ride, despite there being no line except the general line. What's the point of paying extra....


Pros: stroller parking & tram

Cons: parking $25, lines are horrendous

If u want thrill, the roller coasters are great. The scorpion was less fun but cheetah, kumba, iron gwazi,Shekrah.... NIIIIIIICEEE!

Water fountains are available everywhere. U won't get dehydrated. Plus, you can get splash from roller coaster rides as well ;)

Busch gardens has a few safety measures in place for the pandemic. Masks are required by everyone two years and up. And they somehow take everyone's temperature by having us walk in a tent--Cool technology.

The self service ticket lines are a little long because every other one is closed for social distancing, so arrive early if you need to get tickets printed.

This is the first amusement park/attraction I've been to since Covid, so I'm not sure what other places are doing, but I was hoping to see rides and trams sanitized after each use. Or maybe even someone giving hand sanitizer to the guests before they got on the ride to keep it as clean as possible.

I wish there were more shows available, our only options were music and Sesame Street on a Saturday. But we mostly came to see animas with the kiddos on this trip, so they were happy.

The food we had was sub par with a hefty price tag, like other theme parks. We all had chicken strips or chicken sandwiches. The chicken was dry, and the fries were not warm or flavorful. Definitely take advantage of the Food and Wine options if you can, we will be trying that out next time!

We purchased tickets for here instead of Disney, we couldn't be more disappointed. Four of the major rides are "Closed Temporarily" for repairs. The restaurants that were supposed to be open at 11:30, didn't open until 11:50.
Crazy expensive for little items.
All the little shops are closed.
Even though I don't want to support an organization like Disney, we would have had a better time there.

Literally everything is closed. What a waste of money and $25 for parking. Just keep the whole park closed if you can't open things. Almost every rice and food vendor was closed. Can I get my money back?

My husband and I purchased some tickets to take our granddaughters and then my husband passed away 3 weeks ago. Busch Gardens will not give me a refund, only another day to use the tickets. I am moving back to Utah and my granddaughters did not have the chance to fly here in order to go to Busch Gardens. I am very disappointed in the lack of compassion that these people have. If I ever come to Florida again, I will NEVER go to Busch Gardens again.

They really need to control how many people get into the park theres no reason to wait 5 hours JUST TO GET INSIDE! And then another 45- hour and a half for a 2 minute ride and not to mention if you got big tatats , meat and a BBL like me forget even considering that you can fit the rides they're the size for 5 foot skinny people instead of normal inclusive sizes

Had a great time ! They were good about mask mandate .( especially for the ones still fearing no masks don't worry they enforced it ) the lines weren't too long about 30-40 minutes . Can't wait to come back

5 stars for Busch Gardens Tampa Bay! A Tampa Bay classic attraction, tons of exotic animals to see and interact with, great roller coaster rides and (before COVID/hopefully soon), great shows to see! The park is massive so download the app on your phone prior to your visit as they currently aren't giving out paper versions. This is an all day attraction so come when they open if you want to do everything and beat the crowds. I recommend doing at least one of the VIP animal experiences as well - the kangaroo one was really cool, will be returning and hopefully doing the Hippo one next!

This trip to Busch Gardens was very disappointing. We started the day waiting in line as soon as they opened at 10 knowing the ride opens at 10:30. We waited till 11 and finally decided to leave the line after the worker side it was going to be awhile. We downloaded the app to see what rides were open to our surprise many of the roller coasters were closed down for the day (SheiKra, Cobra's Curse, Montu, Kumba, & Montua). When you pay so much money to come to your park and half the good rides are shut down has extremely disappointing. We also purchased the Meal pass and EVERY time we tried to use it, the system did not recognize our what we purchased. Busch Gardens do better, we were so disappointed during our visit and felt cheated!!

Worst experience. Over an hour to just get in the parking lot and pay $38. Another hour in line to get in the gate. People were cutting in front of the line causing total chaos up front. There were maybe 2 or 3 security people who just stood around doing nothing. By the time we got in we were hungry. It was a one hour wait to buy some awful chicken fingers. The employees were rude and uninterested in working there. I guess I'm used to Disney. If you end up here turn around and run as fast as you can!
No communication, huge line to get in security guard directing people back without guidance. Horrible experience!

I love the park itself; plenty of coasters and the fact that that there is also a zoo so to speak is cool. However; the food is less than to be desired. I know...it's a theme park but ...A family of 6 for lunch costs $150?! Not to mention a grilled chicken sandwich with a chicken breast that was about 3/4 the size of an iPhone and cold. I get inflation but come on, do better.

Christmas Town is a waste of money. Kiosks weren't given advertised products. Some weren't operational on a Saturday night and staff werent trained. They wouldn't give us water or soda despite the lanyard clearly indicating it was included. Lines and signage were horrible. Dont do it!
Good rides, the waits weren't too bad. Pretty much comparable to the Williamsburg one but this one has a lot more animals which makes it feel like you're at the safari which is an added plus.

My favorite go to place on my day off! Not only is it great for me, but my kids love it as well so that's a MEGA PLUS!
So many things to do, see, buy LOL.

This park is only getting three stars for just being an ok park. Things I loved about the park was the roller coasters were great!!!!! This is the only reason they are getting three stars and the only reason I would come back. BUT the park seemed dirty, the staff were mediocre and nothing sparked any joy other then the coasters. We had 2 kids under ten and most all the kids rides were shut down. This park could turn things around by training your staff to make a more memorable experience for their guest. And clean up a little.

So much to do. We took our soon to be 5 year old and she really enjoyed her day there. We didn't even get to see everything. Sesame Street area was a big hit and she liked the couple bigger roller coasters we got on as well. We didn't see many shows but the food was good at the smokehouse and overall we had a great experience. We used CityPass.
It's great but it does have a little wait but that not their fault so over all it's great
Busch Gardens is a fun place with thrilling rides, delicious food, amazing shows, and wildlife. I recommend going with your family and friends especially for a vacation.
It was very good but the one bad thing was the wasps on the top of iron gwazi. But 5 stars if they take the nest away

I was out with the kiddos and my husband Raymond, and wow! This place was amazing! We went on many roller coasters. One thing I loved about this place was the boarding passes! I have a very small bladder so the boarding passes helped me a lot! I wish all of the rides had boarding passes because when I went on the cheetah hunt, I almost let out a little tinkle! I also loved the animals and the yummy food! I recommend this if you want a fun day out with the kiddos!

So, we made a decision to go to Busch Gardens on a Thursday. I have never been, and my Grandkids have no memory of going so it was perfect. I arranged to arrive when the park first opened at 10 am. Advanced parking was great. I love this place! The only reason a 4 is because there were quite a few rides not running today. The park has the best of both worlds the animals and the rides. I purchased the Tampa Bay City Pass which includes Busch Gardens - a fantastic value! I did not purchase the all-day meal pass just because, when I paid for a meal at chic filet for the 3 of us it was just under $40. We must have gone on a great day barely any lines for anything. The girls went on water ride about 8 times in a row the canyon something, I can't remember the name. I forget the name and they decided to use the body dryer for $6 after as they were soaked! One other thing I think is great is the day locker rental, you pay a small fee and it transfers very easily to which ever ride your going on. Great rate great day!

Bush gardens is great for the whole family the little ones can see animals and have fun in the Sesame Street area for young kids where you can meet you fave Sesame Street characters Elmo the count big bird Oo so fun.. and for the adults and the kids in us all there are the more adult rides and roller coasters Ooo yea fun all round for all ages the animals and soo fun and just adorable and exciting to see

Was a bit weary of coming here during Covid, but I had such a good experience that I don't regret it.

Rides were periodically spritz with sanitation sprays and then went through a ride cycle without any riders to allow the seats to dry off. Handrails were wiped by employees and temps were taken when you first entered the park.

Another perk was that we went on a Thursday early on in the day and stayed until about 3pm and managed to ride all the rides we wanted to and some even more than once. The lines were much more bearable - typical wait was about 5-10 minutes.

Busch Gardens is a unique amusement park, essentially a Six Flags and a zoo put together. This hodge podge at times seems a bit strange, but I enjoyed the variety - and hopefully the diversity of attractions means there's something for everyone in your party!

Many of the roller coasters are a bit short and may have long waits. If you're going during a busy season, a fast pass will likely be a wise investment. Though I came in December, so it was pretty empty. The animal attractions are pretty cool, with some of my favorites being the kangaroos, the interactive bird houses, and the big cats. You can also take the train through the large plains to see more animals that aren't as easy to see from the pathways.

Pricing can be pretty steep - as much as $100 per person or more! But if you get the Tampa Bay City Pass, that can make it more affordable (as long as you also want to do other bay attractions). Also, while the park itself is nice, the area it's in is a bit run down, so be aware of that if you're booking lodging or finding parking (it's best to pay for the park parking, even if it's pretty steep). Overall a fun experience if you're a fan of thrill rides and wild animals.

So online you had to make a reservation online , buy the tickets and then make a reservation for a parking spot. Once we arrived , we noticed they were letting in people who did not make a reservation for parking , did not buy a ticket as well. What is the point of a reservation and buying it online if you are going to let anyone inside?

My bf and I had the quick passes and the lines for those were about 40mins to an hour. So it felt like a regular line. There are spots in the park for people to buy quick passes as well throughout the park any time they would like which is why I took so long.

The food and wine festival there was my favorite part. You did have to purchase that separate.

You have to wear masks at ALL times except eating or drinking. They will not start a ride unless everyone puts it on.

There is social distancing on the rides as well.

We have lived in the Tampa area for more than 40 years and have had passes of some type many of those years. I bough my son and his girlfriend gold passes, which come with additional 1 day passes, so my husband and I went with them today. While the passes were a pretty good deal, and I enjoyed the beautiful weather there, I was somewhat disappointed in the park. The train is not working still, due to hurricane Milton. Many things, such as the sky ride, the skating show, some eateries, don't open until 2 pm. The sky ride costs extra for non pass holders. Some animals were not seen, such as the tiger, which has a whole exhibit area and gift shop
The plants, green areas and decorations were disappointingly sparse. The children's area looked like the hurricane happened a week ago; the jumping place was not inflated, full of water and leaves.
So, while we enjoyed a day of cool weather, and low park attendance, with family, it just isn't the same great quality park that it used to be. If the park is open, a large majority of attractions should be open also. Step it up Busch Gardens, not everyone wants to spend the day on roller coasters. Probably won't renew those passes.

It has been years since I was at Busch Gardens, so we bought the season tickets. What a mistake. We got to the park only to find all the things we liked were gone. All the shows started later in the afternoon. The train was shut down which I counted on to traverse the park as I am handclapped. They actually charge the handclapped $100.00 for a scoter rental. Most of Christmas village was closed, except for the greatly overpriced shops. Food, it is outrageously overpriced. $5.00 for a bottled water. The whole place reminded me of an overpriced fast-food joint. I managed to limp my way around for the three hours we were there, with frequent rest stops. I was glad to leave. I will NEVER go back, nor will I recommend Busch Gardens to anyone.

Had I known so many things would be closed, I wouldn't have come. I saw like 3 rides closed online, but when I got here way more was closed. Waiting in long lines is to be expected. What's also to be expected it to have places to get drinks and snacks. I'd say 90% of the "snack shacks" were closed. I have more pictures of stuff that was closed (food, rides, exhibits) then of things that were open. I waited over an hour for a coke, waffle cone, and a funnel cake. The long food lines wouldn't be so bad if more places were open. I am highly disappointed with this trip. I'd say the best part was the bacon cheeseburger and I could've gotten just as good for a fraction of the cost outside the park. I spent over $700 on a $100 worth day.

**BG got back to my husband on Facebook and we are going to go tomorrow and try again, so two days for the price of one.**

We are a family of four. We have been annual pass members for two years from Lakeland, Florida. In general, the park are pretty good and clean. We appreciate the tramp promptly service. Our boys do not care much about animated characters or roller coaster rides. We mostly enjoy the train rides, the wide range of animals as in a zoo. We also amaze how ice-skating dancers dedicate their lives to days in and days out to perform consistently with great smiles. By the way, there is a little thing in the ice skating show we truly support to be adjusted. There is a short male dancer with a hairy lower chin. His dark hairy chin was so distracting to the show. We value, respect individual, unique, or cultural appearance. Yet, a well represented talent dancer might need to have uniform representation as to the group of dancers in the spotlight.

Visited the park during this whole covid nonsense and it was dreadful! They expect guests to wear masks not only indoors, but ALL over the park- yes that means outside 12/7. So unless you are tolerable with the heat, humidity, mask combo I don't recommend. A lot of rides were closed, the main playgrounds for kids were closed. A lot of the smaller beverage and food places were closed. A lot of side vendors like face painting were closed. Parking is $25 and it seems as though that is always rising. Some pulled brisket and overcooked waffle fries was $19??? Lol.

Just save your money and go elsewhere- no matter how "good" the deal might be advertised. This visit wasn't worth more than $50 in quality.

What a joke this tired old park is. Haven't been in 25+ years and I'd say nothing has been done in that time. Idk why I went before cept I had free passes. $60 pp got us on one ride. Yes, arrived late at 3 and park closes at 5. (Not 8 as indicated on yelp).

All said and done there are maybe 5 rollercoasters and 1 flume ride. Hardly tempting for a full day attraction. They did have two good looking cheetahs, 5 alligators and 3 giraffes. Yippee!

I can't imagine anyone finding this place worth going to, def not repeating.

Save your $

Busch Gardens Tampa was an awesome experience! From the food, to the animals and roller coasters to the customer service....it was a great day!

When me and my party arrived to the park it was storming. I was surprised at the number of people that were at the park. We didn't have to pay the $22, for parking which started our day off nicely.

I would highly recommend purchasing the meal plan with your ticket as it is worth the extra money! You can eat a full meal every hour if you like. Plus water is free and snacks are buy one get one free, which included most everything even beer.

We started off with a meal upon arrival since it was still raining. We took advantage of the rain and hit up all the animal exhibits the first few hours. Then once it stopped raining and there was no lightning for a certain period of time we were able to start riding the roller coasters.

I had no idea Busch Gardens had such awesome roller coasters. We road them all, and two of the best coasters we rode again after dark. We saw a spectacular fireworks display around 9:30 PM. And we stopped by a snack bar on the way out and got our last free water and I purchased some caramel corn at buy one get one free.

The shuttle was nice too so you didn't have to waste your energy walking from the parking lot to the park. When the park closed the shuttles were coming every 1 to 2 minutes which was great! We even received a free ticket for another day within a year due to the inclement weather. What an awesome deal!

Thank you Busch Gardens for making my first experience at your amusement park GREAT!

Absolute worst theme park in Florida would not recommend anybody waste their money there. The incompetent employees there make for a very unpleasant experience . My family and I were there in September and a lost a Irreplaceable hat that I had to locate and after locating the Hat spoke with employees and also filled out lost and found form and I am still waiting for them to find the Hat even though it was in plain visible sight . Every time I have called there I get the same runaround excuse that they are not able to locate the Hat which is false because as stated above it is in plain sight. But it is very clear that obviously somebody found it and kept it. We'll make sure I go out of my way to never recommend this dump of a theme park ever again and will also let everybody I know that employees there are Crooks and steal lost and found items

Horrible! Disgustingly rude hospitality staff! Girl named Karma working at an outpost spat in my drink when I asked her if she could make me cup of water. Crazy she thought I wouldn't notice and I was standing right there. A family standing near by waiting also saw her do it. The park was okay but I wouldn't trust the staff with your food or drinks.
We are not there yet cause the entrance is crazy: 2 miles in more than one hour! Noe organized for all the people coming to the place in Tampa. Terrible experience.

Ugh! What a waste of time and money. Place is only 1/2 open, lines are crazy long while they seem to have plenty of staff to try to upgrade you to paid extras! Many food places are closed and the prices are extremely high. What a shame that 'this place has become this bad. They make you pay for a whole park, but only open 1/2. Don't waste your time!

Great family fun park with plenty of things to do for all ages. Christmas town was a fun experience, and the Christmas on ice show was amazing. The rides and attractions are suitable for all ages. I haven't been here in years and it is just as fun as I remember. Four stars only because I think some of the facilities need to be cleaned and maintained a little bit better. But overall, I definitely recommend.
It's our go to theme park when we try to get out of the house. Great rides and atmosphere; fun for the entire family!

Ticket counter lady didn't know how to handle seasons passes and guest tickets. Entire sections of park are closed--I mean whole sections of the park! Kids play area in Sesame Street section is mostly closed. The little rides are open but there was one operator who was working two rides (but can only do one at a time) so there was a good bit of waiting here. The kids can't play in the tree house either. Pretty sure I haven't see more sour faces on my kids at a park like this before. First time we ever left a park without buying toys (they couldn't find any in the shops that were open). Waste of time for young kids during COVID restrictions.

The roller coaster lines seems short, but none of my kids are old/tall enough for these yet.

RIDE OPERATIONS need some work!!!!! The ride operations were really bad compared to the last time I went which was 2018. Iron Gwazi was listed as a 50min wait but it wasn't because the line was super long it was not as we were in the station after entering pretty quickly. But we remained in the station for 45 mins due to trains dispatching at an avg of 3.30min per train. This is unacceptable for a two train setup that was taking place on iron Gwazi. Montu was only running 1 train and the line was not moving at all so decided to jump out of that line. Sheikra which has pretty good capacity per train was running two trains and taking forever to get out of the station. Tigris which is a small compact ride and can only run one train had surprisingly the best operations in the park as they got that 1 train out at an avg of about 1min and 50sec.

Besides the ride operations the animals and coasters were awesome but man does the ride ops leave something to be desired.

Went with family (6 of us) and upon entering park we bought "Quick Queue" upgrade to our tickets and were told that if we didn't use them at all, we could return to guest services at the end of the day for a full refund. Well, since so many rides were closed we never had a need to use quick queue tickets and were told by guest services rep that day, 3/9/25, that we would receive a full refund of $604.34. They said it might take a full billing cycle to show up (amazing that they are able to immediately process the charge but not the credit!). I waited until 4/27/25 to allow for credit to get processed--nothing! Called guest services today and was told that they are unable to credit me as promised but "maybe" they could give me tickets for future use! I was promised a cash refund on my credit card and now they refuse to let go of my money. What a fraudulent scam. The FL attorney general should shut them down

This was one of the worst experiences we have had at an amusement park. We have been to Six Flags, Universal and Adventureland to compare it to. Super expensive, long waits and the rides were all broken down. Be prepared to be nickeled and dimed on everything! They force you to rent a locker if you have a small bag with you. You are not allowed to set your bag on the platform like you can in other amusement parks.

We literally went on 2 rides in 4 hours and both broke down while in line. Parking cost $30 for the day, For a family of five, you could easily spend close to $1000 for the day.

We will never go back.

Amusement park or abusement park ?
I have been going to Busch Gardens since the early 1970's and never been disappointed. Well all that changed on Sunday. We decided to visit the park as a quick break before the wife , a teacher, had to go back to work.
We bought our tickets on line including the meal plan. We were shocked when we got to the parking window and it was $30.00 to park. That was the beginning of a terrible day.
We entered the park, waited in line to get our dining wristband and then went to enjoy some rides. The first one we passed was the Iron Gwazi which we had not ridden before, it was not running . Next we came upon the Stanley Falls flume ride , also closed. We kept walking and arrived at SheiKra where there was a 30 min wait. We went to get in line and were sent to put our bag in a locker $3.00 . Walked back to the ride and it was now showing a 45 minute wait. Started in line and there was a constant flow of people with fast passes boarding the coasters faster than they could get the " regular " customers on the ride. We watched the only two coasters fill with fast pass people only , before we were lucky enough to get on the ride. The total wait time was over an hour.
We had been in the park for almost two hours and were only able to board one ride . This was ridiculous and we went up to the customer service line to request a refund. We spoke to Veronica , who said she was the regional manager and were told that since we entered the park gate we had used our ticket and all she would do was offer us another day to come back.
We decided to stay since we had already made the drive and had no intention at this point to ever come back.
At the entry before you get in the park there was a sign listing rides that were closed and wait times, it was blank. The rides that were running were only using half of the lines and the wait times were crazy. A few of the attractions that used to be included in the admission price are now " experiences " and require an additional fee.
We decided to eat and then go to the zoo part of the park. Lunch was a meatball sub and was not worth the time it took to get it. Only half the food lines were open and the wait was long. The zoo continued to disappoint with animals that didn't look healthy and employees that definitely were not happy. Lots of animal habitats were closed as well. There was one poor elephant that didn't even eat the fresh treats being thrown to it by an employee.
Florida is having record high temperatures and the wait times were brutal. Many of the air conditioners in waiting areas were broken as well. We went to the smokehouse to eat and the line was long and we were inside a building with no air . Food was warm , fries were inedible and the staff was miserable. The vents over the food line were rusted and covered with dust and grime. The entire park is in disrepair and needs to be cleaned and updated.
My parents brought me here when I was a teenager , and I brought my children here as well . It saddens me that my children will not continue the tradition due to the terrible condition of the park in general.

There should be more legend maps posted, more customer relations people walking around answering questions, and listening to complaints about people smoking in undesignated areas, the food lines (especially at dragon grill at 1:40pm on 9/4/22) were out of control! Waited in line 50 minutes for chicken tender orders! That is half of my dine all day wait time. The drink area was soaking wet, the food trays were dripping wet, and the utensil dispensers were empty.
Tables were not wiped down after guests left. The food was good and we enjoyed the all day dine. One of the the bathroom at the front when they opened was out of hand towels. We ended up purchasing the quick que tickets after already in line for cobra, however there was no way to use the quick que b/c we were already on line. That was frustrating. The park is pretty clean. The 3 in our party that rode rides enjoyed them.

Being born and raised in Florida I've never came to Busch Gardens before and it was until I saw their BOGO deal that it was a sign to finally visit. Tickets are usually $80 + tax. So the BOGO deal was definitely worth the price.I came on a Sunday morning at around 11am (park opens at 10) and parking was pretty busy especially since there is traffic coming from both sides of the street. After parking you can get on a tram to get to the front of the theme park. The park has a mixture of roller coasters, mini rides from younger children, and zoo spread out throughout the park. There are animals such as tigers, lions, cheetahs, orangutans, rhinos, penguins, and more! It's cool to walk from different roller coasters and see these animals casually living their lives. The rides had decent waiting times and you definitely get an adrenaline rush after riding them.

On this day, it did end up raining around 3pm that lasted 2 hours causing the rides to close down for safety reasons. My group was concerned because we came to Tampa for this one day and it was being wasted by the rain, but! We were in line for guest relations and BG has a rain policy which meant they gave us tickets that we can use on another day!! I can't wait to go back to ride the coasters that I didn't get to go on due to the rain n
Great Place! The new Iron gwazi by RMC is crazy and my number 1 coaster. No waits. I recommend this place!

I shall start by saying....this is hands down, the best theme park I've ever been to in my.life! I grew up in theme parks basically so I know a good one...they are taking great safety precautions after covid opening and I feel very safe there!

The staff is so welcoming and friendly, the rides are top notch (but #MontuisBoss) and I love visiting all the beautiful animals! This place literally changed my life and got me out of a very bad spot. I will always love you Busch Gardens. I even turned down a job out of state just bc I still want you in my life lol crazy, maybe, but I listened to my intuition & my heart. Every day there is always better than the last...why would I ever want to give that up!?
Great theme park with top-tier roller coasters and wonderful animal exhibits. As a local, I try to make a trip at least once a month

I have been to quite a few amusement parks in my time. Over the years they all seemed to have gotten away from leaving people with a smile on their face and more towards how much money they can bilk you out of. This is the first time I was provided free tickets as a veteran, which was great, but again one hand giveth and the other hand taketh away. They slap you with a hefty fee just to park ($35), but do have a tram to take you to the front gate, that is if you want to wait an extra 10-20 minutes in line instead of walking the short distance to the gate. Our first coaster was the Iron Gwazi. A coaster I would not recommend for the faint of heart. I resigned myself to be dead by the end of the ride and kept my eyes closed for most of it to maintain my ability to keep my lunch down. This was bad, but not as terrifying as the giant group of wasps at the top of the first peak. The other coasters were good, but one had us wait an extra twenty-five minutes due to technical difficulties and this coaster had the shortest wait time, which cancelled that out. They had a couple places where you could watch the wildlife without paying for the encounter experiences, which seemed to be more of a rip off than anything. I mean, paying to stand in the back of a truck with no canopy on a safari. I am partially disabled and this is not acceptable. All over the park there were signs indicating you would be ejected if caught cutting in line, but when you inform security, they do nothing-what a joke! After a good amount of rides we decided to eat at Zagora Cafe'. It cost us $115, which included a service charge for three bacon cheeseburgers with fries, three drinks, one water, two cookies, a parfait and a small cup of grapes. Now that is a rip off. Yes, you can by the meal plan, which is only good for the meal, but not any snacks and you only break even if you eat twice, which is only a benefit if you can keep your lunch down after a coaster. All-in-all I would not recommend this park as there are plenty of other sites to see when visiting Tampa.

Now this Busch gardens is Africa themed but reminds me more of Arabian nights or something. It's way bigger than the one in Williamsburg VA and you will get lost even with the map and signs. I followed all the directions and still walked for about an hour before I encountered another ride. This one also is very crowded. I imagine because its florida the sunshine state where the weather is perfect like all the time so prepare for long lines even on off days.

I will say the food was way better at this Busch gardens. I really enjoyed the Zambia smokehouse. Expensive as hell but that's to be expected. I went during the holiday special on the 30th before nye and all the Christmas lights were up and it was beautiful. They had tons of shows for little kids with all the Christmas characters. This Busch gardens also had some type of animal exhibit with hippos, lions, penguins etc...like a damn zoo lol so that was interesting for a theme park.

This place is a total ripoff. Half the park was "under construction" or "closed for the season". This is Florida, this IS the high season.

We went to see the animals but the train was down and no walking access to see the prairie. The areas that had animal displays were empty and closed. Only a few rides were open. The rest were needing repair. No shows or entertainment were available. When we questioned the supervisor she actually said that the performers needed a rest and they would be off for a few weeks... no discount was offered though.

There was no mention of closed attractions, closed shows, or shortened hours on the website or at the call center when we booked the tickets. There were numerous extra charges such as $32 to park and an "administration fee" of $10 in addition to the ticket price. We spent over $300 a couple to just walk around.

The food was terrible and unbelievably expensive. The manager was rude to us and the many customers that waited in line for identical complaints to ours...I could go on and on.

Before you waste your money, PLEASE READ ALL THE REVIEWS.

I Thought The Bush Gardens Rides Were Awesome The Year Before Last Year And I Loved The Manager Tomz Je Bought Me A Howl O Scream Shirt And My Favorite Rides Were Shakira, Scorpion And The Haunted Houses And Food Was Great.

You have to reserve a day due to capacity limitations , and you have to wear a mask. They stop they rides maybe every hour or so to sanitize. We went on a Sunday and there were very few people there. You could zip right through the lines. A few attractions weren't open.
The roller coasters were great! The drop tower was amazing.

Where else can you ride crazy ass rollercoasters and see wild animals? Okay, there is Wild Kingdom an hour away but their rides are tame compared to Busch. Add the fact that Busch is one of the few place in the US where you can see a live hyena and that trumps Disney. This place is chill and if you're a coaster buff, this is the place.

I'd give this place five stars. It many restaurants and rides were closed. I'm assuming it was due to staff shortages. I didn't see any food festival booths operating which was sad.
The park is fairly clean but restrooms were a bit yucky in the afternoon.
I highly recommend the train to take you around the park. When it drives through the safari area have your camera ready! You really do have the hippos, zebras and all up close and sometimes on the path! It would be perfect for it to run more often but it seems to sit for lengthy stops at all stations and wait to fill up instead of making more loops. I recommend doing it early in the day as the heat was rough sitting in the stations so long.
The heat meant no long lines. I think the longest was about ten minutes. They have great gift shops and friendly staff.

We fell in love with Busch Gardens after coming here for the first time in 2010. There's really something for everyone to see- expansive safaris, animal shows, roller coasters, and pizza.

Military discounts? Yes

NEVER AGAIN! The park was not even full capacity and the lines were still long with many rides only running one car. went on 3 rides, that is it! One we waited an hour before they shut it down! there is very little family friendly rides and the sky ride is now a fee. Not many food places and what they had had long lines due to not enough workers. Save your money!
I will never go again and will advise others against it!

Busch Gardens is one of those Tampa Bay staples that every local should be going to at least every other year or so. This theme park is more than just rides: they're a venue which hosts all kinds of events (food and wine festival), they're a zoo, and they're a garden. There's also alot of education they provide about the animals and plants they host. Now I do have one disclaimer which is that I didn't go on any rides or roller coasters besides the train and the carousel, but this is a testimony that there-s something for everyone, whether you're a thrill-seeker, adrenaline junky, or just wanna get away from everything. Perfect place to go for holidays and special events. I myself come every year for their Howl-O-Scream event ALMOST every year but one. This is my second time coming out to their Christmas Town where everything is decked out for the holidays. We spent seven hours at the park, and in those seven hours we did alot that was not roller coasters or rides. We went on the train and saw the animals: antelope, giraffes, rhinos, and zebras wandering and roaming freely. Then we went on the other exhibitions and saw the chimps and even had a chance to go underneath a tiger in his enclosure. We had a chance to pet baby kangaroos and be surrounded by birds. The food here is good, but menu is limited and pretty pricey. I highly recommend the smoked chicken, which was large, fall off the bone, and full of smoke flavor. It really hits the spot on a warm day with sone coleslaw and waffle fries.

I wouldn't waste your money. We paid $650 just to get into the park plus parking. Then almost all the rides are shut down or broken. So far my boys been able to try on 1 ride total!!!!!! Then they want you to pay extra to by pass the line when only 1 ride has been working so far. We get to safari part oh sorry that's a extra $50 per person!! So yeah let's pay $650 then add another $300 on top of that. Nope. So far this trip here has been a total waste of money.

Update so far Mardi Gras parade didn't even happen. No other shows and it's only 2:00 pm. Kids got to go on a total of 4 rides for $650. I would not come here until this company can actually have activities and rides open that we pay for. We were supposed to come back with 5 other families in April that will be a huge NO!!!!!!!

bush gardens is not even close of what it used to be we traveled almost 3 hrs for my kids spring break and what a major dissapointment most rides and kiosks were closed even the train was broken we come almost every year but not anymore what a waste of time. on the way back to the car there was only one tram bus working which is ridiculous oh yeah and $40 for parking the icing on the cake

We just had the worst experience of our lives. We sat in line for over an hour in the extreme heat with no shade and then we're told it will be another three hours till we can get into the park. I was told they are doing construction at the entrance and only have 2 security checkpoints open. Why would you do this construction during this time of year?? It's over 100 degrees and probably the busiest season due to summer break from schools. I watched someone pass out from a heat stroke. It is the most unorganized confusing entry system ever. You guys should be ashamed of yourselves for allowing that at your park.

This review is specifically for the Christmas Town event.

Whoever handles logistics for this failed miserably. The lines for each food and drink stand are too long and slow, because each is understaffed. Also, each food stand should also offer a drink. That will reduce the number of booths that people have to go to.

We got there at 7:30 and a lot of the booths were out of parts of their menu. You didn't expect it to be busy on a Saturday night?

To make matters worse, there was no communication about items being sold out. I only found out that what I wanted was gone after standing in line for 20+ minutes. That happened twice. It's not hard to put a note that says "sold out" on the sign.

The lights are pretty, though.

Ever been a bit more than pleasantly surprised? I am vacationing with teen boys... yes, the hard to please demographic. Ugh! aww man! I dragged them out of bed this morning, hustled them to get into the car. Sped to the complimentary bus pick up point (bus almost left us - note the bus was pulling out exactly ON TIME!). The dreaded looks I got on the hour ride from Orlando.

So you see five stars right? Those skeptics I dragged out of bed this morning had the time of their lives!

The assumption was "gardens and animals with one or 2 mediocre rides." Now they can't say one bad thing. The thrills of the rollercoasters, the shows, the train ride through the mini safari, the food, the deserts (the deserts were GONE before I could get a pic), no complaints.

So I would STRONGLY advise first time visitors like myself to get there for the 10am park opening and head to Cheetah Hunt rollercoaster FIRST. We got there at about 10:45 and the wait was already 50 minutes. Next, the Cobra's Curse - that was 45 minutes. All other rides were 15 to 30 sometimes less. I was told by a regular, those lengthy wait times happen because those rides are the first to open. So best to get there early, get them done, then cruise through the rest of the park.

The event/shows schedule can be picked up with the nap at the front gate. Super easy to plan your entire day so that you see all you want to see and do all you want to do.

Rides for all age groups. Exciting atmosphere. And the lights at night are beautiful.

Truly a fun day with teenage boys! Will return next year same time with the entire family!
