While Universal's Islands of Adventure is better for older kids and adults, Busch Gardens really offers a lot more rides for younger kids. Busch Garden's Sesame Street Safari of Fun is much bigger than Dr Seus Land at Universal. Most of Disney's Magic Kingdom is pretty young kid friendly with exception of their Rollercoasters just like Busch Gardens also has the added benefit of lots of animals on display kinda like Disney's Animal Kingdom! Universal and Disney has Busch Gardens beat for innovative food selections, movie tie-in, ride quality, theming and overrall feel. Busch Gardens food selections need a lot of work as most places just have subpar overpriced burgers, Pizza, and hot dogs aside from the seasonal festival foods. However, Disney & Universal theme parks are comparatively much more expensive for annual passes but only a little more for a one time pass. Busch Gardens let's you come back all year with a fun pass for the price of a 1 day admission! Unfortunately, Busch Gardens is way too spread out and it requires way too much walking in comparison to other theme parks in Orlando.

I really hope Busch Gardens management can bring back more live shows at the Pantopia Theater and Pantopia Dragon Fire Theater, bring more popular concerts, offer a greater variety of foods at restaurants, offer a greater variety of foods at festivals, and offer more types of rides than just Rollercoasters like 4D Rides! If managment could duplicate all the international food offerings at the superior International Food & Wine fest of Busch Gardens Williamsburg VA that would be awesome!

My family visited Busch Gardens Tampa on February 26 during a week-long Florida vacation...grandparents, parents, and a 2-1/2 year old. My daughter had downloaded the BG app prior to vacation, and had our whole day planned out. I had purchased tickets ahead of time, through AAA, that included all-day dine. First of all, there was no signage directing us to parking. My son-in-law actually drove past the parking lot and had to turn around. Once we got in the park and started walking around, we found that many of the attractions my daughter had planned, were closed. The train station we stopped at was closed, and we didn't know, until much later, that there were two others (of which one was also closed). Signage is terrible. Many eating establishments did not accept all-day dine, and it was difficult finding ones that did. It was a long, hot day with a lot of walking, trying to find something that was actually open that we could enjoy. Apparently there was a Mardi Gras celebration on Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays...and we were there on a Wednesday...but still...even the little kiosks were closed! I paid $542.80 for the tickets and I want a refund. I called AAA...they were interested in what I had to say, but said I needed to contact Busch Gardens directly with my complaint. I have now contacted Busch Gardens five times...twice on-line, and three times via phone...and have had no resolution. The on-line methods were via links on their website...but nothing for "Guest Services." The phone call has an automated menu, but again, no "press __" for Guest Services." One phone-call representative told me I needed to return to the park and talk with Guest Services. Well, I'm in New Hampshire, so that's not going to happen! Very disappointed with our visit, and with the lack of customer service.

This is the 7th year in a row I've gone to Hollow Scream and every year we've spent hundreds of dollars to get fast passes. Hollow scream is the biggest rip off ever. They closed the rides down an hour before they close. On top of that they didn't even have all the rides open to begin with. They also had ZERO water I walked around forever to find water. It has gone down hill ever since Covid they don't have as many scarers as they used to. It just feels like it's gotten more and more lame since 2020. I will no longer be spending that kind of money for a crappy product and will start going to Halloween Horror Nights.

why the hell is it sunday right before spring break and they have a whole two roller coasters open if that. i managed to get on one and still got stuck on it. every. thing. is. down. the only thing bumping here is sesame street.

I always have a great time when I go to Busch Gardens. Every single special holiday event is extraordinary and blows my expectations out of the water. Great rides. Yummy food. Great drinks. Cute animals. Parking is a hassle, and so is the tram- but other Orlando theme parks aren't much better in that dept lol
Must do!!!!

A perfect place to go anytime. Will be able to see many animals, ride on fantastic roller coasters, and the food is amazing. It's a 4 star because the food is unnecessarily expensive! Other than that great!

Took three grandkids to Busch Gardens this week, spring break! Best decision ever! We bought the fun passes for about 125 each. But we can use it until December. I use a mobility device and found the park to be very accessible. Even their train ride has a lift so I was able to take the chair right onto the train. It was a perfect mix of thrill rides for the older grandkids and laid-back Safari and animal viewings and gardens for me and the younger one. There was even an amazing Sesame Street land with ball crawls and trees to climb etc.!
The older grandkids did a couple thrill rides but the lines were really long. I learned about their version of fast pass too late to get them for them. So I would recommend if you want to do the more popular rides, check into their version of fast pass. It will cost you but everything else cost you too so...
It happens that they are doing their food and wine festival. Beautiful gardens lovely variety of foods, wines , Bourbons whiskeys and desserts.
My only concern was with the accessible parking. They sell part of the accessible parking lot for preferred parking. Unfortunately for those of us who don't have a choice, if you don't get there early, there is no accessible parking. As I explained to the attendants I really don't have a choice but to use accessible parking as the grandchildren aren't old enough to drive to drop me off and I can't get my chair on their tram. So I just kind of pushed ahead and went into the "full" accessible parking lot. I found a space that wasn't really a space but I made it into one, two days in a row! Freaked the grandkids out that I was parking illegally.lol but I like to keep them on their toes! I promised them we would not get arrested for parking illegally with a handicap parking and my mobility scooter! Would not be good for business to have that on the news! Lol.
So go while it's still tolerable weather! They have a lot of lovely gardens and shaded areas. If you go don't miss the food and wine festival!

This year i have gone twice! In april i went with two of my best friends and we had an amazing time even though i chicken out on a few rides haha. This time around i went with a close friend and rode almost everything therefore died a few times lol. The only ride i absolutely refuse to go on is iron qwazi which my friend said "you would have probably passed out" he wasnt a fan and felt like he was off the seat the entire time. Bush gardens is overall a fun park to go to, so we did decide to get the annual passes. One thing thag would be great is to have multiple mist stations throughout the park because these summers are getting brutal!

Went today with some friend to spend our afternoon doing something fun. Today was the first time riding Tigris and cheetah hunt and I can say that both rides are for people looking for that high but can not do larger coasters that have to many loops or goes to high. The first thing that gets your blood moving with both rides is the acceleration using magnets to get both rides upwards speeds of 45 for Tigris and 65 MPH for cheetah hunt.

One highlight for each ride that makes them both so unique is that cheetah hunt towards the end, zig zags like how cheetahs would run after their prey and with Tigris having multiple twists through out the ride to give you that "big coaster loop" feel.

The wait time for cheetah hunt as of today was only at 5 minutes since they have two coaster cars verses the 20 minutes you can expect for Tigris with it only having one coaster car. Both rides regardless of wait times are both worth the wait.

10/10 perfect for a fun family night out weather you are a local or a vacationer.
It used to be nice. My family is local and went often. Kinda dirty and staff was either dumb or surly trying to buy a lot of stuff at Elmo show. Its long past Covid is an excuse for theme parks.

Best roller coaster park in Florida . The park has lots of rides , games , and lots of interactive games for kids of all ages . The park is full of animals and is a great experience for the whole family !! I would recommend the fast passes for rides it is we'll worth it !!
Does not disappoint; great rides, short lines, fun times. Decent ticket prices too!! Check it.

Another disappointment just right at the beginning. Register person does not have the authority to void another transaction that changed their mind which now caused a 5 minute delay with ice cream in hot temps. Smaller sundaes than the past but higher price. Drop the quality increase the price. Passes will be done after this year.

Bush Gardens leaves much to be desired. From its lack of staff, to dining options, to the much needed park renovation... I wouldn't go back!

Tons of rides are closed, or temporarily closed due to maintenance or mechanical issues... dining plan sucks!

Platinum membership not worth the price.

Don't bother with a membership. Finishing up my one year anniversary of being a platinum member. Not many discounts and/or benefits for the price. Wouldn't suggest more than the entry level membership. 90%of the time you can get the same thing on Groupon for far cheaper than the membership price. Upon logging in my 'membership discount' puts a Howl-O-scream over $85 with taxes and fees. My friend bought the exact same ticket on Groupon for $55 all in.

Had so much fun at this theme park. It's large and definitely need your comfy shoes. There is something for everyone in here. The food is just like any other theme park, expensive and just ok.

I enjoyed The safari train watching the animals was fun.

I don't even know what to say.
My family purchased a 3-park pass and decided to check out Busch Gardens on the 2nd day. This is the park that my husband has been waiting to go to forever... and I was so happy to be able to take him.

So getting there wasn't bad and the parking wasn't the greatest - since we had to park on the other side of the park where you have to take a tram to get to the entrance of the park - but we made the best of it.

We get thru security and showing our tickets to get in and we thought that the adventure was going to begin... like at Sea World... but low-and-behold, we were so surprised to find that you have to walk so much before you even see anything! What?!?

We see a ride coming up and notice that it's not running... come to find out that it's not even finished yet! Ugh!

More walking it is...

So we come upon some areas that have some beautiful flowers and topiary... loved it. But no more... we had to walk even more to find an area with the kangaroo and further down, some birds.

Where the heck are the rides in this joint?!?!
Found Sesame Street area found... our daughter got in one ride there and we walked out.

More walking and we finally find some rides but at this point - almost an hour in and we hadn't found a concession stand to get anything to drink and mind you, it's like 90° while walking! There were about 5/6 concession stands and restaurants that we passed that were just closed.

So we had to stop to get a drink at the first concession stand we found that when we got in line, only one of the registers were being used and the line was super long!
We finally got our drinks and snacks, ate them and decided to jump into the line of the first water ride we see. Score!

No score...
After waiting over an hour and a half, the employees come over the PA system to tell us that the ride was shutting down because it was broken but we were more than welcome to stand in line until it was fixed! What?!?! Why would I continue staying in line for a ride that was completely shut down?!

So we get out of line and were just disappointed with the whole experience. There were barely employees around to help with answering questions, which was very strange to see.

The layout of the park was so strange... I don't know how to explain it but it wasn't easy to read the maps that they have in order to get around.

So we decided to leave the park - and thank goodness we did when we did because a storm came ripping thru the park - and we got to the area where you get on the tram and come to find out that the park had stopped the running of the tram because of the storm that was coming. And they weren't going to start them up again for 45 minutes! What?!?! You bring your patrons from across another parking area that isn't close by on your tram and now we have to walk to the other side - thru a long tunnel and cars whizzing by and falling branches from palm trees, no less! Busch Gardens... do you not care about the safety of your patrons!!!???

So... that's the end of our adventure at Busch Gardens. I felt that it was a waste of time and money... even though the hubby says he'll give it another try one day - maybe never for me.

Amazingly you wouldn't know you were in the middle of Tampa with all the foliage.
Lot of coasters but enough rides for all ages. Nice how the animals are located throughout the park. The train circles around the park and goes through the Serengeti so you can see the animals roaming.
Went during the week after school started back and basically empty, best time to go if you can.
Park clean and bathrooms kept well.

We mistakenly thought that there were gardens and nature trails along with the rides. LOL. We were mistaken! Over half of the rides were closed, the park was very rundown, and I would never recommend coming back to this place. It appears to be sliding towards bankruptcy.
It hasn't been raining for over an hour and some rides are still closed! It is like the staff isn't on the same page!!

Super fun place! There are so many interactive things to do with animals!! It's fantastic! Add that to the already exciting thrill rides, it's a win win. Definitely go when you're ready for a break from the beach!

Such a day filled with fun in the sun!!! I went by myself and surprisingly I had so much fun. I arrived at opening time and got super discouraged because of the super long lines outside of the park. Shortly after I realized it was the Covid-19 prescreening. It did feel like forever but taking how many people were in line (sesame Street opening weekend) it wasn't that long. I was super happy to know the park is taking so many precautions also. The customer service was amazing and the employees were super friendly. This is a great place to have fun and enjoy some educational learning! It's literally a zoo and theme park all in one. I purchased the food package with my entry ticket, and upon arrival I purchased the skip line pass and It was worth every penny. I finished the rides so fast (I rode every ride once) and did the train ride and walking tours in no time! So fast that I didn't even use my food pass! Which the people at customer service were so nice to give me my money back. If you are with a group or by yourself, this place is filled with fun for every age!

Bonus: SUPER CLEAN BATHROOMS!

The quality of this park has gone down significantly the last couple of years. If you don't have small children or you don't want to ride a couple of roller coasters this isn't for you. This park is supposed to close at 8pm so I came at 5pm just to see the animals and maybe eat some food but this place was terrible. They started shutting down the places to see the animals at 6pm. All of the food stations were closed and there weren't any maps throughout the park to tell you where you where in the park. I had to sit down and pull up a map of the park on my phone just to get directions. This place is falling apart I didn't even take pictures. The only reason it gets 2 stars is because people with small children will love the Elmo part of the park and if you like roller coasters there wasn't a line for any of them so you can ride all day without waiting in a long line.

We rented an electric scooter for my spouse that is a cancer survivor and wouldn't be able to walk all day around the park in the Florida heat $140. When we got to the park we wanted to be able to leave and return and still have use of scooter. Every employee was clueless. One of the two was at least nice enough to check with a supervisor and we were assured we could bring scooter back they would mark it reserved for our return in the evening. When it was time to leave the park to rest at our nearby hotel again the employees were just as clueless. Four employees told us we couldn't do that. We asked to speak to a supervisor and as that supervisor was approaching us a rude older blond female who said "I'm the one that rented to you and "I" told you can't do that." The supervisor did tell us it would not be a problem. No apology just an excuse that the employees working at the scooter rental don't usually work there. Sorry no excuse! I knew we weren't at Disney and by the treatment we certainly were at the happiest place on earth!

If you don't get tickets for this attraction on a deal... You might want to reconsider going. They are currently suffering from a lack of employees....as are a lot of different establishments but some of the basics are just completely ignored. Animal attractions are great for young children. The thrill rides are spot on except for some of the ridiculous wait times. Of course you can pay an up charge to cut the line just like all other attractions. I always love a place the charges $30 just to park. As long as you don't set the bar too high, you will probably have an acceptable experience.

My wife went to school in Tampa (we live in California now) and told me about all the fun she had at Busch Gardens. When we finally visited, most of the rides were shutdown. We got in at 11am and waited two hours on Cheetah Hunt. Once we were about to get on, the ride experienced technical difficulties and shut down and all guests were recommended to leave. So frustrating but we figured we would go to the next ride and come back to Cheetah Hunt later.

We decided on Kumba next and once we got to the line we were told the ride was shut down. The park pretty much just opened and the first two rides were busts. We ended up running back and forth through the park using the Busch Gardens app trying to catch open rides only to find them "closed temporarily". We were mainly hoping on riding Cheetah Hunt and the Skyride. Both were closed. We ended up spending 9 hours there and only going on two rollercoasters, the log ride, and the train. Major bust if you ask me and sort of a waste of one of the days of our vacation.

I would avoid this place until they get it together. I don't know if I'll make another trip here.
Better than Disney World in Florida. Easy parking and walking distance manageable. Prices are a lot more manageable as wel.

You know, for an amusement park in the middle of a city block Busch Gardens isn't half bad!

Easy to get to, you can spend part or all day here and really get a chance to experience a lot. I'm a huge roller coaster fan, so a park accessible and fun like this is great to have in Tampa.

I've found Sundays to be a great day to get on rides without crazy waits. Perhaps it's football season, but I managed 6 coasters in 3 hours without really trying.

All day food deal is great at $40, and this gets you a meal an hour all day at select restaurants.

Season passes are pretty inexpensive next to Disney, and the coasters are far more intense! Plus, animal safari!

I recall writing a review about the numerous times I've taken my daughter to Busch Gardens. I'm a fan but not as much as I am of Disney. We always had a great time. It's a full day of rides and libations. I don't drink when I have children in my care. Hahaha. I did back then.

You can enjoy Busch Gardens as an adult. I went with my boyfriend for a day. Naturally it's all about the beer. It's a beautiful place. I'm sure I can find photos to post. I think you're better off visiting if you're staying in the Tampa area. Definitely visit if you're a beer lover. Beer was always my weakness!

Bring your wallet full of cash and credit cards. That's my best advice!

The bacon burger from all over Busch Gardens is disgusting. I paid $80 for a meal pass and all we got to eat was pizza and a disgusting cheese burger since the lines were ridiculous for everything. I wish I could get my $80 back since the food they provided us was not nutritious at all. Sweating and being dehydrated and then eating pizza and fries made me want to throw up. The meat in the burgers should not be allowed. I feel like I ate dog food.

Having grown up in Tampa it's nice to see so many parts of the park being updated. They're currently constructing the new entrance area, but the lines still move efficiently. When we went all of the ride queues had fans to improve airflow during the Summer heat. The refill cups are also a great deal if you're staying all day. Make sure to stop for a show to cool down.

Busch Gardens is definitely a fun place to go with family! Currently they have Summer Nights, which means they have a firework display from Thursday-Sunday when they close at night and more Summer Nights shows/mini performances during the evening time leading up to the park closing time. Of course, like many other theme parks, the lines for the rides do get pretty ridiculous, but it's still well worth the wait. I'm personally a big fan of the loops so my go-to rides are Montu and Kumba.

I don't even know where to start. No food anywhere. We found a churro. Dirty, no employees anywhere, no rides running except kiddie rides, too much money for basically a carnival that's about to be shut down. Save your money and don't go here.
Great day, wonderful animals and birds...the rides are great, had a turkey leg it was amazing.

Just wanted to add this review about this Slushie. It's called the Fireworks Slushie. Simply the best Slushie I've tasted. Went well on my visit to Busch since it was a typical hot day in Florida. Although there are other flavors as well, this one in particular caught my eye and didn't disappoint. You can buy these around the Gwazi Plaza and its surroundings. Just keep you eyes on the look out and you will find them.

I've been coming to this Busch gardens for the last decade. It's great time with friends and it's something different from the usual restaurant and bars. A lot of the rides need maintenance because some of the best ones no longer work. However at the time of when i went there was a wine and food festival event that was happening and i was able to try a variety of foods from around the world. Great time and great experience

Over crowded and understaffed. That sums up the entire experience. It is a given that there will be lines at parks, but the lines were exceptionally longer than what should be allowed. All lines are more than 60 minutes long.....to include lines for food and water. 1 hour wait for chicken strips?? C'mon. We arrived at 10am and by 2 pm we rode 1 roller coaster, got one drink, and got to ride the train for only half the distance, as they force you off. We paid to wait in lines and nothing more. Not all of the food places were open, which led to more overcrowding at the couple places open. In fact, most were closed.

Zero covid protocol enforcement, except when it comes to skipping seats on rides. As long as you have a mask on the ride, you can walk about freely without wearing a mask. This includes the minimum employees who are barely working. All photos were taken from a position where an employee was standing watching and not enforcing anything.

Bathrooms needed serious attention, as did all of the over flowing trash cans within the park.

Do NOT waste your money until they can hire more employees, otherwise prepare yourself to pay money to stand in sunny lines, suffer heat stroke and dehydration all while getting maybe 3 rides in for the day.

Always come here once a year so I can get in for free and every time it's different.
I always come during a weekday and during the school year so it's not super packed.
Love everything about Busch Gardens and I'll always come here when I'm in Tampa every year.

I could give 0 start but unfortunately i can't.
Bush gardens sucks. They are going so deep about the COVID teme by creating more restrictions, you are not allow to bring food or water into the park even if you want to eat a healthy food. The park is huge with just few fast Food kiosks with an extremely bad services. Today I'm here in the park started raining like crazy we decided to leave the park and surprise!! We have to leave by walking because they ordered to all the trains to stop because of the rain. They don't care for pregnants, kids or persons with special needs.
I don't recommend to visit this park.....

This started as an exciting trip to Busch Gardens in Tampa. I'm not from Florida but I have traveled the world and have visited many theme parks.
Today, 23 November 2021, my husband, son, mother-in-law and niece started at Busch Gardens around 2 pm and we bought the express pass ($69.00 each) to skip all of the ride lines because it's my husband and My vacation before we both start our next military duty station. We started off in the Emporium and bought super cute refillable cups. As we walked the entire park, we found that all of the rides were "temporarily closed" except the ride Mantu, to our knowledge. All of the refillable stations were closed and the one we did find had a long line and didn't open until 4 pm. The three disrespectful girls that were working at this beverage stand next to the swan garden sculpture and Iron Gwazi (which didn't open until March 2022) have no customer service skills. My husband approached the stand and asked if they refilled the water cups there and with attitude one of the girls shoot her head no. He shook it off and went back into the line with my mother-in-law. Finally it's there turn, they approach the counter once more my mother-in-law asks "What products do you have?" The girl behind the counter, with attitude says water. So, my mother-in-law asks if they have sprite and the girl just said "water". My mother-in-law said," Y'all don't have anything else?" She shook her head saying no. We have water. My husband walks back up and says "ma, they have coke, I'm looking right at it." The girls response was, "well if you knew that then why'd you even ask?" My husband told her his mother was asking and she wasn't telling her. I understand there was a long line (as these girls who were working this station kept saying in front of all of the customers) but they literally just opened the stand and already complaining for everyone to see. As Military, it's hard to believe that there are such unprofessional and disrespectful people out there when literally all they have to do is go to work, put on a smile for a couple hours and sell prepackaged beverages. Other than this, the park was beautiful and there were plenty of photo opportunities. We got our caricature drawn around 7 pm and the lovely artist did an amazing job, I can't wait to hang it up in our house,Thank you for that experience. She was so kind and very professional. We made the most out of our trip so it still turned out amazing but it only takes one bad experience to ruin a good time and that's unfortunate. I hope y'all find this helpful so you aren't disappointed. Have a great trip!

This review took a little longer to get done than I'd have liked, but I had so much fun here I wanted to make sure I finished detailing all of the individual rides first.

The first time you visit a theme park you've never been to before is always something special. New rides, new attractions, all sorts of surprises around every corner. Busch Gardens gave me this feeling and then some. I've been to Universal Studios a few times in the past couple years, and while that's always been fun it's a different type of experience. Universal has a focus on 3D/4D motion rides, which again not complaining, but they only have two real roller coasters at the moment. Well, full disclosure, this Yelper right here is roller coaster fanatic, and as soon as I walked in the gates at Busch Gardens I looked at the park map and got a childlike giddiness I haven't had in a while. This place was my playground for the day and I couldn't have been happier.

Busch Gardens Tampa is themed around African places and environments. The Cobra's Curse is in Egypt, while the Cheetah Hunt is in The Edge of Africa and Kumba runs through Congo, with the Serengeti Express running around the whole park. The park does an extremely good job giving off the appropriate vibe for the area they're imitating. Sure there are snack carts and restroom signs, but it's more immersive than just about any other park I've visited. (They're sister park in Williamsburg, VA does the same thing with a European countries idea instead of African theme here.) What I'm trying to say is this place is beautiful. It's an enormous theme park with world class roller coasters and more impressive animal enclosures than your typical zoo. There's almost too much to mention about this wonderful place but I'll try and go for the highlights.

ROLLER COASTERS! Seven incredible coasters with another two being built before 2020. I've visited theme parks all up and down the east coast and some of these stand out among the best. The Cobra's Curse with it's independently rotating carts and crazy elevator lift is easily the most unique coaster I've ever been on, the giant wing and true vertical drop of Sheikra was exhilarating and Cheetah Hunt's crazy launch system, smooth ride and amazing views make it extremely well rounded and a real contender for favorite coaster of all time.

ANIMALS! I know the topic of zoo's can be polarizing for some, and I'm no wildlife expert, but in my humble opinion they handle the idea of animals in captivity better than most places. Alligators, Tigers, Elephants, Flamingos, Kangaroos, Gorillas, Orangutans, Giraffes. Every enclosure is massive and more than allows for the animals to move about and enjoy themselves. (The Serengeti Plain "exhibit" is practically a third of the whole park.) They're are at least three different areas of the park where you can get very intimate with tropical birds (while supervised by park staff). I can't say I've been anywhere else that I've been inches away from a brilliant Peacock and later the same day get bit by a Parakeet and watch a Kookaburra nesting. It really is something special.

It's also worth mentioning that my visit was just before Christmas and when the sun goes down you get to see just how elaborate their lights and decorations are for the season. Traditional Christmas bulbs adorned the train station while tunnels of elaborate LEDs illuminated the paths between park sections and projectors display giant snowflakes over the gates. They go above and beyond to feel festive.

If there is anything that could be described as a "con" against the massive list of "pros" to this place it would be that they do fall victim to the curse of theme park food. Our small samplings during the day were fairly tasty, but the prices are a bit steep, the exception being the all day unlimited refill souvenir cup (which was a life saver).

Busch Gardens may not be the most famous Florida theme park, but just because the pair of giants down in Orlando get all the attention doesn't mean this place can't stand on its own. It depends on what you're looking for in a theme park experience, but for me the cheaper ticket prices, far superior roller coaster selection and the plethora of animals make this place an instant win. I fully intend to make this place a recurring destination.

2/19/19 10:30am Preferred and handicapped parking SOLD OUT once again. Wtf is the point in preferred parking? What is the point in Platinum Passes??? Why pay all the extra money!?!! Busch Gardens has changed in such a bad way in so many ways. They changed their menus. The lines are beyond long for EVERY single thing. And when you call to complain.... ... it's pathetic. They tell me I can go wait in line at the customer service center but that's an hour long wait too. Really? I need to wait an hour to complain? Not a good way to make an unhappy customer happy.

Oh and yesterday the rides kept going down and down and down multiple times.



6/9 here again... why? Why ??? Why???? 80 mins for Cheetah.... upstairs of the outpost reserved. What a joke.

1/27 here again. This time the bar is all out of everything. It's 11:30 am and the lines for rides are 55 min wait lol... yeah... just spent an additional $300 so my family can "enjoy" the day. Smh... this park.... why do I keep coming back!!!


11/11 - Here again. After last incident BG gave us skip the line passes. Only good for one line jump per ride (not quick que unlimited). We used them yesterday and it was an ok day. Today the kids were in line for Cobras Curse. Estimated wait showing? 65 mins. Actually time? 2 hours 5mins!!! Yes my children spend half a day waiting to ride a ride !!!! Why? Lightening? ZERO. It was Busch Gardens favorite recording "Due to technical difficulties".

Yesterday they removed the Platinum parking without notice. That was a total nightmare trying to navigate two handicapped folks about a mile to get into the park. I sent a message to guest relations... (nobody cares.). We will purchase quick que unlimited for the kids tomorrow ($150.00 extra) because without it the Park is a complete waste of time. Unless you want to spend all day waiting in line.





Completely awful!!!! I can understand weather but holy sh!t... every single ride "Down for technical difficulties...". Omg.... we've been here two days and our children have got on 3 rides. Between weather and "technical difficulties." We paid $1500 PLUS HOTEL for me, my husband, and 3 children. This park has gone down hill BIGTIME!

Also our kids went to get on the SHEIKRA and they were in the seat and the ride shut down. The tech workers are there working within a few minutes but it too ma few hours. They tried to go back and they were told NO too small. I've posted photos. It's close but NO....

I am headed to customer service to voice my complaints and I bet they don't do crap!

2/17/19 here again. Got here at 10:00am and preferred parking and handicapped was FILLED. So that leaves me 7 months pregnant and my handicapped daughter to walk and walk and walk. I tell the guy directing traffic but he just screamed at us. I call customer service and they recommend I can pay $50 for the motorized scooter!?! Whaaaat?! I pay $200 per month for 5 platinum passes. The like wait right now for Cheetah is 70 mins. Thank God I bought skip the line for $1000.00 yeah that's right $1000 additional otherwise my kids would get to ride on 5 rides all day. What a nightmare this place is.... oh yay I just
waited 1.5 hrs to get a Cuban sandwich in the Dragon Fire Grill and "sorry we are all out.." so I go to get a pizza "15 min wait.."

I always expect theme parks to have long lines and be quite expensive, but this was by far the worst experience I've had yet.

I made my reservation and tried to buy my tickets online ahead of time, but the 1/2-off coupons would only work at the self-service ticketing. When I arrived and tried this, the machines wouldn't take them, and I had to wait 45+ minutes in the ticket line. When I finally arrived at the window to get my tickets and the Unlimited Quick Que pass, the employee informed me that the QQ price had been increased to $130 each! On their website and in all their advertising, QQ's are listed as only $20. Nowhere online does it say that their prices are subject to change or that they WILL jack up the prices on busy days.

When we finally got to our first ride, I was told that I couldn't bring/wear my cross-body bag in line and would need to purchase a locker! Bags are allowed in the park, but they removed the bins outside rides for loose items, so you are forced to pay even more to store your belongings once inside the park!

After 2+ hours getting tickets, finding out I couldn't have a bag, going back to my car to ditch it, then getting back into the park, we finally reached the roller coasters only to find out that even with the Quick Que ticket, we had to wait 45+ for the rides. Never in my life have we had to wait this long with Quick Q/Fast Pass tickets and because of the lines, we couldn't even take full advantage of the Unlimited Quick Que!

I want to start off this review with the fact that I have been to over 20 amusement/theme parks around the country (Cedar Point, Magic Mountain, Great Adventure, Universal Parks, Disney Parks, Hersheypark, Kings Island, etc.) and Busch Gardens and the Sea World Company has provided the worst experience I have ever had at an amusement park. I would strongly advise considering other attractions such as the Disney or Universal Parks, or the Tampa Zoo if you are in the area.

Our visit included 2 standard tickets for $90 each, 2 Quick Queue standard passes for $100 each, and $80 spent on food and water for one meal. This is all before any sales tax, and incudes Busch Garden's lovely 5% commission tax they place on every product or service they have.

Our day was pretty much a complete rainout (7/17/23) and featured rides operational for just under 2 hours and an early park close. I want to clarify that this alone does not depict the 1 star score, as weather is out of the parks control and I don't fault parks for operating safely. My real issues come in terms of the operation of the park itself.

To start, during our brief time of operations the quick queue system is completely broken. There was no one to check our quick queue in front of the two rides we were able to get on (Cheetah Hunt & Montu). We experienced several times people entering the line who did not purchase fast passes and made our $200+ purchase of Quick Queue null and void when we had to still wait over an hour on a weekday due to horribly slow operations and a lack of care from employees. We noticed employees visibly high on drugs and not actively checking restraints on rides (Montu). Their operation team brought a girl to tears as she was concerned her safety harness was not locked in place. Their operations were on average between 5-10 minutes per train without using all trains they had available. Lines easily stacked up to the 2 hour point during our brief operation period.

The food quality was some of the lowest and the most expensive I have ever purchased. Yes, Disney and Universal both have cheaper food options by far. We ordered 2 plates of chicken tenders and fries with one large drink and our purchase came out to $50. When using the standard of a Tyson Chicken Tender, this was far below the mark when factoring how undercooked and little food you get for that steep price. Yes, let me repeat the fact they charge a 5% charge on any food, services, or souvenirs you purchase. We had to wait an hour at a kiosk by Kumba (roller coaster) during inclement weather to purchase a drink and fan because their computer wasn't working and security did not want to walk to that part of the park and help.

Then we get to the part of the day where they decide to close the park 2 hours early just as the radar is showing a clear few hours. We felt very disappointed in spending over $400 for 2 park tickets to ride almost nothing, 2 quick queues that were pointless as they don't properly enforce them, and dealing with safety concern after safety concern. The guest service line was hours long, so I decided to call the hotline to get assistance. I explained the situation that I live 800 miles away and wouldn't be able to visit the Busch Gardens Parks even if I wanted to. I was honestly terrified with how the park was being operated and didn't want to step into another one of their parks and receive a refund.

How does SeaWorld Entertainment try and make a horrible experience up to a returning customer? They offer 2 tickets with 1 year expiration on them. No inclusion of our over $200 spent on fast passes, and no concern about the ability to be able to return to a SeaWorld location within a year. Not everyone has the luxury of being able to go on vacations each year and I don't think SeaWorld understands that and their target audience. After further review, I have come upon forums and forums of people with similar situations and a lack of customer service from the parks. Even for people who bought tickets during COVID, SeaWorld and Busch Gardens used the claim of "unforeseen circumstances" to still not refund customers who never even entered the gates.

I'm very disappointed in what Busch Gardens calls their customer service and their lack of care to try and keep customers returning back to their theme parks. They can keep on adding roller coasters year by year all they want, but the declining crowds and negative experiences people have is what is going to destroy this company if someone doesn't step up their customer service and care for the people that keep this place running.

Please second guess if you are considering visiting one of SeaWorld's many parks (Busch Gardens Tampa/Williamsburg, Sea World Orlando/San Diego/San Antonio, Aquatica, etc.) as they do not have your best interest in mind and only care about thinning your pockets as much as they can. You will get much better value and care at any of the other theme parks in Florida they compete with.

Well I have been going to this place since 1977. I have seen this place change over the years. I have never seen this place so very frustrating to go to. I truly believe when you open your place of business it should be completely opened. You get there the parking lot opens about 9:30am. You pay $28.00 U.S. or $14 if a pass holder. The ticket for 1 day at the gate is $109. So you go to stand in line to get in. At 10:00 am you go in the gates. Can you ride the rides, go to sesame street, or go see animals, no you cannot. You have to wait till 10:30am for some of the earlier attractions to open, even the concession stands aren't open. If you want to ride some of the rides, you have to wait till noon. Some places don't even open till a line forms and the attendant has to finish with another ride. This is Florida it's very hot and humid. If your handicapped and have a parking permit you can get kinda close to the gates sometimes. They are painting the parking lot which could be done at night. Be prepared to go a football field distance before you get to the only gates to enter. Your being robbed of so much when the WHOLE park isn't opened all the way.
If they treat paying customers this way, how are the animals and employees treated?
Don't give a "standard" reply about contacting at an email or a premade answer, like you have done to so many before. Just fix the problem.

Spent the day at Busch Gardens on 5/29. My boyfriend, a Marine Veteran received a free ticket through the waves of honor program. We had a blast. Upon entering the park we decided to purchase a water bottle for 14.99 in which you could refill for 99¢ throughout the park. We are very glad we did because a water is $4-5 and it was a HOT day!! Our only complaint is it was very hard to find the places to fill it up, and when we did the line was insanely long. The wait times were long, but we expected it. We did watch the ice skating show and it was AMAZING!! Overall a great day!!

Horrible...apparently they let a lot of people go and now the park is not operating at full capacity and there are lines everywhere, worst than pre-covid!!

Restaurants are partly closed and food selections are worst... no healthy options for adults or kids.

Price gouging.....$169.99 for a quick queue?!?!
We loved Busch Gardens last year 2020 but this year it felt like they were taking pricing consultations from Disney!

I only gave a star because i had to. This whole family of parks completely suck: busch, seaworld, aquatica. They don't give a flying f about the customer. No one competent works here and they just let their teenage employees be rude and sarcastic while they tell you complete lies and send you to different counters where you have to repeat your story and still not get helped.

Why sell a photo pass if more than half of the rides on the list are either not running or the camera is broken and why in the hell would you make the customer have to remember when they went on the ride so these undertrained pisspoor child laborers have to search the database for it?? And why can't anyone just give a stupid refund? How are you going to have absolutely no manager in the area each shift to process refunds when managers can only work in their section?? And if someone is having a bad time at your stupid parks why tf would you offer them free return passes? Not a single person wants to return to this purgatory.

Staff are rude af,the food was decent,these rides wait times are ridiculous and on the apps are not accurate, save your money for a good day. And don't come if there's a chance of rain because they will shut down every single ride all day, and half of them were closed permanently until the next day. Honestly, a waste of my time, and I will never get this day back. Good thing I haven't died today or else. I'll be pissed. That is the last thing I did was alive
Kids had a blast. Absolutely will bring the kids back when it is not 100 degrees and humidity. Best time is October, but the kids wore there bathing suits

Plus size person review: I searched for a few days ahead of going looking for anything about being heavier and being able to ride the rollercoasters and came up empty handed. So I went with friends in town visiting excited to spend the day with them. I am 5'6 265 I could go on one ride in the entire park. Every ride had the signs below but refused to show there are size restrictions prior to purchasing tickets: I think to not post what size exclusions you have in your ride summary is wrong. Busch gardens is willing to take your money but won't give full disclosure so you can plan if your going to be able to ride any rides. I spent the day trying to get my real human size hips into seats all day but instead had to be the non rider and hold the bags all day. I am Very disappointed
If you are plus size or your kids or family members please be aware it's embarrassing to be excited to ride rides but get there and you can't.

I really enjoyed this park! We went for our first time as a group. I'm happy we bought annual Florida passes because we plan to come back again and also to use it at other parks. My experience coming to this park was as a complete novice. Parking was $25 and filled up quickly! They don't seem to have enough handicapped and/or preferred parking. Come early if you fit into either of these two categories. I like that they have a tram system to take you from the parking lot to the park, however, I had a large stroller and there was no space to put it on the tram without taking up space for other riders. So we walked about 15 minutes to the entrance. At the entrance, as a first timer with annual passes, we were made to wait in line at booth B. I estimate that we waited in line for at least 1.5 hours!!! And we got there around noon. The park opens at 10am. I suggest to go early if you're trying to redeem your printed online passes and free kiddy passes!
After we got our passes we were able to quickly go through security! A plus that there is a metal detector! The guards also look through bags! Good!
Once in the park, it was a joy! The only thing I didn't like was that to exit the park once you're ready to go, the options of where to walk is extremely limited! There's a good supply of bathrooms and rides!
I had a good latter half of my day there! I recommend.

*Rating based solely off of Food service *
Let me start out by saying Busch Gardens has the worst food in Tampa . I know it's a theme park so it mostly know for it attractions and Animals but you'd think such a major park would provide better food . I purchased two meal plans costing me over $80 only to receive hamburgers on soggy bread with stale fries . I was even told by one worker that it's the same food they serve to employee in the back for $2 . The lack of variety is shameful and the level of food they serve to paying customers really needs some attention brought to it IMMEDIATELY! As I mention earlier this review is solely based off food .

Today has been the WORST experience in my 30 years of experience at Busch Gardens. I don't know if BG isn't paying well or if it's incompetent management, but waiting to eat is longer then the rides.

If you are coming to BG DO NOT purchase the the all day dining. It's a waste of money. Pack your own food!

I'm so disappointed The level of incompetence in the workers is unfathomable.

BG absolutely needs to hire an outside consultant to evaluate current processes to make major improvements. ​I cannot comprehend how it would take so long to serve food that's already prepared. It is sad and an unfortunate day for Busch Gardens.

fun time but do not waste money on the fast pass the only two rides you need it on they dont allow they also dont include your food other than cafeteria!!!! its a big marketing scam!!! to make money off of you!!!! i repeat dont waste your money on the quick queue wrist band

Fast entry, fun rides, great Safari ride, snacks on almost every corner $.99cent refills if you have a Busch Gardens logo cup. Employees were great with finding discounts and telling you the cheapest way to buy stuff.

One of Florida's lesser visited gems. Great park at decent admission prices and always a pleasure to visit. Just visited to attend a concert by Walk Off The Earth during the Food & Wine Festival and had a great time at both. The Concert venue is great once the sun goes down and there is plenty of up close general seating so there is really no need to pay for VIP seating. The Food & Wine Festival is awesome and a great deal if you purchase the sampler lanyards.

I came here on Christmas Day, it was about an hour from where we were staying. The staff here was extremely friendly. The theme park is a decent size but does focus more on animals - which is okay with me. My two favorite rides were Falcons Fury (best drop tower I've ever been on!) and Cheetah's Run (I think that's the name). The staff was extremely good at keeping everything clean and due to this, yes the waits were pretty long but at least everything was sanitary. I will say I went to the Dragon Fire Grill for dinner which was the wrong decision. I ordered a Slushee, Beef with Broccoli and a piece of Raspberry Cheesecake and it was almost $30. My husband ordered a Burger with Fries, Soda and a Cookie and his was almost $30. The portions weren't even that big! I've been to Six Flags and they have OUTRAGEOUS prices but never even spent that much at Six Flags for a meal like that. I wouldn't go back to that grill, that's for sure. I was going to get a magnet or some sort of souvenir but after that dinner, no thanks. Regardless, it was a really nice park. Would I come back? Probably not. Would I recommend? Yes.

Today was my first time going to Busch Gardens. We had reservations to the special event of LUNCH with Elmo and Friends. I was told on the phone it would be inside but be warned it is in an outdoor pavilion. The employee I spoke to was wrong about the set up of the facility. In addition it is self seating so therefore the tables were confusing. For example we were table 11 but there was another family that was also considered "table 11". Therefore it was a bit chaotic when different groups were meeting the characters. Personally I think each family should be assigned a number instead.

There was an outdoor cheer competition happening directly next to our pavilion. Their music was off the charts loud to the point you couldn't hear the person sitting across from you. Therefore the Sesame Street staff did the "logical" thing and tried to blast their music louder than the cheerleaders music. With 2 completely different songs playing at full blast it was absolutely horrible.

The food: The quality of the food was lacking despite my picture not showing the lack of flavor. It honestly tasted comparable to an elementary school's cafeteria. Someone in my group had a roll that was still clearly frozen. There was mashed potatoes, macaroni n cheese, rolls, veggies, chicken strips, pulled pork, salad, cupcakes, and fruit. I tried almost everywhere and was very disappointed.

Characters: First off there is no show during this lunch. I was misinformed over the phone about the entertainment involved during lunch. The characters dance for about 30 seconds. Don't get me wrong it is cute. However I wouldn't pay money just for a few spins. The characters were supposed to walk to one table at a time. However the staff wasn't facilitating enough as people kept approaching the characters, ruining the flow of meet n greets. Our table for completely skipped over by a character and I eventually had to go approach him since they were leaving soon.

Photo: The photographer lady wasn't very friendly at all. I wasn't thrilled with the quality of the photos taken. However 1 of them turned out alright out of about 5.

Favorite character: The count was the best character there for sure. He was fair on the amount of time he spent with each family and I felt he actually invested time with my experience with him. I would've liked to have been able to meet the Cookie Monster or grouch as well. However there was no guaranteed characters except for Elmo.

The cheerleading music interrupted the entire meal and it was honestly miserable. The characters were cute but not $20 a person kind of cute.

I went to guest services and they tried to work out some accommodations due to our poor experience. However I was not satisfied with how things were being handled. I ended up calling Busch garden customer service who attempted to tell me there was nothing left they could do. However I eventually was able to speak with Busch Garden corporate who took my complaints serious. We were able to work out something that I considered to be fair and respectful. The corporate employee I spoke to was kind, empathetic, and made sure things were made right.

Whoever scheduled a cheer competition next to Elmo's special event lunch clearly didn't think through the logistics of sound. The food however does need help, especially for how expensive it was.

I was very disappointed on my experience at Busch Gardens. However clearly corporate does care about customer satisfaction and is willing to take appropriate actions.

It should not have taken 1 guest service trip to speak to a supervisor, being put on hold several times with customer service for a good 30+ minutes, before FINALLY getting someone who could help me out.

So for now Sea World would be my go to place out of the two pairs. Busch Gardens has some growing to do with customer service and with their food.

This was our first visit as a pass holder and I was left feeling like why do people love this park so much?? Several rides and most restaurants were closed. I get it during the off season, but this was a holiday week and it was super packed. No social distancing was going on other than everyone wearing masks. Lines were everywhere, hell even to grab some popcorn was 20+mins. The rides - you can forget it. The average was over 100 minutes. The shortest at 1 hour. They off quick queue but that's if you want to pay $120!! Today the line was put to the parking lot and we decided to just turn around and leave. We did get to see some of the animals on the trip but overall this trip was a disaster.

What a disappointment. Tried to come for beer fest, but apparently it's only on the weekends. They don't tell u that until ur in the park already. Came on a Wednesday and even though there weren't a lot of people which was perfect for me, hardly nothing was open, lots of the rides were close and the entire park closes at 5. Don't waist your money and come on the weekdays, definitely not worth it! Honestly don't even waist you money to come here unless you get a really good deal of 50 dollars or less per person for the entry. This place has really gone down!

Hello. Visiting from out of town .. went to one of the recommended rides , while waiting in line . Figured out a lot of things /rides / attractions wasn't working as I waited in line, me and guest waited 70 mins . Unacceptable .. when we ask for fast pass,. definitely was more then the Price I came last time . Overall come to have a good time with family and friends.

I've always been a fan of Busch Gardens and am very thankful that Tampa has a theme park along with a water park as well. We used to have an annual pass but didn't use it as often as we should have so we only go during Howl-O-Scream or a random day. It's an awesome place for a theme park and is very big in comparison to other parks. For days where we don't feel like dealing with Universal/Disney World traffic or lines, we go to Busch Gardens for our alternative and I must say that I've never been disappointed with Busch Gardens. They also have animal safaris and a lot of things to do there. It's always a grand time every time. If you are going during busy peak days and hours such as spring break, summer, Howl O Scream, I would highly recommend the fast pass because if not, you will not be having any fun waiting in those extreme lines.

Waited over an hour to get in and park today. Many staff members were surly. Paid 17 dollars for dipping dots and a flat Diet Coke. The animals were great to see which is the only reason I'm giving three stars. However with how frequently it rains in Florida they need more available "rainy time" rides. Park is dated. Won't go back. It was crazy expensive after buying the one time quick cue passes. Need more rides that are adult rides but not crazy roller coasters.

I should have read the reviews before going but I had been to busch gardens Tampa a few years ago and it was amazing. I guess they're trying to make back their revenue losses from covid...

$100 a ticket, paid $45 for preferred parking online only to arrive and be told that parking was full and i'd have to go get a refund. Why would they keep selling preferred parking when they knew it was full? Plus once in the park they didn't even give me a refund, but a $20 park credit which I ended up spending on ONE (1) nasty hotdog and some bottles of water. If they actually cared about customers they'd have water bottle filling stations. The only other food I purchased was 4 chicken tenders and cold fries costing $15.

Here's the kicker: The entire day, never stepped foot on a ride. Some rides had over two hour waits and all others were shut down. On top of that, now you have to PAY to leave your belongings in a locker in order to get on any ride, either $8 for the whole day or $2 a ride I think. It's a small thing but it really made me feel that the park was trying to squeeze out every penny they could. Even the sky ride was closed. I wouldn't be upset if I knew any of this beforehand. Also, bird exhibit where the birds come and land on you: gone.

The day was spent walking around sweating our butts off looking for literally anything to do.
Such a huge waste of so much money and the day.
What a waste of money. App for the park sucks . Sign said 70 minute wait over two hours and still waiting. Horrible experience

Busch gardens is an absolute NO FOR ME!!! We waited 2 hours in line for food, it came out cold. The park is messy and bathrooms littered with bugs, muddy water and toilet paper everywhere. Squirrels were let into the bathroom and were running around causing commotion upon guests. Service was horrible. Food places were out of forks, napkins and serving containers. Trays were un washed and sticks. As well as the tables, covered in food and sticky. Paid lots of money for my family to enjoy this park and most of our time was wasted standing in line for things. Communication between workers is awful we were sent to 4 different places so we could renew our drink cups. Prices are absolutely ridiculous one small pickle is $9 and one bottle of water is $10. Food options are always out and don't get restocked for a long time. Workers in the places take their own time and waste the quests' time. Grout of the bathrooms is covered in sludge and mud. Napkin, paper towels and toilet paper are out in most of the bathrooms. Definitely do not attend Busch gardens!!

I really really enjoyed busch gardens! It's one of new favorite theme parks. I saw they have the Christmas light town so I figured so that, explore what animals they have and get on some rides. You do have to make reservations before you go but they still let you in if you don't. I guess it's luck. The day I went it was ridiculously packed! I probably waited in line between75-120 minutes between each ride, only got on three of them! I didn't like that. Another thing is you have to use lockers and you have to pay for them. Every other theme park I have gone to, they have cubby and you leave your stuff there until you get back. Not sure if it's a new thing with Covid. So you pay $2 for first hour then $1 for each consecutive hour or $8 for the day. Anyways so park is huge they have lots of animals and rides. Parking if free is you have silver or up passes. I got a season pass so I will be back

If I could give them a 0 I would. This company is a predator. Do not give them your cc information for auto renewal of any sort. They keep your information on file and charge you without your authorization. They just processed over $1300 on my cc they've had on their files without my consent. Since we didn't get the cc past the 39 days they pitched it wasn't refundable. Very shady company. Buyers be careful.

What a fitting way to end 2020.

Zero social distancing. No one had a mask on and the "mask police" didn't enforce it.

There were like 2 open restaurants for the whole park. 1 hour for food. They can pack the park full of guests like sardines but they can't pay a few people minimum wage to run an extra food hut?

I can't wait to cancel my membership.

If you're worried about covid I would HIGHLY suggest not coming here until after the pandemic. They don't take even a quarter of the precautions Disney and Universal do. People wear masks if they feel like it and aren't told to put them on EVER! Seriously saw employees talk to guests without a mask or their mask on their chin and not say anything.

There is absolutely no social distancing in lines AT ALL, I was so uncomfortable because every line I got in was packed with people, EVERY TIME I was in line the person behind me left not even a foot of space between us and ignored floor markings, which was again ignored and not enforced by employees.

The park seemed to be full of the type of people who think masks are a political thing- there was even an altercation while i was in line between an anti masker family and a black family that made myself and the people around me EXTREMELY uncomfortable.

As for the rides:
Lines were pretty long, averaging at about an hour of each of the coasters minus kumba which is closed for refurbishment.

I'd recommend purchasing quick queue tickets if/when you go, but make sure to do so online, as it took an hour and a half for me to get through the line for the ticket window.

Overall wouldn't recommend visiting at this time. Give it a few months to a year, or just go to the parks in Orlando.

If I could rate this 3.5/5 I would. It is basically exactly a 3.5/5.

This is the most Florida place of my whole visit. It is an amusement park / zoo / ice skating show?

It tries to be everything and ends up being nothing. Compared to most amusement parks in California it comes up far short in every regard.

The attractions are worse.
The food is worse.
The entertainment is more limited.
The shops are more limited.
The staff is less friendly.

I guess if you want to get drunk for cheap and yell at a giraffe from a roller coaster this is the place for you.

I did like the ice skating show, it was quality. Unlike everything else. It did not belong here.

I would have given this 4 stars and rounded up if not for the fact that the kangaroo exhibit is next to the road between the road and a loud rollercoaster. The road frequently had Harley motorcycles making noise scaring the kangaroos into the corner of the exhibit. That was very frustrating to see and is not acceptable. I am not a huge animal rights person, I eat meat, I don't donate all my money to animal rights causes. Still, this was totally unacceptable.
Always a great place to take a lap and watch the animals. They have great walking paths with markers which make it a great place to go get out for the day and stretch your legs.

What a great park! They really keep everything clean and sanitary and obide by the COVID masks and rules. I found that the guests were following the rules. The park is 95% out doors, which made it easy to social distance and stay away from people.

They have a lot of cool and unique animals you don't see in every zoo like the Hyenas, elephants, hippos, lots of flamingos... really neat. The roller coasters are not my thing, but my wife road a few and said they were fun and exhilarating. The grill in the middle of the park was decent food: I had chicken fingers and fries, wife had cheese pizza and cheese sticks, and we also tried the pretzels from the pretzel place nearby. The cinnamon sugar pretZel and regular salted were just as good as an auntie Annie's or any other pretzel spot. Overall, a good and fun day. Wait times were short (could be because a Tuesday). Would
Highly recommend!

Thoroughly enjoyed all of the shows at the park! The skating show was very good and the costumes were the most beautiful I've seen! The Cirque Electric was exciting and entertaining. The acrobats were thrilling and the lights were beautiful. The Animal Encounters was fun and the information was interesting. The Icons show was entertaining, but I thought some of the music was maybe too "today" to be considered iconic. But the costumes were pretty and the band was great. Had a good time all around!
Great park with even better coasters. This is my home park and one of if not my favorite theme park.

First of all the app is useless, and that is when it is up and running. It wasn't for most of the day, and the company provides no paper maps in the event of the apps being down. The posted maps they have are almost as useless as the app. And asking anyone for assistance is also useless. I'm surprised employees don't get lost since they can't help giving directions. Second of all they have gone cashless, another irritant, I always use cash. Luckily my husband uses both so we were fine. There is ONE water fountain on the grounds and of course never found it. They want you to buy their $6.50 and up bottles of water. Bring a large thermos of water if you can. You will get thirsty a lot, it is hot in FloridaA simple magnet cost $13.99. T shirts with Busch Gardens logo started at $36.99 And people who want to get their kids souvenirs is going to cost them! Yikes!!! It was just my husband and I and I didn't see a thing I couldn't live without for what they were charging. The wild life enclosures were adequate, and we saw several species. The best thing I can say about Busch Gardens is it does have some awesome roller coasters. So there is that. My roller coaster loving granddaughter would have loved it! Wish she could have joined us. Anyway prepare to spend a lot of money, stand in long lines, and do lots and lots of walking

most of, if not all of the rides were either 'delayed' or shut down, the one ride that we did want to go one was shut down from 10:00 AM to 4:30 PM, and when we did get in line it took about 2 and a half hours to get even close to getting on, then it shut down again. every single ride had some kind of technical difficulties every time we tried to get on with the exception of the White Water Raft, but it was still a long wait, about an hour and a half. I do NOT recommend going for the rides, the animals were pretty cool, aleast the ones we could see, it was pretty hot so most of them were hiding. All in all not a very satisfying experience and to me, not worth the money.
Exceeded my expectations. The park was very clean. Everyone was very friendly. No wait times. Glad we made it when it wasn't busy-we almost got to ride everything.

Always fun time a Busch Gardens! Make sure to ride Cheetah Hunt. Ride the train around the park at least once but do it earlier in the day. Every time I make it to the park at night it closes earlier than the other rides.

I love Busch Gardens. I really enjoyed my time here with my kids, since it was off season the waits were not long at all and their military deal is just amazing. So I got their military deal, then the meal deal, but not the fast pass because if I was not military the ticket $90, meal $34 and weekend fast pass $65 would of been over 200 a person and this isn't universal or Disney you know. Don't get me wrong I do really like this place, that's just really really expensive.

The rides were pretty fun. I've been to like 20 amusement parks and their rides are in top 6. The staff was friendly and helpful and worked with me well when my kid didn't want to ride the coaster and let me hang out while my other kid rode.

They could really improve by adding more things in their line than just the line game. The newer rides have started to take advantage of this, but the older rides is straight line game and the log drop is so narrow lines that I had to stand and walk sideways the whole time.

Gotta appreciate the charging stations, cell phone charging cells and free WiFi. That's always a bonus.

I would always come back here if in the area.
March 29, 2023 - 4-5 rides shutdown during spring break. Several for filming of commercials?!? Poor timing Bush Gardens. Take care of your paying customers not your future maybe's.

Stood on long lines with most of the fans not working. I don't know if they are too cheap to turn them on or if they didn't work and they are too cheap to fix them. The rides themselves are good. I think any and every business should be required to accept cash - which they don't anymore. I will not be changing my cash to card and have money left on the card for them to keep. I won't be going again until they accept cash - LEGAL TENDER.

We've been here several times, with Covid the crowds were down substantially. The stay off at Busch Gardens and the facility are always pleasant and relaxing.

We were working on a adjustment because of Covid on senior passes. If you ever have a customer service problem or question with any of the sea world parks, go to Busch Gardens and ask for Raven, LOL, in all seriousness she is about one of the best customer service manager if you could have

We visited the park on Oct 16th and purchased the Dining Package which was a waste of money, most of the places were not open, the ones that were, the food was terrible. The bbq place had a two hour wait so it must have been the only good place to eat. Most of the bars were not open, the caricature place not open. The rides were all good. Not one employee had a smile on their face, clearly no one working there enjoys their job it WAS VERY SAD. There was no interaction between staff and guests at all on ant of the rides, their speeches were monotone. We went to catch the train, and after waiting 20 minutes, an announcement was made when the train rolled in, that it was a one way trip and would not be continuing. There were no apologies given by staff to all who had been waiting. I think management is clearly lacking.

Do not do the monthly installment on their annual pass. It's a scam. They will continue to charge you even after you try to cancel the payments. I called them 2 days after my pass expired, when I saw the charge on my CC, and was told that because I visited the park within 30 days (I went prior to my pass expiring) I was not eligible for a refund. So essentially it's an 11 month pass not a 12 month pass.

There is no excuse for a place this large to have so few places with air conditioning. It's Florida in July so obviously we know it's going to be hot, that's no one's fault. But the shady spots are minimal. About half of the ceiling fans were off. There were not even any misting fans along the walkway! Even the gift shops were sweltering.

The other huge issue is the lack of transportation within the park. Good luck if you use a cane, you're not going to get very far before you have to sit down.

For a company as large as Busch Gardens to not have ANY inner park trolley system or tram system to get people from one end of the park to another is inexcusable and irresponsible.

I'll never return or recommend this venue to anyone.

This park is run by Seaworld!!

Park was fun, glad I got to ride Sheikra, however the food was awful and I had an unpleasant experience with guest services. Also, the animals looked extremely sad compared to the animals at the Lowry Park Zoo where I'm sure there is a tremendous difference in conservation efforts between the two.

I purchased a "two-park pass" during the "memorial day weekend sale" 50% off. Of course under the fine print the exact weekend the sale was advertising was blacked out. I was let in to Busch Gardens in the morning however was turned away at Adventure Island Water Park. I was told by Gabby at Adventure Island that I could get my two park pass refunded however when I returned to Busch Gardens for the refund Marilyn who seemed to be the manager was rude and not very accommodating to my situation. The situation could have been handled better on the ground level and could have been avoided entirely without the expected shady business practices of Seaworld.

All in all, If you are dying to ride some rollercoasters it could be fun but unless eating an overpriced dogmeat hot dog and looking at depressed komodo dragons integrated with your rollercoaster experience is your thing go to the beach instead. Not returning.
